morbid temperament / impaired temperamen.def: Derangement or imbalance of temperament either in terms of four physical properties or qualitative or quantitative predominance of humours Description

simple morbid temperament.def: Morbid temperament in which only change in four physical properties,i.e. hotness, coldness, dryness and wetness/moistness takes place

non-composite simple morbid temperament.def: Morbid temperament in which only change in any one of four physical properties, i.e., hotness, coldness, dryness and wetness/ moistness takes place

morbid hot temperament.def: When heat dominates abnormally in the body

morbid cold temperament.def: When cold dominates abnormally in the body

morbid wet temperament.def: When wetness dominates abnormally in the body

morbid dry temperament.def: When dryness dominates abnormally in the body

morbid temperament associated with subst.def: Morbid temperament in which change in four physical properties, i.e., hotness, coldness, dryness and wetness/moistness takes place with the involvement of substance

non-composite morbid temperament with su.def: Morbid temperament in which change in any one of four physical properties, i.e., hotness, coldness, dryness and wetness/moistness takes place with the involvement of substance

morbid hot temperament with substance st.def: Morbid hot temperament caused by the predominance of hot substance Description

morbid cold temperament with substance.def: Morbid cold temperament caused by the predominance of cold substance

morbid moist temperament with substance.def: Morbid moist temperament caused by the predominance of moist substance

morbid dry temperament with substance.def: Morbid dry temperament caused by the predominance of dry substance

compound/composite morbid temperament.def: The compound morbid temperament which occurs when any two (one active and one passive) of the four physical properties, i.e., hotness, coldness, dryness and moistness dominate abnormally over the others

compound/composite morbid temperament wi.def: The compound morbid temperament which occurs when any two (one active and one passive) of the four physical properties, i.e., hotness, coldness, dryness and moistness dominate abnormally over the others without involving

simple morbid hot and wet temperament.def: Morbid hot and wet temperament caused by only predominance of hot and wet temperament without involving substance

simple morbid hot and dry temperament.def: Morbid hot and dry temperament caused by only predominance of hot and dry temperament without involving substance Description

simple morbid cold and moist temperament.def: Morbid cold and moist temperament caused by only predominance of cold and wet temperament without involving substance

simple cold and dry morbid temperament.def: Morbid cold and dry temperament caused by only predominance of cold and dry temperament without involving substance

compound/composite morbid temperament wi.def: Compound morbid temperament which occurs when any two (one active and one passive) of the four physical properties, i.e., hot, cold, dry and moist, dominate abnormally over the others due to the predominance of substance

morbid hot and moist temperament with su.def: Morbid hot and moist temperament associated with predominance of hot and wet substances

morbid hot and dry temperament associate.def: Morbid hot and dry temperament associated with predominance of hot and dry substances

morbid cold and moist temperament with s.def: Morbid cold and moist temperament associated with predominance of cold and wet substances

morbid cold and dry temperament asso- ci.def: Morbid cold and dry temperament associated with predominance of cold and dry substances

stable morbid tempera- ment / constant i.def: Morbid temperament, which continues in a steady mode and does not get corrected easily Description

unstable morbid temperament.def: Morbid temperament, which does not continue in a steady mode and gets corrected easily

humoral predominance/ preponderance of h.def: When any of four humours exceeds quantitatively or qualitatively

predominance of sanguine.def: Quantitative increase in volume of sanguine humour exerting pressure on the vessels that may lead to heaviness in the body in general and eyes, head and temporal region in particular and may also lead to bleeding due to

predominance of bilious humour.def: Qualitative imperfection or quantitative excess of bilious humour in the body characterized by rapid and swift pulse, urine that is flame yellow and of thin consistency, increased thirst, dryness and roughness of tongue

predominance of phlegmatic humour.def: Qualitative imperfection or quantitative excess of phlegmatic humour in the body characterized by sluggishness, soft skin, excessive secretion of sticky saliva, delayed digestion, increase in sleep, slow pulse, etc. Desc

predominance of melancholic humour.def: Qualitative imperfection or quantitative excess of melancholic humour characterized by rough, dry and dark skin, increased appetite, increased viscosity of blood, dark coloured/blackish urine, tendency to develop splenic

sanguineous diseases.def: Diseases which arise due to the abnormal quantity or quality of sanguine humour; these diseases are considered to be of hot and moist temperament

bilious diseases.def: Diseases which arise due to the abnormal quantity or quality of bilious humour; these diseases are considered to be of hot and dry temperament

phlegmatic diseases.def: Diseases which arise due to the abnormal quantity or quality of phlegmatic humour; these diseases are considered to be of cold and moist temperament

melancholic diseases.def: Diseases which arise due to the abnormal quantity or quality of melancholic humour; these diseases are considered to be of cold and dry temperament

structural diseases.def: equivalent is temperamental diseases. Diseases occurring in compound organs. Its possible English equivalent is structural diseases.

constitutional diseases / malformation d.def: Diseases in which the original shape of an organ is changed One of the types of disease of constitution involving particularly morphological alterations in the compound organs Description

diseases of tracts.def: One of the types of disease of constitution specific to tracts/ tubular structures such as vessels, ducts, etc.; in these conditions the lumen may become dilated, narrowed or completely blocked

vascular diseases / cavitational disorde.def: One of the types of disease of constitution seen in hollow organs leading to either abnormal narrowing or dilatation; includes increase, decrease, occlusion or emptiness

obstruction of vasa.def: Vasa/ cavity becoming obstructed or repleted, e.g., obstruction of ventricles of brain in apoplexy

diseases of surface of the organs.def: One of the types of disease of constitution associated with alterations in surface of viscera/ organ causing compromised physiological functions, e.g., a viscera originally having smooth surface becomes rough/puckered an

roughness of surface.def: Roughness of a surface which should be smooth, e.g., roughness of the internal surface of the trachea

smoothness of surface.def: Smoothness of a surface which should be rough, e.g., smoothness of the internal surface of the stomach

diseases of size.def: One of the types of disease of constitution concerning pathological changes in the size / weight of body viscera/organs; all kinds of hypertrophies or atrophies are in this category

increase in size.def: Increase in the size of an organ, e.g., elephantiasis

generalized increase in size.def: Generalized/as a whole increase in size, e.g., obesity

localized increase in size.def: Localized increase in size/increase in size of part/organ of the body, e.g., hepatomegaly, splenomegaly, etc.

generalized decrease in size.def: Generalized / as a whole decrease in size, e.g., emaciation

localized decrease in size standard term.def: Localized decrease in size / decrease in the size of part of the body e.g., shrinkage of organ / atrophy Description

numerary diseases.def: Diseases affecting the numbers of organs of the body; one of the types of disease of constitution which is associated with congenital or acquired numeral abnormality of viscera or body organs

supernumerary diseases.def: Increase in number of organs; increase in number of body parts, e.g., appearance of extra teeth and fingers

nonpathological increase in number of bo.def: Congenital increase in number of parts of the body, e.g., additional teeth or additional fingers

abnormal or pathological increase in num.def: Acquired pathological increase in parts of the body, e.g., tumour

decrease in number.def: Decrease in number of body parts, e.g., presence of one kidney instead of two

normal decrease in number.def: Congenital decrease in number of body parts, e.g., congenital absence of one finger

abnormal decrease in number.def: Acquired decrease in number of parts of the body, e.g., accidental loss of one finger

proximity disorders / diseases of positi.def: One of the types of disease of constitution in which defects in position and proximity of body organs occur; the defects in position include total or partial shifting of any organ; also included are defects in voluntary

diseases related to position.def: There are four types of disease related to position: 1. total shifting of an organ from its position; 2. partial shifting of an organ, as in hernia; 3. involuntary abnormal increased movement of an organ as in tremors; 4

complete displacement.def: Complete displacement of a body part, as with joint displacement; when the head of the bone completely subluxates from its normal position

incomplete displacement.def: Incomplete displacement or displacement of part of an organ, as in intestinal hernia

abnormal movement.def: Abnormal (sometimes involuntary) movement of an organ, as with tremors

abnormal fixity.def: Fixity of an organ to its location, as with calcification of joints in gout

diseases of interrelation- ship.def: The disease of an organ that arises due to its association with another organ

restricted movement.def: Restriction of movement of an organ that was previously moveable

loss of continuity.def: Loss of continuity of any organ or part of the body

scratch standard terminologies on unani.def: Loss of continuity of skin caused by a sharp- pointed object Description

excoriation/attrition/ abrasion.def: 1. Loss of continuity of skin due to excoriation/ scraping. 2. Sometimes refers to scraping of internal lining of intestines

wound(s).def: Loss of continuity of tissue without suppuration

suppurated ulcer.def: Loss of continuity of tissue with suppuration; this term does not cover Qarḥa mentioned under diseases

longitudinal fracture.def: Longitudinal loss of continuity of bone
Longitudinal fracture of bone, occurring mostly in flat bones, e.g., skull, scapula, etc.; usually too small to trace Description
Breaking of bones along their length (longitudinal bones)

nerve injury.def: Longitudinal minimal loss of continuity of nerve

split of muscle.def: Longitudinal loss of continuity of muscle

severance.def: Transverse loss of continuity of nerve; this term does not cover Batr mentioned under surgical interventions

incision/severance.def: Transverse loss of continuity of muscle

rupture/laceration.def: Sudden break in continuity of a body part

loss of continuity of artery.def: Loss of continuity of an artery Description

herniation.def: This term does not cover Fatq mentioned under diseases

compound diseases.def: Diseases in which more than one component of simple disease, i.e., abnormal temperament, abnormal composition and loss of continuity, are involved in the causation of a single disease, e.g., inflammation

inflammation/swelling.def: A kind of compound disease/pathology comprising abnormal temperament, abnormal composition and loss of continuity

hot inflammations.def: Inflammatory swellings which are hot in nature; arising from blood, yellow bile and other substances which as such are hot or become hot due to putrefaction (putrefaction may be interpreted as infection in present terms)

cold inflammatory swelllings (inflammati.def: Inflammatory swellings which are not hot in nature; caused by atrabilious or phlegmatic or aqueous or pneumatic matter

phlegmon / phlegmona / sanguineous infla.def: Sanguineous inflammation caused by qualitative or quantitative predominance of sanguine humour

bilious inflammation.def: Bilious inflammation caused by qualitative or quantitative predominance of bile humour

soft (benign) tumour standard terminolog.def: Soft tumours which are usually phlegmatic but distinguished by their sac Description

oedema/puffiness / tumefaction.def: An oedematous swelling where the air is mixed with fluid

flatulence.def: Accumulation of air in a hollow organ but not admixed with the organ and remaining discrete from the tissue, leading to distension and tumescence
Accumulation of gas in alimentary canal

acute diseases.def: Diseases with severe signs and symptoms, commonly reaching their peak on the fourth day; their signs and symptoms are severe but do not last more than twenty days

chronic diseases.def: Diseases which have a slow onset, longer duration and lesser severity and reach their peak after a long time
The diseases which have a slow onset, longer duration and lesser severity. Its possible English equivalent is chronic diseases.

external diseases.def: Diseases which appear externally on the body and can be diagnosed commonly by general physical examination, e.g., diseases of skin, nails, hair, etc.
The diseases which appear externally on the body and can be diagnosed by clinical examination generally e.g. diseases of skin, nails, hair etc. Its possible English equivalent is external diseases.

internal diseases.def: Diseases not evident externally, occurring inside the body; such diseases cannot be diagnosed merely by general physical examination
Diseases, which are not evident externally and occur inside the body. Such disease can not be diagnosed merely by clinical examination. Its possible English equivalent is internal diseases.

specific diseases.def: Diseases associated with particular organs, e.g., blindness (eyes) and deafness (ears)
The diseases which are associated with particular organs e.g. blindness with eyes and deafness with ears. Its possible English equivalent is specific diseases.

secondary diseases.def: Disease of an organ due to disease of another, interconnected organ; one organ shares the disease with another, because of the interconnection, e.g., the brain and stomach are interconnected through nerves Description

infectious diseases / contagious disease.def: Infectious diseases that transmit from one person to another; some of the diseases are infectious, such as leprosy, scabies, smallpox, epidemic fever, and putrid ulcer; they commonly occur when houses are congested and t

hereditary diseases.def: Diseases continuing from one generation to another
Diseases that run from generation to generation. Its possible English equivalent is hereditary diseases.

ethnic diseases.def: Diseases which are peculiar to a race, tribe or people of a region; this term does not cover sexual disorders

stages /phases of disease.def: stages/phases of a disease in its due course

stage of onset of disease.def: The stage during which a disease manifests itself and its characters start to develop

stadium augmenti/ stadium incrementi.def: Stage of advancement / time of increase of a disease; the stage during which an increment in the degree of illness takes place

stadium acmes / height of malady / perio.def: Stage of culmination/peak of a disease; the stage during which all the characters of the illness attain a standstill after reaching their peak and before the time of decline

stadium decrement / time of decline.def: Stage of decline of a disease; stage at which signs and symptoms of illness start to decline

signs and symptoms / symptomatology.def: The knowledge of signs and symptoms of health and diseases

clinical feature.def: Clinical feature of a disease which indicates its present condition Description

reminding symptom.def: Symptoms reminiscent of previous disease or symptoms of the disease showing previous condition of the disease; inference drawn by the physician from the existing signs and symptoms of the patient about previous disease c

prognosis.def: Symptoms of the disease indicating its future condition; this helps the patient and the physician to adopt preventive measures and develops the confidence of the patient in the clinical expertise of the physician

clinical features of health.def: Signs and symptoms of health include normal temperament, form, position, quantity and number of an organ and signs and symptoms of normal functions of every organ

signs and symptoms of disease.def: Signs and symptoms related to disease may be characteristic features of a particular disease or may indicate the location or cause of the disease

signs of structure of the organ.def: Signs pertaining to the structure, position, numbers, quantity of an organ either in health or disease

signs of external appearance standard te.def: Signs of the external appearance of an organ, not directly related to the structure of the organ but pertaining to its beauty Description

signs of structure and function of the o.def: Signs of the structure of the organ pertaining to its functional ability

temporary signs and symptoms.def: Signs and symptoms that persist for a specific condition, starting with a disease and ending as the diseases ends, e.g., high grade fever, breathlessness and stabbing chest pain are specific to pleurisy

non-specific symptoms.def: Signs and symptoms not specific to a disease, like headache, may be developed with fever and without fever

signs and symptoms of external diseases.def: Signs and symptoms of external diseases that can be detected by a specific sensory organ or a combination of sensory organs Description

signs and symptoms of internal diseases.def: Signs and symptoms of internal diseases, like disease of liver, disease of lungs, etc.

signs and symptoms of abnormal hot tempe.def: Signs and symptoms of abnormal hot temperament,viz., heat intolerance, excess fatigue, increased thirst, dislike of eating foods of hot temperament, etc.

signs and symptoms of abnormal cold temp.def: Signs and symptoms of abnormal cold temperament, viz., indigestion, weak joints, frequent cold and cough, dislike of eating things of cold temperament

signs and symptoms of abnormal wet tempe.def: Signs and symptoms of abnormal wet temperament, which are similar to those of abnormal cold temperament but additionally there may be flabby body, puffiness on the face, excessive salivation, increased sleep and discomfo

signs and symptoms of abnormal dry tempe.def: Signs and symptoms of abnormal dry temperament, which are dry skin, lean and thin body, decreased sleep, feeling comfort on using substance of wet temperament

signs and symptoms of predominance of hu.def: Features of predominance of humours in the body Description

signs and symptoms of plethora/predomina.def: These include temporal headache, lethargy, easy fatigability, sweetish taste in the mouth, etc.

signs and symptoms of predominance of ye.def: These include dryness of mouth and nostrils, bitter taste in the mouth, yellowish discolouration of skin and eyes, rapid pulse, etc.

signs and symptoms of predominance of ph.def: These include flabby body and loose skin, excess of body fluids like saliva, increased sleep, poor mental ability, soft and slow pulse, etc.

signs and symptoms of predominance of bl.def: These include dryness of skin and body as a whole, increased viscosity of blood, psychiatric disorders, etc.

signs and symptoms of plethora.def: These include fullness of vessels along with full volume pulse, tenseness and redness of skin, heaviness in the body, etc.

signs and symptoms of quantitative reple.def: Signs and symptoms of quantitative repletion of blood vessels with blood, humours or pneuma with same proportion

signs and symptoms of qualitative replet.def: Signs and symptoms of qualitative repletion of blood vessels against the medicatrix naturae

signs and symptoms of obstruction.def: Signs and symptoms of obstruction due to retention of morbid matter in any organ, cavity or vessel Description

signs and symptoms of flatus.def: Signs and symptoms of distension/ retention of flatus; signs and symptoms of distension commonly in the abdomen, which are pain, movement and sound due to flatus and feeling of flatus by touch

signs and symptoms of swelling/ inflamma.def: These include heaviness, loss of function, increased temperature and pain if found in pain-sensitive organ

signs and symptoms of loss of continuity.def: These include pain, discharge and signs and symptoms which vary with different organs

heaviness in the whole body.def: Heaviness in the whole body is a feature of predominance of sanguine

heaviness of the head.def: Heaviness of the head is a usual symptom of predominance of sanguine

heaviness in the eyes.def: Heaviness in the eyes is a usual symptom of predominance of sanguine

heaviness in the temporal region.def: Heaviness in the temporal region is a symptom of predominance of sanguine

breaking bodyache.def: Breaking/aching/stretching pain in the body. Feeling of pain and breaking of organs of the body, is a symptom of predominance of sanguine

fatigue.def: Feeling of strain/stretching of an organ of the body is a symptom of predominance of sanguine

stretching the body standard terminologi.def: Stretching of a body organ due to fatigue is a symptom of predominance of sanguine Description

yawning.def: An involuntary deep inspiration due to fatigue is a symptom of predominance of sanguine

dullness of sensorium.def: Disturbance/diminution of functioning of sensorium of the body, is sign and symptom of predominance of sanguine

mental dullness.def: Mental dullness is a sign and symptom of predominance of sanguine

redness of face.def: Redness of the face is a symptom of predominance of sanguine

flabby body.def: Flabbiness/softness of body, i.e., loose, soft and fleshy body, is a symptom of predominance of phlegm

sluggishness of the body.def: Feeling of dullness and tiredness of the body is the symptom of predominance of phlegm

looseness of the nerve.def: Looseness of nerves is a symptom of predominance of phlegm

excessive pallor of body.def: Whitish discolouration of body/ excessive pallor of body is a sign of predominance of phlegm Description

softness of body.def: Feeling of softness of the body is a sign of predominance of phlegm

excessive salivation.unani: Kathrat-i-Lu‘āb / Kathra al-Rīq/Kathra al-Baṣq/ Kathra al-Buṣāq
excessive salivation.def: Excessive salivation is a symptom of predominance of phlegm; the term does not cover Kathra al-Lu‘āb mentioned under diseases

sour eructation / acidic eructation.def: Sour eructation/belching is a symptom of predominance of phlegm

decreased thirst.def: Decrease in thirst is a symptom of predominance of phlegm

yellowish complexion.def: Yellowish discolouration is a feature of predominance of yellow bile

yellowish face.def: Yellowish discolouration of face is a sign of bilious predominance

yellowish eyes; icterus.def: Yellow discoloration of eyes is a sign of bilious predominance

bitter taste in mouth.def: Bitter taste in mouth is a symptom of bilious predominance

roughness of tongue.def: Roughness of tongue is a symptom of bilious predominance

feeling pleasure in a cold breeze polydy.unani: Al-Iltidhādh bi’l Nasīm al-Bārid Shidda al-‘Aṭash
feeling pleasure in a cold breeze polydy.def: Feeling pleasure in a cold breeze is a feature of predominance of yellow bile Increased thirst is a symptom of predominance of yellow bile; the term does not cover ‘Aṭash Mufriṭ mentioned under diseases Description

bitter vomiting.def: Bitter vomiting is a symptom of predominance of yellow bile

excessive dryness of the body.def: Excessive dryness of the body in which the body seems very dry is a feature of predominance of black bile

dryness of the body.def: Dryness of the body is a feature of predominance of black bile

dusky complexion.def: Swarthy/dusky complexion of the body is a feature of predominance of black bile

blackish complexion.def: Blackish/brown/dark colouration of the body is a feature of predominance of black bile

excess of obsessive thoughts.def: Excess of obsessive thoughts is a feature of predominance of black bile

ill thought.def: Incidence of ill thought is a feature of predominance of black bile

epigastric burning / heart burn.def: Burning sensation of chest due to the movement of secretion of stomach toward oesophagus

pain in head.unani: Al-Alam fi’l Ra’s / Al-Waja‘ fi’l Ra’s

pain with tension in head.unani: Al-Waja‘ Ma‘ al- Tamaddud fi’l Ra’s

severe pain in head headache shifting to.unani: Al-Waja‘ al-Shadīd fi’l Ra’s Mayl al-Ṣudā‘ ilā Shiqq al-Ra’s al-Ayman

headache shifting to left half of head.unani: Mayl al-Ṣudā‘ ilā Shiqq al-Ra’s al-Aysar

staring.def: Staring/ fixing one’s glance on a certain object or point

bad mood witout known cause; nausea.def: 1. Bad mood witout known cause; 2. nausea

migration of pain.def: Migration of pain from one part of body to another

burning sensation of face without feelin.unani: Al-Iltihāb fi’l Wajh bi Ghayr al-Thiqal

inability to see at night.def: The term does not cover Al-‘Ashā’ mentioned under diseases

inability to see in daylight.def: The term does not cover Al-Jahar mentioned under diseases

crust-forming deep dirty corneal ulcers.def: The term does not cover Awqūmā mentioned in the diseases section Description

cloudy appearance in pupil.unani: Ẓuhūr Shabīh al-Ḍabāb fī Thaqb al-Ḥadaqa

epiphora.def: A disease characterized by watery eyes and continuous lacrimation in later stages

excessive lacrimation without irritation.unani: Kathra al-Dumū‘ min Ghayr Ladh‘ wa Lā Ḥarāra

photophobia.def: Intolerance of light caused by sanguineous meningitis or diseases of eye

mydriasis.def: Dilatation of pupil due to headache, trauma or any other cause related to eye

miosis.unani: Inqibāḍ Thaqb al-Nāẓir / Ṣighar Thaqb al-Nāẓir

blockage of lacrimal duct.unani: Imtinā‘ Fuḍūl al-‘Ayn mina’l Jarayān ila’l Anf

excessive secretion from eyes passing of.unani: Kathra Jarayān al- Ruṭūba mina’l ‘Ayn Jarayān al-Midda al-Muntina mina’l Mankhar ila’l Fam

feeling of distension in ear without thr.unani: Al-Tamaddud fi’l Udhun min Ghayr Ḍarabān

feeling of distension in ear with severe.unani: Al-Tamaddud fi’l Udhun ma‘ al-Alam al-Shadīd

feeling of distension in ear without sev.unani: Al-Tamaddud fi’l Udhun min Ghayr Alam Shadīd

tinnitus.def: Ringing of ears; the term does not cover Dawī-o-Ṭanīn-o-Ṣafīr mentioned under diseases

Ringing or buzzing noise in ears; a disease characterized by perception of high-pitched or low-pitched sound in the ears which is not from an external source; this term does not cover Ṭanīn al-Udhunayn mentioned under si

anosmia.def: A disease characterized by loss of sense of smell Description

inability to swallow.def: The term does not cover ‘Usr al-Bal‘ mentioned in the disease section

dysphagia.def: The term does not cover ‘Usr al-Bal‘ mentioned in the disease section
Difficulty in swallowing; a morbid state caused by disturbed temperament of oesophagus and characterized by difficulty in swallowing

dryness of mouth.unani: Yubs al-Fam /Jafāf al-Fam/Nashāfa al-Fam/ Qaḥal al-Fam

salty taste of mouth sour taste of mouth.unani: Ṭa‘m al-Fam al-Māliḥ Ḥumūḍa al-Fam/ Turshī-i-Dahan

sialorrhoea.def: A morbid state usually caused by excess of heat and moistness of stomach; this term does not cover Kathrat-i-Lu‘āb mentioned under signs and symptoms

heaviness of tongue.def: A morbid state characterized by difficulty in speaking and pronouncing words

dryness of tongue.def: A morbid state caused by increased heat and dryness or presence of sticky and viscous secretion Description

severe burning eruptions of oral cavity.unani: Shidda al-Iltihāb fi’l Qulā‘ Ghilẓa al-Shafa

long breathing.def: Breathing with a longer span of inspiration and expiration

short breathing.def: Breathing with a shorter span of inspiration and expiration

double respiration.def: Breathing with abnormal pause during inspiration and expiration

difficulty in breathing.def: The term does not cover Ḍīq al-Nafas mentioned under diseases

exacerbation of cough on movement or wit.unani: Ziyāda al-Su‘āl ma‘ al- Ḥaraka wa’l Jū‘

burning sensation in abdomen excessive b.unani: Al-Iḥtirāq fi’l Baṭn Shidda al-Iḥtirāq fi’l Baṭn

pruritus in anus.def: The term does not cover Ḥikka al-Maq‘ad mentioned under diseases

comfort from reduced intake of diets of.unani: Al-Intifā‘ bi’l Iqlāl mina’l Aghdhiya al-Ḥārra

phlegmatic vomiting.def: A morbid state characterized by expulsion of phlegmatic material through vomiting, decreased thirst and sour, salty or sweet taste, borborygmi and increased salivation

borborygmus.def: Rumbling noise in stomach; a morbid state caused by cold morbid temperament of stomach and its decreased innate heat, intake of bad quality food or accumulation of phlegm or black bile in stomach

desire to eat clay and gypsum.unani: Al-Ishtiyāq ilā Akl al-Ṭīn wa’l Jaṣṣ

passing of fresh blood in stool.unani: Khurūj al-Dam al-Aḥmar al-Mukhaḍḍab fi’l Barāz

passing of blood similar to raw meat was.unani: Khurūj al-Dam al- Ghusālī fi’l Barāz

heaviness in abdomen without pain.unani: Al-Thiqal fi’l Baṭn min Ghayr Waja‘

episodic discharge of blood in urine.unani: Khurūj al-Dam fi’l Bawl bi Adwār

distended bladder without pain.unani: Al-Tamaddud fi’l Mathāna min Ghayr Waja‘

erection of penis without any stimulus.unani: Intishār al-Qaḍīb min Ghayr Sabab / Al-In‘āẓ min Ghayr Ikhtilāj

frequent passing of urine without irrita.unani: Kathra Khurūj al-Bawl bi Ghayr Ladh‘

priapism.def: Uncontrolled penile erection; uncontrolled painful penile erection

cessation of menstruation for a long tim.def: The term does not cover Iḥtibās al-Ṭamth mentioned in disease section

bleeding during intercourse feeling of u.unani: Khurūj al-Dam Waqt al-Jimā‘ Iḥsās al-Thiqal ilā Jānib

distension of breast.def: Non-inflammatory distention of breast due to accumulation of milk Description

stable pain without severe burning.unani: Al-Waja‘ al-Rāsikh al-Dā’im bi Ghayr Talahhub

feeling of heaviness without severe burn.unani: Iḥsās al-Thiqal min Ghayr Talahhub wa Lā Ḥurqa

whitish discoloration of joint skin with.unani: Bayāḍ Lawn al-Mafāṣil min Ghayr Ḥarāra

severe pain of joints without swelling.unani: Al-Alam al-Shadīd bi Ghayr Intifākh fi’l Mafāṣil

anorexia.unani: Buṭlān al-Shahwa / Suqūṭ al-Shahwa / Dhahāb al-Shahwa
anorexia.def: Loss of appetite; a morbid state in which patient has no desire to eat

greying of hair.def: A morbid condtion due to weakness of innate heat of body or predominance of phlegm

high-pitched voice.def: A morbid state caused by predominance of coldness, excessive evacuation, physical exertion and excessive sleeplessness

blephrospasm.def: The term does not cover Ṭarf al-Ajfān mentioned under diseases

inference from character of excretory pr.unani: Al-Istidlāl bi mā Yabruzu mina’l Badan

inference from rate of responsiveness of.unani: Al-Istidlāl min Sur‘a Infi‘āl al-A‘ḍā’ wa Buṭū’ihā

decreased responsive- ness of heart to f.unani: Qilla Infi‘āl al-Qalb ‘an Asbāb al-Khawf wa’l Faz‘

increased responsive- ness of heart to f.unani: Kathra Infi‘āl al-Qalb ‘an Asbāb al-Khawf wa’l Faz‘

rigidity.def: The term does not cover Jumūd mentioned under diseases

moderate temperature on touch.unani: I‘tidāl al-Malmas fi’l Ḥarr wa’l Bard

moderately dry and moist to touch.unani: I‘tidāl al-Malmas fi’l Yubūsa wa’l Ruṭūba

moderate texture on touch.unani: I‘tidāl al-Malmas fi’l Līn wa’l Ṣalāba

moderate/ normal colour of skin.unani: I‘tidāl al-Lawn fi’l Bayāḍ wa’l Ḥumra
moderate/ normal colour of skin.def: With respect to race, heredity and geography

moderate appearance in terms of build of.unani: I‘tidāl al-Saḥna fi’l Siman wa’l Qaḍāfa

moderately prominent veins.unani: I‘tidāl al-‘Urūq bayn al-Ghā’ira wa’l Bāriza

moderate appearance of vessels in terms.unani: I‘tidāl al-‘Urūq bayn al-Ghilẓa wa’l Diqqa

moderate hair in terms of volume and len.unani: I‘tidāl al-Sha‘r fi’l Zabab wa’l Dhu‘r

moderate hair in curliness and straightn.unani: I‘tidāl al-Sha‘r fi’l Ju‘ūda wa’l Subūṭa

coordinated movements of organs.unani: Muwātāt al-A‘ḍā’ fī Ḥarakātihā wa Salāsatihā

moderate elimination of bodily wastes.unani: I‘tidāl al-Nafḍ fi’l Kayf wa’l Kamm

sharpness of imagination, thinking and m.unani: Quwwa al-Takhayyul wa’l Tafakkur wa’l Tadhakkur

balanced behaviour.unani: Al-Tawassuṭ mina’l Akhlāq bayn al-Ifrāṭ wa’l Tafrīṭ

moderate behaviour in terms of cruelty a.unani: Al-I‘tidāl bayn al- Qasāwa wa’l Riqqa

signs/symptoms related to crisis indicat.unani: Al-Dalā’il al-Mundhira bi’l Salāma fi’l Buḥrān

warning signs/symptoms indicating bad pr.unani: Al-‘Alāmāt al-Mundhira bi’l Ridā’a fi’l Buḥrān

warning fatal signs / symptoms during cr.unani: Al-‘Alāmāt al-Mundhira bi’l Halāk fi’l Buḥrān

pulse.def: Movement of expansion and contraction of arteries for the preservation of innate heat of body, supply of vital pneuma and production of psychic pneuma

pulse-beat.def: Each beat of pulse felt on palpation of arteries, which consists of two periods of movement (contraction and expansion) and two of pause (pauses after each contraction and expansion)

features of pulse.def: Ten features of pulse, through which inferences regarding various conditions of the body are derived

volume of pulse.def: Feature of pulse related to its degree of expansion

long pulse.def: Pulse whose parts are perceived to be greater in length when compared with absolutely normal pulse

short pulse.def: Pulse whose parts are perceived to be shorter in length when compared with absolutely normal pulse Description

broad pulse.def: Pulse whose parts are perceived to be broader in breadth when compared with absolutely normal pulse

constricted pulse.def: Pulse whose parts are perceived to be narrower in breadth when compared with absolutely normal pulse

elevated pulse.def: Pulse whose parts are perceived to be more elevated in height when compared with absolutely normal pulse

sinking pulse.def: Pulse whose parts are perceived to be less elevated in height when compared with absolutely normal pulse

pulsus magnus/ large pulse.def: Pulse increased in both length and breadth as well as in elevation

pulsus parvus/ small pulse.def: Pulse diminished in both length and breadth as well as in elevation

thick pulse.def: Pulse increased in both breadth and height

slender pulse.def: Pulse decreased in both breadth and height

strength of pulse.def: Feature of pulse related to quality of force produced by the movement of pulse on the arterial wall

pulsus fortis / strong pulse.def: Pulse which resists fingers of physician during palpation

weak pulse standard terminologies on una.def: Pulse which does not resist fingers of physician during palpation Description

duration of pulse movement.def: Feature of pulse related to movement of pulse between two beats

rapid pulse.def: Pulse whose movement is completed in a shorter time

slow pulse.def: Pulse whose movement is completed in a longer time

duration of pause.def: Feature of pulse related to its pause between two beats

pulsus frequens.unani: Nabḍ Mutawātir / Nabḍ Mutadārik/ Nabḍ Mutakāthif
pulsus frequens.def: Pulse having small pause between two beats

pulsus rarus.def: Pulse having long pause between two beats

consistency of pulse.def: Feature of pulse related to its softness or hardness

competence of pulse.def: Feature of pulse related to its fullness and emptiness

pulsus plenus / full pulse.def: Pulse which seems to be full of blood

pulsus vacuus / collapsing pulse.def: Pulse which seems to be empty Description

feel of pulse.def: Feature of pulse related to its temperature

moderate normal pulse.def: A term applicable to average of long and short pulse, large and small pulse, thick and slender pulse, strong and weak pulse, swift and sluggish pulse, soft and hard pulse, full and collapsing pulse, hot and cold pulse, a

equality and inequality of pulse.def: Feature of pulse related to its equality and inequality in terms of similarity of the strokes or the parts of a stroke or a single part of a stroke in largeness and smallness, strength and weakness, swiftness and sluggis

equal pulse.def: Pulse equal in its five features, i.e., largeness and smallness, strength and weakness, swiftness and sluggishness, rapidness and slowness, and hardness and softness

pulse equal in its five features standar.def: Pulse equal in largeness and smallness, strength and weakness, swiftness and sluggishness, rapidness and slowness, and hardness and softness Description

pulse equal in any one of its five featu.def: Pulse which is equal in any one of its five features, i.e., largeness and smallness, strength and weakness, swiftness and sluggishness, rapidness and slowness, and hardness and softness

unequal pulse.def: Pulse unequal in its five features, i.e., largeness and smallness, strength and weakness, swiftness and sluggishness, rapidness and slowness, and hardness and softness

pulse unequal in its all five features.def: Pulse unequal in largeness and smallness, strength and weakness, swiftness and sluggishness, rapidness and slowness, and hardness and softness

pulse unequal in any one of its five fea.def: Pulse unequal in any one of its five features, i.e., largeness and smallness, strength and weakness, swiftness and sluggishness, rapidness and slowness, and hardness and softness

intermittent pulse.def: Variation of a pulse-beat felt only under one finger, which is marked by faint interruption
Compound pulse in which pause occurs where movement is expected

recurrent pulse.def: An abnormal condition of pulse in which a large pulse becomes small and returns to the original height in a gradual manner

continuous pulse.def: Pulse in which the increase or decrease in any feature appears in such a gradual manner that the change cannot be perceived

orderliness and disorder- liness of puls.def: Feature of pulse related to its orderliness and disorderliness Description

regularly irregular pulse.def: Pulse which varies in a definite order

regularly irregular pulse in general.def: Pulse in which only one irregularity is repeated

recurrent regularly irregular pulse.def: Pulse, in which two or more irregularities go on repeating in cycles

irregularly irregular pulse.def: Pulse which does not vary in in a definite order

rhythm of pulse.def: Feature of pulse related to the time between the two periods of movement and the two periods of rest

pulse with normal rhythm.def: Pulse with rhythm in accordance with age of person

pulse with abnormal rhythm.unani: Nabḍ Radī al-Wazn / Nabḍ Sayyi’ al-Wazn
pulse with abnormal rhythm.def: Pulse with rhythm not in accordance with age of person

rhythm of pulse similar to that of perso.unani: Nabḍ Mutaghayyir al-Wazn/ Nabḍ Mujāwiz al-Wazn
rhythm of pulse similar to that of perso.def: Pulse which acquires the rhythm of the pulse of person belonging to adjacent age group, e.g., rhythm of a child’s pulse becomes that of the pulse of a young man
Pulse which acquires the rhythm of the pulse of person not belonging to adjacent age group, e.g., rhythm of a child’s pulse corresponds to that of an old man’s pulse

arrhythmic pulse standard terminologies.def: Pulse whose rhythm does not match any age Description

compound pulse.def: Pulse that is irregular in two or more features

spindle-shaped pulse.unani: Nabḍ Misallī / Nabḍ Munḥadir/ Nabḍ Mā’il al-Wasaṭ
spindle-shaped pulse.def: Compound pulse which starts from smallness, increases up to a certain limit, starts declining and comes back to the original position

supernumerary pulse.def: Compound pulse in which an extra beat appears during the period of rest after the first beat has ended

dicrotic pulse.unani: Nabḍ Dhu’l Qar‘atayn / Nabḍ Mutadākhil/ Nabḍ Miṭraqī
dicrotic pulse.def: Compound pulse which is considered to be single-beat by some physicians and coupled- beat by others

mouse-tail pulse.def: Compound pulse which gradually increases or decreases in one or more features of the beats

elapsed mouse-tail pulse.def: Mouse-tail pulse which is irregular in the expansion of the beat and starts as large pulse but does not end on a definite limit of smallness

continuous mouse-tail pulse.def: Mouse-tail pulse which starts as large pulse and ends as small pulse

recurrent mouse-tail pulse.def: Mouse-tail pulse which starts as large pulse and ends as small pulse, thereafter rebounding to the state of large pulse

deer-leap pulse.def: Compound pulse which has its beats sluggish in initial phase but later suddenly becomes quick Description

vermicular pulse.def: Compound pulse similar to wavy pulse but is small and rapid

pulsus formicans.def: Compound pulse smaller and more rapid than vermicular pulse; in this kind of pulse, height and length are more affected than width in comparison with vermicular pulse

serrate pulse.def: Compound pulse resembling wavy pulse but firm and hard in nature

spasmodic pulse.def: Compound pulse in which there are repeated jerky movements similar to spasm

pulsus tremulus.def: Compound pulse which is feeble and trembling

twisting pulse.def: Compound pulse which seems similar to thread being twisted

cord-like pulse.def: Compound pulse which is a variety of twisting pulse but resembles the tremulous pulse

normal pulse.def: Strong, regular, rhythmic pulse which lies within the normal range of volume, duration of movement and pause, consistency, temperature and competence

pulse of children.def: Pulse which is swift, rapid and large

pulse in age of decline.def: Pulse which is smaller, more sluggish, slower and hard

pulse of old age.def: Pulse which is smaller, more sluggish and slower in comparison with pulse of age of decline

pulse of males.def: Pulse which is stronger and larger than the pulse of females

pulse of females.def: Pulse which is less strong and large in comparison to pulse of males

pulse of pregnant woman.def: Pulse which is larger, more swift and regular

pulse during spring.def: Pulse which is larger and stronger

pulse during summer.def: Pulse which is swift, rapid and weak

pulse during winter.def: Pulse which is sluggish, slow and weak

pulse during autumn.def: Pulse which is weak, unequal and hard Description

pulse of anger.def: Pulse which is elevated, strong, swift and rapid

pulse of extreme fear.def: Pulse which is unequal, swift, feeble and trembling

pulse of sorrow.def: Pulse which is sluggish, small and slow

pulse of joy.def: Pulse which is slow and sluggish but large and soft

pulse of grief urine.def: Pulse which is small, weak, sluggish and slow

examination of urine.def: Physical examination of urine sample to make a diagnosis; the term does not cover chemical and microscopic examination of urine

indicators of urine.def: Markers of urine, e.g., colour, density, turbidity or transparency, sediment, quantity, odour and froth

indications from colours of urine.def: Colours of urine (yellow, red, white, green and black), which are observed in order to make a diagnosis

degrees of yellowness of urine standard.def: Extent of yellowness of urine indicating different body conditions Description

straw-yellow urine.def: Urine that resembles fodder-soaked water and indicates a lesser degree of heat as compared to normal urine

citron-yellow urine.def: Colour of normal urine, resembling citron peel

reddish-yellow urine.def: Urine which indicates some degree of heat in comparison to normal urine

orange-yellow urine.def: Colour of urine that resembles orange peel but the red colour is more prominent as compared to reddish-yellow urine and indicates higher degree of heat

flame-yellow urine.def: Colour of urine that resembles saffron-soaked water and indicates higher degree of heat in comparison to orange-yellow urine

saffron-yellow urine.def: Colour of urine that resembles filaments of saffron and indicates higher degree of heat in comparison to reddish-yellow, orange-yellow and flame-yellow urine

degrees of redness of urine.def: Extent of redness of urine indicating different body conditions

yellow-reddish urine.def: Urine indicating predominance of sanguine in body

rose-red urine.def: Urine indicating predominance of sanguine in body

blackish-red urine.def: Urine indicating predominance of sanguine in body Description

intense red urine.def: Urine indicating predominance of sanguine in body

degrees of greenness of urine.def: Extent of greenness of urine indicating different body conditions

pistachio-green urine.def: Yellow-coloured urine mixed with a little black colour, indicating predominance of cold in the body

verdigris-green urine.def: Urine which indicates extreme combustion in the body

sky-green urine.def: Urine which indicates extremely cold morbid temperament

emerald-green urine.def: Urine which indicates cold morbid temperament

leek-green urine.def: Urine which indicates extreme combustion in the body but is less risky than verdigris-green urine

degrees of blackness of urine.def: Extent of blackness of urine indicating different body conditions

dark urine approaching blackness.def: Urine which denotes extreme combustion of humours, extreme cold, extinction of the innate heat and evacuation of melancholic wastes by the medicatrix naturae after crisis

white urine standard terminologies on un.def: A type of urine which may be transparent or translucent Description

transparent urine.def: White-coloured transparent urine which denotes coldness of temperament and defective maturation

translucent urine.def: White-coloured urine which resembles milk and is viscous in nature

mucilaginous urine.def: Urine that denotes excess of immature phlegm

oily urine.def: Urine that contains emulsified fat derived from tissues

waxy urine.def: Urine that contains phlegm from actual or threatened dissolution of tissues

champagne-like urine.def: Urine mixed with pus indicates ulceration of urinary tract,without pus indicates presence of partially matured phlegm or stone in the bladder

semen-like urine.def: Urine that passes during crisis of phlegmatic swellings, in case where the tissues are loose and in diseases caused by vitreous phlegm

lead-white urine.def: Urine which is a grave sign when passes without sediment

milky urine.def: Urine which is a fatal sign when passed in the course of an acute fever

complex colours of urine.def: Colours of urine which are a mixture of different colours

urine like colour of raw- meat washings.def: Urine which denotes weakness of liver or excess of blood Description

urine like colour of olive oil.def: Clear, viscid and moderately dense urine, in which the yellow colour is tinged with oily translucence; it has an oily sheen and indicates bad prognosis

purple urine.def: Urine that points towards fatal prognosis as it is an indication of the combustion of yellow and black bile

flame-red urine.def: Urine in which red colour is tinged with black and is a sign of any complicated fever or fever occurring due to thick humour

density of urine.def: A term referring to thinness and thickness of urine

urine of thin consistency.def: Urine which generally shows that maturation is incomplete and sometimes indicates obstruction in the blood vessels or weakness of kidneys

urine of thick consistency.def: Urine which generally shows defective maturation and sometimes indicates elimination of thick mature humours

odourless urine.def: Urine indicative of cold morbid temperament, excessive rawness and extinction of innate heat in case of acute diseases

foul-smelling urine standard terminologi.def: Urine indicative of predominance of yellow bile; foul sour-smelling urine is indicative of predominance of black bile Description

froth of urine.def: A term referring to froth arising from moisture and gas forced into flask with the voiding of urine

blackness of froth.def: Froth of urine, which denotes jaundice

reddish-yellow froth.def: Froth of urine, which denotes jaundice

coarseness of urine bubbles.def: Urine bubbles, which denote viscidity of matter

excess of urine bubbles.def: Abundance of urine bubbles, which denotes viscidity of matter and presence of gas

delayed bursting of urine bubbles.def: Bursting of urine bubbles in a slow manner, which indicates viscidity of matter and presence of gas

sediments and deposits in urine.def: A term referring to deposits as well as suspended matter in a urine sample Description

normal sediments.def: Sediments that are white, smooth, round, regular, light and delicate in appearance, which settle at the bottom and consist of uniform particles

abnormal sediments.def: Sediments devoid of qualities of normal sediment

flaky sediments.def: Large red or large white sediments, usually coming from organs of urinary system; white flaky sediments come from the bladder and red or fleshy flaky sediments come from the kidney

flaky sediments like hulls of grain.def: Small and thick scales denoting bladder disorder or wasting of organs

flaky grumous sediments.def: Reddish small and thick scales denoting the destruction of liver substance or combustion of blood; occasionally they come from kidneys

flaky gritty sediments.def: Scales which are produced due to burning of blood

flaky sediments resembling coarse partic.def: Scales when reddish denote burning of blood; dark scales show excessive destruction of blood

flat and large flaky sediments.def: Flat and large white or red scales which generally arise from the immediate urinary organs, e.g., kidneys, bladder

fleshy sediments standard terminologies.def: Sediments which arise from kidneys or from the breakdown of tissues in the body Description

fatty sediments.def: Sediments which generally denote dissolution of solid and liquid fat as well as flesh

purulent sediments.def: Sediments which denote discharge of pus from ulcers of urinary organs

mucoid sediments.def: Sediments which generally denote presence of large quantity of thick immature humour in the body

sediments like a wet piece of leaven.def: Sediments which denote weakness of stomach and intestine and impairment of digestion

sediments of blood clots.def: Sediments which generally denote weakness of liver when found in large quantity and ulceration or injury of the urinary tract when found in small quantity

fibrillary sediments.def: Sediments which indicate that active heat has coagulated the elongated exudates and converted them into fibrils

sandy sediments.def: Sediments which indicate stone formation or stone disintegration

ashy sediments.def: Sediments which denote change in colour of pus or mucus due to their prolonged stagnation

quantity of sediments.def: A term referring to abundance and scantiness of sediments Description

abundance of sediments.def: A term denoting the abundance of sediments in urine due to excess of causative agent

scantiness of sediments.def: A term denoting the scantiness of sediments in urine due to lack of causative agent

dark-coloured sediments.def: Sediments which denote excessive burning of blood, excessive cold or failure of innate heat

red sediments.def: Sediments which denote the predominance of sanguine or impaired digestion

yellow sediments.def: Sediments which denote the presence of strong heat or insidious disease

green sediments.def: Appearance of dark-coloured sediments in urine

white sediments.def: Sediments which may be normal or abnormal (purulent, mucoid and gummy denoting deficient maturation)

composition of sediments standard termin.def: A term referring to constitution, coherence and consistence of sediments Description

smoothness of sediment.def: State of sediments which is normal when found in normal urine but a bad sign when found in abnormal urine

discreteness of sediments.def: State of sediments which denotes gases and impaired digestion

position of sediments.def: A term referring to position of sediments in urine sample

floating sediments.def: Sediments usually indicating a lesser degree of maturation

excess of urine.def: Excretion of more than a sufficient quantity of urine, denoting wasting disease or elimination of liquefied wastes

scanty urine.def: Excretion of insufficient quantity of urine, which denotes the impairment of vitality, excessive perspiration, diarrhoea or impending dropsy

urine of infants.def: Urine which tends to be milky due to milk intake and whitish due to predominance of moisture on the temperament during this age

urine of children.def: Urine which is more thick and viscid in comparison with urine of adults Description

urine of adults.def: Urine which is yellowish and of moderate consistency

urine in age of decline.def: Urine which is usually whitish and of thin consistency but may at times become thick due to the elimination of waste materials

urine of elderly person.def: Urine which is white and of very thin consistency

urine of females.def: Urine which is always more thick, white and dull than in men

urine of males.def: Urine which, on shaking, becomes turbid at the top and shows intertwined threads when collected after coitus

urine of pregnant women.def: Urine which is clear with appearance of a faint cloud at the top and a piece of ginned cotton floating in the middle

urine of women during puerperium stool i.def: Urine which is almost jet black Drawing inference from properties of stool

quantity of stool standard terminologies.def: A term referring to increased or decreased quantity of stool Description

larger quantity of stool.def: Quantity of stool which denotes presence of excessive humours in the body

lesser quantity of stool.def: Quantity of stool which denotes presence of low quantity of humours in the body or retention of much of stool in caecum and colon

consistency of stool.def: A term referring to moistness and dryness of stool

moist stool.def: Consistency of stool which denotes indigestion, obstructions, weakness of the mesentery and catarrhal secretions coming from head

dry stool.def: Consistency of stool which denotes fatigue, excessive urination, presence of excessive heat in the body, use of dry foods and constipation

colour of stool.def: A term referring to different colours of stool denoting various body conditions

markedly yellow stool.def: Colour of stool which denotes excess of yellow bile in the body

white stool.def: Colour of stool which denotes obstruction in the bile duct and jaundice

blackish stool.def: Colour of stool which denotes excessive combustion, maturation of causative matter of melancholic diseases or intake of any coloured diet

greenish stool.def: Colour of stool which denotes extinction of the innate heat Description

shape of stool.def: A term referring to compactness or puffiness of stool

puffed-up stool.def: Shape of stool which denotes flatulence

bowel transit time.def: A term referring to time occupied in passage of stool through the bowel

rapid passing of stool.def: Passing out of stool before the usual time, which denotes excess of bile in gall bladder and weakness of retentive faculty

delayed passing of stool.def: Delay in passage of stool, which denotes weakness of digestion, coldness of intestines and excess of moisture

normal stool sputum.def: Stool which passes out easily without any irritation and remains compact, homogenous and yellowish in colour

yellowish sputum.def: Sputum that denotes predominance of yellow bile and severity of heat

saffron-red sputum.def: Sputum that denotes predominance of sanguine

flame-red sputum standard terminologies.def: Sputum that denotes predominance of sanguine and heat Description

light black sputum.def: Sputum that denotes coldness, immaturity and heat

blackish sputum.def: Sputum that denotes combustion and bad quality of disease-causing matter

thin sputum.def: Sputum which denotes that disease-causing matter is still in an immature state

thick sputum.def: Sputum denoting the thickness of disease- causing matter

excessive expectoration.def: Excessive expectoration denotes maturity of disease-causing matter and stationary phase of disease

scanty expectoration.def: Scanty expectoration denotes progressive phase of disease

delayed expectoration.def: Condition which denotes serious disease condition and bad quality of disease- causing matter

sputum of good quality sweat.def: White and smooth sputum of normal consistency, coughed up easily

yellowish sweat.def: Sweat that denotes predominance of yellow bile in the body

reddish sweat.def: Sweat that denotes predominance of sanguine in the body

light black sweat.def: Sweat that denotes predominance of black bile in the body

greenish sweat.def: Sweat that denotes predominance of black bile in the body

blackish sweat.def: Sweat that denotes predominance of black bile in the body

whitish sweat standard terminologies on.def: Sweat that denotes healthy condition of the body Description

bitter sweat.def: Sweat that denotes predominance of yellow bile in body

sweet sweat.def: Sweat that denotes predominance of sanguine in body

sour sweat.def: Sweat that denotes predominance of phlegm and black bile in body

fetid sweat.def: Condition that denotes severity of putrefaction and heat

sharp-smelling sweat.def: Condition that denotes presence of bilious, pungent and sharp matter

sweat of thin consistency.def: Condition that denotes thinness of disease- causing matter

sweat of thick consistency standard term.def: Condition that denotes thickness of disease- causing matter texts. Description

simple headache.def: Headache without the involvement of matter, characterized by dryness of nostrils and absence of heaviness in the head

simple hot headache.def: Headache due to simple hot morbid temperament, characterized by increased temperature of head, dryness of tongue, oral cavity and nostrils, sleeplessness and burning sensation in head and eyes along with absence of heavi

external heat-induced headache.def: Heat-induced headache without involvement of matter, caused by simple hot morbid temperament as a result of exposure to certain external factors, e.g., walking under sun, staying near fire, etc. Description

internal heat-induced headache.def: Headache due to the intake of heat-producing things without involvement of matter, caused by simple hot morbid temperament as a result of intake of pepper, ginger, garlic, etc.

simple cold headache.def: Headache due to simple cold morbid temperament, characterized by decreased temperature of head and low-intensity pain in the posterior part of head with a tendency to increase in the morning and evening

external cold-induced headache.def: Cold-induced headache without involvement of matter, caused by simple cold morbid temperament as a result of exposure to certain external factors, e.g., exposure to cold environment, staying in cold places, etc.

internal cold-induced headache.def: Headache due to intake of cold-producing things without involvement of matter, caused by simple cold morbid temperament as a result of intake of diets or drugs of cold temperament and drinking of water after eating fruit

headache due to morbid matter.def: Headache due to involvement of matter, characterized by moistness of nostrils and heaviness of head

headache due to hot morbid matter.def: Headache due to involvement of hot causative matter, characterized by moistness of nostrils and heaviness of head along with redness of face

sanguineous headache standard terminolog.def: Headache caused by predominance of sanguine, which is characterized by heaviness, red face and eyes, nasal irritation and engorged jugular vein Description

bilious headache.def: Headache caused by predominance of yellow bile, characterized by increased intensity of pain with mild heaviness in the head, yellow discoloration of face and eyes, rapid pulse, decreased sleep, bitter taste and dry oral

headache due to cold morbid matter.def: Headache due to involvement of cold matter, chronic in nature and characterized by decreased temperature of head along with heaviness in the head

phlegmatic headache.def: Headache caused by predominance of phlegm, characterized by heaviness in the head, decreased temperature of head, moist nostrils, whitish discoloration of face, lethargy, sleepiness, mental dullness, etc.

melancholic headache.def: Headache caused by predominance of black bile, characterized by mild heaviness in the head, sleeplessness and irrelevant thoughts

headache due to internal gases.def: Headache caused by gases arising from the body or brain and accumulating in the head, characterized by decreased temperature of head and ringing in ear

headache due to exter- nal gases.def: Headache caused by gases of external origin which get accumulated in the brain and produce morbid condition through their quality and quantity, characterized by decreased temperature of head and ringing in ear

headache due to internal vapours.def: Headache caused by vapours originating from the head itself, characterized by severe pain, throbbing and ringing of ear Description

headache due to external vapours.def: Headache caused by accumulation in the head of vapours of bad quality from outside the body, characterized by severe pain, throbbing and ringing in ear

headache due to obstruction.def: Headache caused by obstruction produced in the brain itself or in its vessels or vessels of its coverings, characterized by heaviness and congestion of face

inflammatory headache.def: Headache due to inflammation, caused by inflammatory conditions of brain, meninges, skin covering the head or other related organs, e.g., stomach, uterus, etc.

throbbing headache.def: Headache characterized by congestion of blood vessels and their pulsation

headache due to weak- ness of brain.def: Headache triggered by insignificant causes, e.g., flatulence, fragrance, sound, etc., due to weakness of brain; this term does not cover Ṣudā‘ Ḥissī (headache due to increased sensitivity of brain) mentioned under diseas

headache due to increased sensitivity of.def: Headache triggered by insignificant causes due to increased sensitivity of normal functional brain; this term does not cover Ṣudā‘-i-Ḍu‘f Dimāghī (headache due to weakness of brain) mentioned under diseases

headache due to dryness standard termino.def: Headache caused by predominance of dryness in the body due to excessive evacuation, haemorrhage, sleeplessness, sorrow, grief, etc. Description

post-coital headache.def: Headache which occurs after excessive sexual intercourse

liquor-induced headache.def: Headache occurring after intake of excessive amount of liquor

smell-induced headache.def: Headache occurring after smelling good or bad smells

traumatic headache.def: Headache caused by head injury resulting in damage to brain, meninges, vessels, etc.

concussional headache.def: Headache occurring after a blow on the head, characterized by heaviness and feeling of distension in the head, forgetfulness and inability to differentiate between smells

headache due to worms.def: Headache caused by worms found in the area of brain nearest to nostrils, characterized by irritation in brain, rotten smell in nose, occasional dripping of blood or yellowish fluid through nostrils and increase in pain w

headache due to morbid matter of related.def: Headache due to morbidity of any related organ, aggravating or subsiding according to condition of causative factor

headache due to mor- bidity of stomach.def: Headache which starts from the anterior part of the head and is associated with nausea, indigestion, loss of appetite, etc. Description

headache due to morbidity of liver.def: Pain occurring on right side of head

headache due to morbidity of spleen.def: Pain occurring on left side of head

headache due to morbidity of peritoneum.def: Pain occurring on most anterior part of head

headache due to intestinal worms.def: Pain occurring on anterior part of head, characterized by increase in pain during hunger and movement and by foul smell from mouth and nose

headache due to morbidity of uterus.def: Pain occurring in middle of head, usually associated with amenorrhoea, abortion or childbirth

headache due to morbidity of diaphragm.def: Pain occurring in anterior part of middle of head

headache due to morbidity of kidneys.def: Pain occurring in posterior part of head

headache due to morbidity of spinal colu.def: Pain occurring in most posterior part of head

headache due to morbidity of extremities.def: Pain occurring in middle of anterior part of head preceded by sensation of ants crawling from extremities towards head Headache accompanied by any other disease, e.g., catarrh, fever, joint pain, gout, etc. Description

headache due to fever.def: Headache caused by fever, characterized by increase or decrease in its severity with increase and decrease of degree of fever

headache during crisis period of fevers.def: Headache during crisis period of fevers/any disease

circumferential head- ache.def: Severe continuous headache involving the whole head, characterized by liking of darkness, loneliness and silence, dislike of sound, light and movement, and aggravation by insignificant factors, e.g., slight movement, wal

sanguineous circumfer- ential headache.def: Circumferential headache due to predominance of sanguine, characterized by redness of face and other features of predominance of sanguine along with specific clinical features of circumferential headache

bilious circumferential headache.def: Circumferential headache due to predominance of yellow bile, characterized by burning sensation and sensation of piercing in the head and other features of predominance of yellow bile along with specific clinical feature

phlegmatic circumferen- tial headache.def: Circumferential headache due to predominance of phlegm, characterized by heaviness of head, whitish discoloration of body, lacrimation and puffiness of face along with specific clinical features of circumferential headac

melancholic circumfer- ential headache.def: Circumferential headache due to predominance of black bile, characterized by darkening of body colour and other features of predominance of black bile along with specific clinical features of circumferential headache

gaseous circumferential headache.def: Circumferential headache due to vapours and gases, characterized by feeling of tightness in the head and shifting of pain along with specific clinical features of circumferential headache

migraine.unani: Shaqīqa / Ṣudā‘ Niṣfī / Ṣudā‘ Ghathayānī / Ādhā Sīsī
migraine.def: Episodic headache; type of severe headache involving one half of the head, accompanied by nausea and vomiting

acute migraine.def: Episodic headache caused by sanguine or yellow bile and characterized by heaviness of head, severe pain, pulsation at the temples and relief with use of cold things

chronic migraine.def: Episodic headache caused by phlegm or black bile and characterized by heaviness of head without an increase of its temperature, repeated attacks of cold in the winter season and relief with use of hot things

episodic headache due to gases standard.def: Episodic headache caused by accumulation of gases in the head and characterized by absence of heaviness in the head without an increase in its temperature, feeling of tightness in the head and hissing in the ears Descrip

episodic headache due to vapours.def: Episodic headache, caused by vapours reaching to the head and characterized by absence of heaviness in the head with an increase in temperature of affected part, rapid pulse, tinnitus, increase in intensity of pain durin

frontal headache.def: Severe pain in eyebrow region with tearing sensation occurring in both eyebrows or in either with inability to open the eyes and dislike of light

irritation in brain.def: Irritation in the brain without pain, relieved by head massage and irrigation with lukewarm water on head

inflammation of brain or its meninges.def: A morbid state characterized by gradual increase in irrational talk and mental confusion, continuous fever with tendency to be higher during afternoon, restlessness, continuous movement of fingers, lint-picking, loud tal

sanguineous meningitis.def: Acute inflammation of meninges due to predominance of sanguine, characterized by continuous high-grade fever, headache, heaviness of head, redness of face and eyes, bleeding from nose, roughness of tongue, photophobia, i

bilious meningitis.def: Acute inflammation of meninges due to predominance of yellow bile, characterized by high-grade fever, excessive irrational talk, sleeplessness, anger, misbehaviour, absence of heaviness of head, yellowish colour of eyes,

phlegmatic meningitis.def: Chronic inflammation of meninges due to predominance of phlegm, characterized by continuous mild fever, sluggish reaction to stimuli, yawning, oversleeping, astonished eyes, prolonged silence, insanity and forgetfulness

melancholic meningitis.def: Chronic inflammation of meninges due to predominance of black bile, characterized by mild fever, decreased intellect, astonished eyes, excessive irrational talk, restlessness, fear, sleeplessness, absence of heaviness of

phlegmon.def: Inflammation of brain tissue due to sepsis in the sanguine humour, characterized by red eyes and cheeks, bulging eyes, nausea and vomiting, convulsions and tendency to fall backward

inflammation of brain tissue due to pred.def: A morbid state characterized by high-grade fever, pain and burning sensation in the head, cold face, yellow discoloration of face and eyes and dryness of mouth

inflammation of brain tissue due to burn.def: A morbid state characterized by extreme burning sensation in the head Description

inflammation of vessels of brain due to.def: A morbid state characterized by disappearance of cornea due to abnormal movement of eyes, comfort on lying on back, discoloration of face and flatulence

meningismus.def: Delirium without inflammation of brain or its meninges, characterized by sudden appearance of features of irrational talk and mental confusion

violent behaviour associ- ated with bili.def: A morbid state associated with inflammation of meninges due to yellow bile, characterized by sleeplessness, restlessness, nightmares leading to abrupt awakening, red eyes, irrelevant answers to questions along with sympt

subacute inflammation of brain of childr.def: A subacute type of inflammation, usually occurring in brain of children, accompanied by increased thirst along with features similar to inflammation of brain tissue due to predominance of yellow bile or bilious sanguine

sanguineous inflammation of brain and it.def: A morbid state characterized by severe pain, red face, bulging eyes and nausea

hydrocephalus.def: Build-up of fluid in brain, characterized by heaviness of head, open eyes and continuous lacrimation

deep sleep with phases of consciousness.def: A morbid state caused by predominance of phlegm, characterized by prolonged phase of deep sleep with short periods of consciousness, lethargy and delayed response to queries Description

consciousness with phases of deep sleep.def: A morbid state caused by predominance of yellow bile, characterized by consciousness accompanied with short periods of sleep, delirium, staring and deep sleep

prolonged state of deep sleep.def: A morbid state in which patient looks like he or she is sleeping and cannot be awakened easily; this term does not cover Sakta (unconsciousness) mentioned under diseases

prolonged state of deep sleep caused by.def: A morbid state characterized by decreased temperature of head, greenish or bluish discoloration of skin, forgetfulness, absence of puffiness of face and eyes and whitish urine
A morbid state characterized by heaviness of anterior part of head, excessive salivation, discharge from nostrils, twitching of eyebrows and eyes and delayed response to stimuli
A morbid state characterized by increased temperature of head, redness of face, eyes and tongue, and engorged vessels of neck
A morbid state characterized by stupor, rapid pulse and phlegmatic fever along with features related to organ from which the vapours have originated
A morbid state characterized by gradual loss of consciousness, general weakness with history of excessive physical exercise and excessive loss of blood and body fluids Description
A morbid state characterized by loss of consciousness with history of head injury

insomnia.def: A morbid state characterized by sleeplessness of such a degree as to cause dryness in the body

dryness-induced insomnia.def: State of sleeplessness caused by predominance of dryness either in terms of matter, i.e., black bile and yellow bile, or simple and marked by dryness of nose, mouth and tongue, etc.

moistness-induced insomnia.def: State of sleeplessness due to accumulation of salty humours in the brain, marked by heaviness of head, moist nostrils, excessive discharge from eyes and salty taste in mouth

amnesia.def: A morbid state caused by lack of focus due to preoccupied mind or derangement of temperament of brain

amnesia caused by cold- ness and moistne.def: A morbid state characterized by remembrance of current events and failure to remember past events, excessive sleep, heaviness of head and excess of secretions from mouth, nose and eyes

amnesia caused by coldness and dryness.def: A morbid state characterized by remembrance of past events and inability to remember current events, decreased sleep and dryness of mouth, nose and eyes

amnesia caused by hot- ness and dryness.def: A morbid state characterized by increased temperature of head, dry nostrils and hallucination of fire and other colourful things Description

complete loss of memory.def: A disease characterized by complete loss of memory due to predominance of coldness and moistness or coldness and dryness in the hind brain; this term does not cover Nisyān (amnesia) mentioned under diseases

mental derangement.def: State of mind in which ability to differentiate and think is disturbed

delirium.def: Abnormality of rational thinking, leading to irrational talk

idiocy/stupidity.def: Weakness of thinking faculty leading to imbalances in routine work and social behaviour

weakness of thinking faculty.def: Behavioural disorder due to weakness of thinking faculty

insanity.def: Disturbed state of mind characterized by false perceptions and hallucinations accompanied by behavioural changes, e.g., extreme mood changes, violent behaviour, etc.

mania.def: A morbid state characterized by frown, hostility, excessive anger and violent behaviour

cynanthropy.def: A type of mania characterized by hostility alternating with friendly behaviour

melancholia standard terminologies on un.def: Derangement of imagination and thinking, leading to fear, sadness,suspicion and false imaginations Description

sanguineous melancholia.def: A type of acute melancholia caused by burnt sanguine, characterized by mental derangement along with laughing and feeling of happiness, tawny skin, engorged vessels, red eyes and large and swift pulse

bilious melancholia.def: A type of acute melancholia caused by burnt yellow bile, characterized by madness, passion, irrational talk, shouting, restlessness, insomnia, excessive anger, increased body temperature and yellowish discoloration of sk

phlegmatic melancholia.def: A type of chronic melancholia caused by burnt phlegm, characterized by laziness, cold and clammy skin along with other features of melancholia

melancholic melancholia.def: A type of chronic melancholia caused by black bile or burnt black bile, characterized by mental derangement along with distress, excessive thinking, fear, panic, crying, bad thoughts and intentional loneliness

melancholia due to involvement of perito.unani: Mālankhūliyā Marāqī / Nafkh Marāqī/ Marāqi- yya/Mālankhūliyā Nāfikh/‘Illat Nāfikha/ Sharsūfiya
melancholia due to involvement of perito.def: A morbid state caused by accumulation of heat-induced vapours arising from intestine or waste products of ingested food in the peritoneum, reaching to the brain and leading to intentional loneliness, palpitation, vertigo

melancholia with intentional loneliness.def: A morbid state characterized by pacing, unwarranted suspicion, yellowish discoloration of skin, dryness of tongue along with emaciation, violent behaviour, and non-healing ulcers of calf muscles Description

lovesickness.def: A morbid state associated with deep emotional affection for a person, change in behaviour, pale skin, silence, sunken eyes, frequent deep sighs, etc.

dizziness on standing.def: A morbid state characterized by darkness in front of eyes while standing from sitting position; this term does not cover Duwār (vertigo) mentioned under diseases

vertigo.def: A morbid state characterized by sensation of spinning of objects around the affected person; this term does not cover Sadr (dizziness on standing) mentioned under diseases

dizziness and vertigo due to predominanc.def: A morbid state characterized by dizziness and vertigo along with lacrimation in the initial phase, red face and eyes, engorgement of vessels with their pulsation, sweet taste, excessive sleep and fatigue

A morbid state characterized by dizziness and vertigo along with burning sensation, yellowish discoloration of skin, bitter taste, thirst, sleeplessness and intensification of symptoms in hot environment

A morbid state characterized by dizziness and vertigo along with excessive sleep, salty or sour taste, lethargy, delayed response, decreased thirst, soft and weak pulse and whitish urine of thick consistency Description
A morbid state characterized by dizziness and vertigo along with deranged thoughts, fear, silence, decreased salivation, dryness of tongue, hard and weak pulse and dark urine

dizziness and vertigo due to gases and v.def: A morbid state characterized by short repeated episodes of dizziness and vertigo along with ringing in ears

vertigo due to associated organs.def: A morbid state caused by the involvement of stomach, liver, uterus or heart and is characterized by dizziness and vertigo along with clinical features of associated diseased organs

excessive lethargy and fatigue.def: A morbid state caused by excessive accumulation of humours, gases and vapours in vessels and muscles, leading to fatigue, excessive yawning and pandiculation, and redness of face and eyes

nightmares with feeling of compression.unani: Kābūs / Al-Khāniq/ Al-Jāthūm wa’l Nīdlān/ Khānūq/ Ḍāghūṭ/Ifiyālṭis
nightmares with feeling of compression.def: Frightening dreams with feeling of tightening of chest; a morbid state in which a person, while sleeping, feels that a heavy object is lying on his or her chest, is making him/her unable to breath, move or speak, and he/

sanguineous nightmare.def: A morbid state caused by vapours arising from sanguine and reaching the brain, characterized by features of predominance of sanguine, e.g., redness of face and eyes, imagination of red things, etc., along with other feat

phlegmatic nightmare.def: A morbid state caused by vapours arising from phlegm and reaching the brain, characterized by features of predominance of phlegm, e.g., excessive salivation, lethargy, imagination of white things, etc., along with featur

melancholic nightmare.def: A morbid state caused by vapours arising from black bile and reaching the brain, characterized by features of predominance of black bile, e.g., excessive thinking, decreased sleep, sunken eyes, etc., along with features
