gastric perforation.def: Perforation of stomach; a morbid state in which a punctured lesion is formed in stomach

hepatic insufficiency.def: A morbid state characterized by decreased appetite, skin discoloration, emaciation and diarrhoea similar in colour to raw-meat washings

morbid temperament of liver.def: A morbid state occurring due to predominance of heat, cold, moistness or dryness in liver

hot morbid temperament of liver.unani: Sū’ Mizāj al-Kabid al- Ḥārr / Sū’-i-Mizāj-i-Jigar Ḥārr
hot morbid temperament of liver.def: A morbid state characterized by intense thirst, bitter taste, roughness of tongue, decreased appetite, swift pulse, yellowish discoloration of body and eyes and increased temperature at site of liver

cold morbid tempera- ment of liver.unani: Sū’ Mizāj al-Kabid al- Bārid / Sū’-i-Mizāj-i-Jigar Bārid
cold morbid tempera- ment of liver.def: A morbid state characterized by diarrhoea, puffiness of face, decreased thirst, whitish skin and lips, slow pulse and white urine of thick consistency

moist morbid tempera- ment of liver.unani: Sū’ Mizāj al-Kabid al- Raṭb / Sū’-i-Mizāj-i-Jigar Raṭb
moist morbid tempera- ment of liver.def: A morbid state characterized by decreased thirst, increased salivation, puffiness of face and eyes, increased sleep, lethargy, disturbed senses and indigestion

dry morbid temperament of liver.unani: Sū’ Mizāj al-Kabid al- Yābis / Sū’ Mizāj-i-Jigar Yābis
dry morbid temperament of liver.def: A morbid state characterized by thirst, weight loss, decreased quantity of stool, firm and incompressible pulse, white urine of thin consistency and decreased quantity of blood

obstructions of liver.def: A morbid state characterized by pallor, weight loss, decreased quantity of blood in body, heaviness without pain in liver and soft and whitish stool Description

collection of gaseous matter in liver.def: A morbid state characterized by feeling of distension below the lower ribs at right side

hepatalgia.def: Pain in liver; a morbid state characterized by mild to severe pain in liver depending on the causative factor, e.g., inflammation of liver, accumulation of gases, hot or cold morbid temperament of liver, etc.

severe hepatalgia.def: Severe pain of liver caused by intake of cold water just after strenuous exercise, empty stomach, or after steam bath

inflammation of liver.def: A morbid state characterized by unpleasant appearance of body and face, heaviness below right lower ribs, inability to sleep on right side

sanguineous inflamma- tion of liver.unani: Waram al-Kabid al- Damawī / Waram-i-Jigar Damawī
sanguineous inflamma- tion of liver.def: A morbid state characterized by high-grade fever, intense thirst, restlessness, burning sensation at site of liver with heaviness and severe pain, redness of face and tongue, loss of appetite, dry cough and hiccup

bilious inflammation of liver.unani: Waram al-Kabid al-Ṣa- frāwī / Waram-i-Jigar Ṣafrāwī
bilious inflammation of liver.def: A morbid state characterized by high-grade fever, excessive thirst, restlessness, severe burning, piercing pain, rapid pulse, yellowish urine and bilious vomiting and loose motions

phlegmatic inflammation of liver standar.unani: Waram al-Kabid al-Bal- ghamī / Waram-i-Jigar Balghamī
phlegmatic inflammation of liver standar.def: A morbid state characterized by inflammation of liver without pain and fever, whitish face and tongue, decreased thirst and white-coloured phlegmatic loose motions Description

melancholic inflamma- tion of liver.unani: Waram al-Kabid al- Sawdāwī / Waram-i-Jigar Sawdāwī
melancholic inflamma- tion of liver.def: A morbid state characterized by hardness of liver, increased thirst and dryness of tongue

liver injury due to trauma.def: A morbid state in which liver becomes swollen and in severe cases there may be hematemesis and diarrhoea with blood

eruptions appearing on surface of liver.def: A morbid state characterized by burning sensation in liver, rigor and chills

decreased size of liver.def: A morbid state characterized by heaviness after intake of food in moderate quantity, weak digestion and gradual weight loss

fluttering sensation in liver.def: A morbid state characterized by sensation of quivering in liver and piercing and cold sweats

liver abscess.def: A morbid state characterized by high-grade fever, pain, burning sensation in liver, intense thirst, redness of face, loss of appetite, restlessness, redness of face

hepatic calculi.def: Calculi formation in liver; a morbid state characterized by vomiting after complete digestion of food, and pain and irritation in the liver

mild oedema with hepatic insufficiency.def: Inability of liver to produce normal humours; a morbid state caused by deranged temperament of liver whose debility is characterized by pallor and oedema of extremities Description

dropsy.def: Collection of cold matter in organs and their cavities; a morbid state in which matter of cold temperament gets accumulated in external and internal organs of body

anasarca.def: Generalized pitting oedema; a morbid state characterized by whitish lustreless face, generalized pitting oedema, sour taste, decreased thirst, wavy pulse and whitish stool and urine

abdominal distension due to accumulation.def: A morbid state characterized by abdominal distension without heaviness, flatulence, excessive belching, bulging of umbilicus, gradual weight loss, and tympanic sound on percussion of abdomen

ascites.def: A morbid state characterized by abdominal distension with heaviness, breathlessness, weight loss, tension of abdominal skin, puffiness of face and eyes and scanty urine

yellowish or blackish discoloration of s.def: A morbid state usually occurring due to abnormal condition of liver, gallbladder or spleen

icterus/ jaundice.def: A morbid state characterized by yellow discoloration of skin and white of eyes

obstructive jaundice jaundice due to inf.def: Yellow discoloration of skin and white of eyes due to blockage of bile ducts A morbid state characterized by yellow discoloration of skin and white of eyes along with severe pain, fever, excessive thirst, restlessness an

jaundice due to poisoning.def: A morbid state characterized by yellow discoloration of skin and white part of eyes along with intense thirst, bad breath, restlessness and burning sensation

jaundice due to crisis of any disease.def: A morbid state characterized by yellow discoloration of skin and white part of eyes at the turning-point of a disease for better or worse, fever, bitter taste, nausea, bilious vomiting, loss of appetite, sleeplessness an

blackish discoloration of the skin.def: A morbid state characterized by gradual blackish discoloration of skin along with distension and heaviness below left lower ribs, blackish face, decreased appetite, slow pulse and weight loss

morbid temperament of spleen.def: A morbid state occurring due to predominance of heat, cold, moistness or dryness in the spleen

hot morbid temperament of spleen.def: A morbid state characterized by intense thirst, burning sensation at site of spleen, yellowish discoloration of face and body, disorderly conduct, unequal pulse, reddish urine and blackish stool

cold morbid tempera- ment of spleen.def: A morbid state characterized by decreased appetite, absence of thirst and excessive salivation

moist morbid tempera- ment of spleen.def: A morbid state characterized by flabby body, excessive salivation, decreased thirst and blackish tongue Description

dry morbid temperament of spleen.def: A morbid state characterized by hardness of spleen, excessive fearfulness, callousness, emaciation and blackish discoloration of skin

inflammation of spleen.def: A morbid state occurring due to putrefaction of sanguine, obstruction within spleen and trauma

sanguineous inflamma- tion of spleen.def: A morbid state characterized by pain, burning sensation, excessive thirst and fever intensifying every fourth day

bilious inflammation of spleen.def: A morbid state characterized by burning sensation at site of spleen, yellowish discoloration of skin, eyes and tongue and fever intensifying every third day

phlegmatic inflammation of spleen.def: A morbid state characterized by enlarged spleen with mild pain, whitish discoloration of face, eyes and tongue, puffy eyes and blackish white stool and urine

hard melancholic inflam- mation of splee.def: A morbid state characterized by enlarged hard spleen, abdominal distension, emaciation, diarrhoea, etc.

splenic abscess accumulation of gases in.def: Formation of pus in spleen; a morbid state characterized by fever with chills and rigor, severe piercing pain in spleen and foul-smelling turbid urine A morbid state characterized by distension below left lower ribs Desc

debility of spleen.def: A morbid state occurring due to weakness of any of the four faculties of spleen, i.e., absorptive, assimilative, digestive or expulsive faculty

obstruction within spleen.def: A morbid state characterized by pain and heaviness in spleen

calculus in spleen.def: A morbid state characterized by piercing pain in spleen

pain of spleen.def: A morbid state caused by gases, inflammation, deranged temperament, etc.

diarrhoea.def: A morbid state characterized by frequent passage of loose watery stools; this term does not cover Ishāl mentioned under regimenal therapy

diarrhoea due to dietary irregularities.def: A morbid state occurring due to intake of any particular food material

epidemic diarrhoea.def: Widespread occurrence of diarrhoea due to exposure to extremely hot or cold climate or to polluted air

diarrhoea due to morbid condition of any.def: A morbid state occurring due to disease of any other body organ

diarrhoea due to involve- ment of brain.def: A morbid state accompanied by catarrh, characterized by frequent frothy loose motions and coughing after prolonged sleep Description

diarrhoea due to involve- ment of stomac.def: A morbid state characterized by loose motions usually occurring during daytime with excretion of undigested food

diarrhoea due to involve- ment of liver.def: A morbid state characterized by frequent painless and foul-smelling loose motions usually occurring at night in large quantity

diarrhoea similar to raw meat washings d.def: Diarrhoea due to involvement of liver in which there is passage of stool similar to raw meat washings

bilious diarrhoea due to involvement of.def: Diarrhoea due to involvement of liver in which there is passage of bilious matter not mixed with stool as a result of accumulation of morbid matter in liver

purulent diarrhoea due to involvement of.def: Diarrhoea due to involvement of liver in which there is passage of pus not mixed with stool as a result of accumulation of morbid matter in liver

diarrhoea similar to curdled milk due to.def: Diarrhoea due to involvement of liver in which there is passage of matter similar to curdled milk

diarrhoea with sediments of burnt blood.def: Diarrhoea due to involvement of liver in which there is passage of sediments of burnt blood accompanied with excessive thirst, decreased appetite and fever

diarrhoea due to bilious fever or hectic.def: A morbid state occurring after bilious fever or hectic fever Description

diarrhoea due to involvement of spleen.def: A morbid state characterized by features of predominance of black bile, diseased state of spleen and diarrhoea mixed with sediments

diarrhoea due to involvement of intestin.def: A morbid state characterized by quick passage of stool mixed with causative matter from intestine

diarrhoea mixed with mucus due to involv.def: A morbid state characterized by passage of stool mixed with mucus, flatulence and borborygmi

diarrhoea due to eruptions of intestine.def: A morbid state characterized by diarrhoea mixed with scales, burning sensation in intestine, excessive thirst, bitter taste, dry tongue and burning sensation during defecation

diarrhoea due to pouring of yellow bile.def: A morbid state characterized by features of predominance of yellow bile, burning and irritation during defecation

diarrhoea due to pouring of black bile o.def: A morbid state characterized by passage of black bile or matter similar to pus having smell of vinegar

diarrhoea leading to emaciation.def: A morbid state characterized by passage of foul-smelling loose curdled stools, gradual decrease of appetite, darkening and dryness of skin, nausea, dysuria, excessive flatus and borborygmi

purulent diarrhoea.def: A morbid state in which stools contain pus due to ruptured abscess of internal organs, e.g., stomach, liver, etc. Description

bloody diarrhoea.def: A morbid state characterized by passage of loose stool mixed with blood

bloody diarrhoea of hepatic origin.def: Bloody diarrhoea due to involvement of liver; a morbid state characterized by passage of loose stool with fresh blood in episodes, dull pain at site of liver, emaciation, etc.

bloody diarrhoea of intestinal origin.def: Bloody diarrhoea due to involvement of intestine; a morbid state characterized by passage of loose stool mixed with small quantity of fresh blood and scales, severe abdominal pain, emaciation at later stage, etc.

infantile diarrhoea.def: A morbid state usually occurring during teething period

abrasion and ulcers of intestine.def: A morbid state characterized by abdominal pain and tenesmus occurring due to abrasion of internal surface of intestine

diarrhoea due to abra- sion of intestine.def: A morbid state characterized by passage of loose stool mixed with scales, excessive thirst, abdominal pain and tenesmus

diarrhoea mixed with blood due to abrasi.def: A morbid state characterized by sudden or gradual passage of loose stool mixed with blood

diarrhoea caused by abrasion of intestin.def: A morbid state caused by thick matter, characterized by passage of loose stool, usually during compound fevers Description

diarrhoea mixed with thin or thick scale.def: A morbid state characterized by passage of loose stool mixed with thin or thick scales

diarrhoea due to abrasion of intestine c.unani: Ishāl Saḥjī Ṣadīdī / Ishāl Saḥjī Middī
diarrhoea due to abrasion of intestine c.def: A morbid state caused by pus coming to intestine from other organs, leading to its abrasion and characterized by passage of loose stool mixed with pus

frothy diarrhoea due to abrasion of inte.def: A morbid state occurring due to epidemic fever, characterized by passage of frothy loose stool

lienteric diarrhoea.def: A morbid state in which food is not retained in the intestine for the required time and slides out quickly

sprue.def: continuous loose stools; a morbid state caused either by weak retentive faculty or strong expulsive faculty of intestine

dysentery.def: Dysentery/tenesmus; abnormal movement of rectum in which there is frequent passage of stools mixed with mucus and blood (sometimes) along with severe pain, tenesmus and intestinal distension

false dysentery.def: False dysentery/false tenesmus; a type of dysentery due to retention of dried faecal matter in small intestine, characterized by continuous urge to defecate, passage of viscous matter and scales, severe pain and intestin

true dysentery.def: True dysentery/true tenesmu; a type of dysentery occurring due to pouring of yellow bile or saline phlegm on rectum, acute inflammation of rectum, exposure of anus to cold or intake of things having cold temperament

bilious dysentery.def: Bilious dysentery/bilious tenesmus; a type of dysentery characterized by excretion of bile, scales and blood with stool, excessive thirst and anal burning

phlegmatic dysentery.def: Phlegmatic dysentery/phlegmatic tenesmus; a type of dysentery characterized by excretion of mucus with phlegm, flatulence, borborygmi and decreased thirst

dysentery due to inflammation.def: Dysentery/tenesmus due to inflammation; a type of dysentery characterized by throbbing pain, feeling of heaviness and distension in rectum, fever and dysuria

traumatic dysentery.def: Traumatic dysentery/traumatic tenesmus; a type of dysentery caused by injury to the rectum as a result of sitting on a hard object for a long time

epidemic dysentery.def: Epidemic dysentery/epidemic tenesmus; a type of dysentery accompanied by palpitation, frequent fainting, excessive thirst, bad breath and itching and burning along with small eruptions all over body

infantile dysentery standard terminologi.def: Infantile dysentery/infantile tenesmus; dysentery occurring in children due to predominance of coldness or yellow bile Description

simple hot intestinal colic.def: A morbid state occurring after intake of spicy food and characterized by gripping abdominal pain, excessive thirst and burning sensation

bilious intestinal colic.def: A morbid state characterized by gripping abdominal pain, burning sensation, heaviness, excessive thirst, restlessness and anal burning

phlegmatic intestinal colic.def: A morbid state characterized by gripping abdominal pain, heaviness, excessive salivation and stool mixed with viscous phlegm

melancholic intestinal colic.def: A morbid state characterized by gripping abdominal pain and heaviness along with features of predominance of black bile

intestinal colic due to flatulence.def: A morbid state characterized by gripping abdominal pain relieved by passing of wind, flatulence, borborygmi and abdominal distension

intestinal colic due to impaction of dri.def: Abdominal gripping pain occurring due to impaction of dried faecal matter

helminthic intestinal colic.def: Abdominal gripping pain due to worm infestation

infantile intestinal colic.def: A morbid state characterized by gripping abdominal pain due to weakness of stomach, abdominal distension and excessive crying Description

flatus and borborygmi.def: A morbid state characterized by abdominal distension and gurgling due to accumulation of gases in abdomen

flatus and borborygmi due to intake of f.def: A morbid state caused by intake of flatulent or bad-quality food, characterized by abdominal distension and gurgling

flatus and borborygmi due to involvement.def: A morbid state caused by involvement of other organs, characterized by abdominal distension and gurgling

flatus and borboryg- mi due to crisis of.def: A morbid state occurring at the critical day of any disease, characterized by abdominal distension and gurgling

large intestinal colic.def: Abdominal pain due to large intestinal obstruction, severe pain of colon due to obstruction or accumulation of thick flatus leading to obstructed defecation

bilious large intestinal colic.def: A morbid state characterized by severe pain of colon with burning sensation, excessive thirst, sleeplessness, bitter taste and bilious vomiting, and loose motions

phlegmatic large intestinal colic melanc.def: A morbid state characterized by severe pain of colon accompanied by constipation, loss of appetite, heaviness, coldness of body part below the umbilicus and phlegmatic vomiting A morbid state characterized by less severe

gaseous large intestinal colic.def: A morbid state characterized by severe shifting and piercing pain of colon accompanied with nausea, tenesmus, constipation, excessive flatus and gurgling and inability to emit wind from anus

colic of large intestine due to faecal i.def: A morbid state characterized by gripping pain in colon accompanied with severe heaviness and excretion of only viscous and thick material during defecation

colic of large intestine due to worm inf.def: A morbid state characterized by pain in colon and nausea, aggravated at empty stomach along with features of worm infestation

colic of large intestine due to inflamma.def: A morbid state characterized by gradual occurrence of pulsating pain with feeling of distension and heaviness

colic of large intestine due to sanguine.def: A morbid state characterized by gradual occurrence of pulsating pain with feeling of distension and heaviness, continuous fever, redness of face, puffiness of eyelids, sweet taste and cold extremities

colic of large intestine due to bilious.def: A morbid state characterized by gradual occurrence of moderate pulsating pain with feeling of distension and heaviness, bilious fever, severe burning sensation and bitter taste

colic of large intestine due to phlegmat.def: A morbid state characterized by gradual occurrence of pulsating pain with feeling of distension and severe heaviness, intermittent fever, lethargy, puffiness of face and phlegmatic vomiting Description

colic of large intestine due to melancho.def: A morbid state preceded by splenic disorder and characterized by gradual occurrence of moderate pulsating pain with feeling of distension and heaviness, quartan fever and sour taste

colic of large intestine due to multiple.def: Colic due to accumulation of thick humours and thick flatus in colon

epidemic colic of large intestine.def: Widespread occurrence of colic at a particular time accompanied with severe pain in colon, excessive thirst, burning sensation, palpitation and bad breath

colic of large intestine as a complicati.def: Colic occurring as a result of complication of diseases of liver, spleen, kidneys and bladder

volvulus.def: Colic of large intestine due to twisting of intestine; a morbid state usually occurring in caecum

colic of large intestine due to herniati.def: Colic due to intestinal obstruction caused by herniation of intestine

severe colicky pain of small intestine s.def: A morbid state characterized by severe pain above the umbilicus, severe constipation, vomit containing faecal matter, bad breath and fetid belching Description

constipation.unani: Qabḍ / I‘tiqāl al-Baṭn/ Ḥuṣr/Iḥtibās al-Baṭn/ Iḥtibās al-Ṭabī‘a/I‘tiqāl al- Ṭabī‘a/Imsāk al-Baṭn
constipation.def: Difficulty in emptying bowels due to decreased functionality of large intestine

intestinal worms.def: Different types of intestinal worm that can cause infestation include hookworms, tapeworms and threadworms

hookworm infestation.def: A morbid state characterized by tenesmus, grinding of teeth during sleep, persistent nausea, excessive salivation and occasional passage of worms from mouth during vomiting

tapeworm infestation.def: A morbid state characterized by excretion of segments from tapeworm in stools

threadworm infestation.def: A morbid state characterized by anal pruritus and excretion of worms in stools

haemorrhoid.def: Piles; a morbid state characterized by appearance of polyp-like growth in region of anus due to accumulation of black bile in vessels

bleeding haemorrhoid.def: Bleeding piles; a type of piles characterized by episodic passage of fresh blood from anus

non-bleeding piles.def: A type of piles characterized by absence of fresh bleeding from anus Description

accumulation of thick gas in the aliment.def: A morbid state characterized by pain, flatulence, borborygmi, constipation or occasional diarrhoea

fistula / fistula in ano.def: Anal fistula; external opening in skin near the anus through which there is continuous oozing of fluid or pus having communication with rectum

perianal sinus.def: Opening in skin near anus with a blind ending that does not communicate with rectum

proctitis.def: Inflammation of anus; a morbid state caused by predominance of sanguine, bilious sanguine or humours of cold temperament and trauma

anal fissure.def: A morbid state usually caused by passage of hard stool during defecation

atony of anus.def: A morbid state characterized by involuntary excretion of stool and gases

rectal prolapse.def: Protrusion of part of rectum from anus

pruritus ani.def: A morbid state caused by threadworm infestation or humours of bilious or salty nature

anal ulcers.def: A morbid state caused by external factors or internal factors, e.g., humour of sharp nature or any irritant

imperforated anus standard terminologies.def: Congenital abnormality in which patient has no anal opening Description

opening of anal vessels diseases of urin.def: A morbid state leading to haemorrhage without any symptoms of piles

renal atrophy.def: Atrophy of kidney, which becomes thin due to loss of its fat content, abnormal hot temperament or abnormal cold temperament

renal insufficiency / renal debility.def: Weakness of kidney due to alteration in its parenchyma or due to abnormal temperament, calculus, inflammation, etc.

renal calculus and sand.def: Stones of kidney are formed when matter is thicker and sand is formed when matter is thinner

flatulence of kidney.def: Flatulence around the kidney; when thick gas is collected around the kidney, tension develops in the kidney and pain develops in the back

renal pain.def: Pain in kidney is a symptom and not a disease, which develops due to many causes, e.g., flatulence, weakness, inflammation, calculus or wound, etc. Description

obstructions in kidney.def: Obstructions in the kidney may be due to viscid thick humour or calculus or inflammation manifest as small amount of urine and discomfort in the back (with fever if due to inflammation)

hot inflammation of kidney.def: Hot/acute inflammation of kidney is caused by thick blood, thin bilious blood, etc., and is manifest as pain and fever

cold phlegmatic inflam- mation of kidney.def: Cold phlegmatic inflammation of kidney is manifest as mild pain in back, whiteness in urine, slow pulse, etc.

hard inflammation of kidney.def: Hard inflammation of kidney is usually melancholic in origin or is a complication of other inflammation

renal abscess.def: Pus collection in the kidney as a consequence and complication of wound formation in it

renal ulcers.def: Ulcers of kidney; wound formation due to loss of continuity of renal parenchyma, usually associated with pus formation in it

renal irritability.def: Small eruptions of kidneys characterized by irritation at the site of kidneys, with severe pain; pus and mucoid casts appear in the urine

hard inflammation of urinary bladder.def: Hard inflammation of the urinary bladder is melancholic in origin or develops as a complication of other inflammation

urinary bladder abscess.def: Pus collection in bladder/ bladder wall

insufficiency of urinary bladder.def: Morbid state characterized by decreased functional ability of urinary bladder, i.e., collection of urine, contraction of bladder muscle due to cold impaired temperament, hard swelling or weakness of muscles of neck of bl

vesicular ulcers.def: Ulcers of urinary bladder; wound formation in urinary bladder is either due to corrosive humour, injury by calculus or inflammation of urinary bladder

vesicular irritability.def: Irritation in urinary bladder is manifest as pain, foul-smelling urine, etc.

clotting of blood in urinary bladder.unani: Injimād al-Dam fi’l Mathāna / Jumūd-i-Dam Dar Mathāna
clotting of blood in urinary bladder.def: Blood-clotting in urinary bladder occurs as complication of injury/trauma of urinary bladder

atonia of urinary bladder.def: Loss of tone of urinary bladder muscle; urine passes on pressure over the bladder but not as a forceful stream Description

displacement of urinary bladder.def: Displacement of urinary bladder occurs due to trauma and is manifest as either incontinence or retention of urine

vesicular pain.def: Pain in urinary bladder is mainly due to six causes, viz., inflammation, wound formation, small eruptions, calculus, abnormal temperament and flatus

flatus in urinary bladder.def: Flatus in urinary bladder occurs due to use of flatus-producing substance or weakness of bladder, etc.

burning micturition.def: Burning micturition or irritation during urination is a symptom of many diseases of urinary tract

gonorrhoea.def: Burning micturition with pus discharge; an inflammation of lower urinary tract, manifest as burning micturition with pus discharge

chronic gonorrhoea.def: Manifest as pus or pus-like discharge for a long time

gonorrhoea in females.def: An inflammation of lower urinary tract as well as genital tract of females, manifest as burning micturition with pus discharge

retention of urine dysuria.def: Inability to pass urine despite collection in urinary bladder, due to bladder outflow obstruction Difficulty or pain during passage of urine

urinary incontinence.def: Passage of urine involuntarily (without will of patient)

nocturnal enuresis/ bed-wetting.def: Involuntary passage of urine during sleep

polyuria.def: Increased quantity of urine may be due to diabetes, etc.

sexual debility.def: Decreased ability to perform sexual act

penile flaccidity / incom- plete erectio.def: Penile flaccidity or incomplete erection is main cause of sexual debility

semenorrhoea.def: Discharge of semen or any constituent of semen without sexual act, commonly on erection

increased nocturnal emission.def: Increased nocturnal emission may be commonly due to thin consistency of semen, hypersensitivity, lack of coitus and hot temperament Description

penile tremors / uterine tremors.def: Tremors of penis and os of uterus

involuntary passage of stool during coit.def: Involuntary loss of stool/ faecal incontinence during sexual act

orchitis.def: Inflammation of testicles; inflammation in the parenchymal tissue of testicles

pain in testicles and penis.def: Pain in testicles and penis develops due to abnormal temperament, collection of flatus or trauma, etc.

fissuring/cracking of prepuce or glans o.def: Fissuring/cracking of foreskin/prepuce and glans of the penis develops due to dryness

itching of penis.def: Itching of penis usually occurs due to flow of irritant matter through it

obstruction of penile urethra chordee st.def: Obstruction in duct of penis may develop due to eruption or sticking of thick material or abnormal growth, etc. Distortion of shape / bending of penis Description

testicular hypertrophy.def: Hypertrophy of testicles; increased size of testicles due to hypertrophy either physiologically at puberty or due to inflammation (painful)

testicular atrophy.def: Decreased size of testicles due to atrophy

varicocoele.def: Presence of tortuous and congested veins in scrotal sac

scrotal hardening.def: Swelling and hardening of scrotal sac occurs due to collection of thick flatus

desire to get anal penetration.def: Desire to get anal penetration / anal sex; a kind of perversion that develops in some elderly persons

dilatation of the penile orifice.def: In this condition the penile orifice gets dilated due to gonorrhoea, etc., causing sexual debility and premature ejaculation

hydrocoele.def: Collection of fluid in scrotal sac

testicular ascending.def: Ascending of testicle often occurs due to cold exposure

penile warts.def: Warts of penis are found like warts of other organs and treatment is same

decreased quantity of semen gynaecology.def: Decreased quantity of semen which may be due to decreased sexual power, semenorrhoea and other causes

morbid temperament of uterus.def: Abnormal temperament of uterus; derangement in temperament of uterus due to predominance of hot, cold, dry and moist state which may be simple or humoral resulting in impaired function and diseases of uterus

hydrometra.unani: Istisqā’ al-Raḥim / Ijtimā‘- i-Āb Dar Raḥim
hydrometra.def: Accumulation of fluid in uterus due to weakness in expulsive faculty, retention of menstrual blood containing yellowish fluid, predominance of phlegm or fluid in blood

cervical stricture.def: Atresia/closure of uterus; a disease in which the cervix is blocked due to acute and chronic inflammation, mucosal plug, fibroid and any abnormal growth, tumour and fibrosis after healing of uterine ulcers

uterine prolapse pruritus uteri standard.unani: Inzilāq al-Raḥim / Nutū’ al-Raḥim Ḥikka al-Raḥim
uterine prolapse pruritus uteri standard.def: Prolapse of uterus with infiltration of phlegmatic humour into ligaments attached to uterus weakening ligaments, after which uterus descends and results in its prolapse through vagina Sensation of itching in uterine regi

cracking of uterus.def: Cracking of uterus and cervix during childbirth, especially in abnormal pregnancy

uterine inversion.def: Inversion of uterus; a condition where uterus turns inside down, fundus of uterus comes out of vagina

metritis.def: Inflammation of uterus, which may be hot or hard in nature, or phlegmatic, etc.

uterine ulcers.def: Ulcers of uterus which may be due to internal or external factors

uterine polyp.def: A growth similar to piles accompanied by dilatation of uterine blood vessels

leucorrhoea.def: Vaginal discharge; yellowish white discharge from the uterus due to uterine diseases like amenorrhoea, metritis, displacement of uterus and sometimes due to anaemia and general debility

fistula in utero.def: An ulcer with constant pain and discharge, usually unresponsive to treatment for at least 40 days, transforming into fistula which can extend to pubic bone and muscles of anal sphincter and urinary bladder

uterine carcinoma.def: An abnormal growth which starts from cervix and spreads quickly inside and outside the uterus, neighbouring lymphatic nodes become enlarged and uterus gets distended Description

physometra.def: Uterine distension caused by altered cold temperament of uterus which weakens uterine faculty due to conversion of its nutrition into air/pneuma

uterine eruptions.def: Small eruptions in internal lining or other part of uterus, especially near cervix, due to involvement of abnormal matter or fluid containing blood reverting back towards uterus during menstruation; may be due to leucorr

uterine abscess.def: Uterine abscess manifest as throbbing pain and fever with chills and rigor, etc.

hysteria.def: A state of mind with unmanageable emotions

abortion.def: Expulsion of fetus before period of viability; this term does not cover Isqāṭ-i-Janīn mentioned under principles of treatment

habitual abortion.def: Three or more consecutive spontaneous abortions

phantom pregnancy/ false pregnancy/ pseu.def: A condition in which a woman may develop all the symptoms of pregnancy accompanied by a growth in uterus but there is actually no pregnancy Description

dystocia.def: Difficult labour may be due to either maternal causes or foetal causes such as large baby, malpresentation, foetal abnormalities, multiple pregnancies, etc.

retained placenta.def: Prolonged retention of dead fetus or retention of placenta in uterus after birth of baby

retention of lochia.def: Retention of blood containing vaginal discharge after childbirth

coagulation of milk.def: Coagulation of milk in mammary glands

mastitis.def: Inflammation of breast due to predominance of humours

breast abscess and ulcers.def: Formed when inflammatory matter becomes infected and collected in breast

enlargement of breast.def: An abnormal condition in which breast becomes large and flaccid

smallness of breast.def: A condition where breasts become smaller in size

amenorrhoea.def: Absence of menstruation which may be either physiological or pathological

polymenorrhoea.def: Increased menstrual bleeding which includes increase in amount of bleeding, prolonged duration of bleeding and increased number of cycles

dysmenorrhoea.def: Painful menstruation, pain being felt before, during or after menses

oligomenorrhoea.def: Scanty menstruation arising due to lack of blood in body, causing weakness and pallor

fever standard terminologies on unani me.def: An abnormal heat (raised body temperature) leading to disturbance in normal functions of body; a morbid condition where an abnormal heat is produced in the heart and spreads to whole body through blood vessels, leading t

ephemeral fever/ short- term fever.def: fever of one day/ short term fever where body temperature rises minimally, commonly due to irregularity in diet and exercise, insomnia, etc.; a morbid condition where body temperature rises minimally for one day or a few

ephemeral fever due to excessive sorrow/.def: A morbid condition where body temperature rises due to excessive sorrow, which causes pneuma to move towards interior of body

ephemeral fever due to excessive dilemma.def: A morbid condition where body temperature rises due to rapid movement of pneuma in and out because of excessive dilemma

ephemeral fever due to excessive fear.def: A morbid condition where body temperature rises due to sudden movement of pneuma towards interior of body because of excessive fear

ephemeral fever due to excessive stress.def: This is a morbid condition where body temperature rises due to sudden and extraordinary movement of pneuma

ephemeral fever due to excessive anger.def: A morbid condition where body temperature rises due to sudden and extraordinary movement of pneuma towards exterior

ephemeral fever due to excessive joy/hap.def: A morbid condition where body temperature rises due to sudden and extraordinary movement of pneuma towards exterior Description

ephemeral fever due to excessive awakeni.def: A morbid condition where body temperature rises due to prolonged movement of pneuma due to awakening

ephemeral fever due to excessive fatigue.def: A morbid condition where body temperature rises due to prolonged movement of pneuma due to excessive physical work

ephemeral fever due to diarrhoea.def: Short-term fever caused by excessive loose stools

ephemeral fever due to excessive pain.def: A morbid condition where body temperature rises due to extraordinary movement of pneuma caused by excessive pain

ephemeral fever due to unconsciousness.def: A morbid condition where body temperature rises due to movement of pneuma to interior caused by unconsciousness

ephemeral fever due to excessive thirst.def: A morbid condition where body temperature rises due to heating of body fluid caused by excessive thirst

ephemeral fever due to excessive hunger.def: A morbid condition where body temperature rises due to heating of body fluid caused by excessive hunger

ephemeral fever due to obstruction in or.unani: Ḥummā al-Yawm Suddiyya Ḥummā al-Yawm Tukhmiyya
ephemeral fever due to obstruction in or.def: Short-term fever caused by obstruction/ partial obstruction at various levels including viscera, blood vessels Description

ephemeral fever due to inflammation.def: A morbid state where fever comes when there is inflammation anywhere in body

ephemeral fever due to sunstroke.def: Short-term fever caused by excessive sun exposure or sunstroke

ephemeral fever due to lack of perspirat.def: Short-term fever caused by closure of skin pores, due to cold exposure, taking bath in astringent water, etc., leading to lack of perspiration

ephemeral fever due to liquor consumptio.def: Short-term fever caused by liquor consumption; this type of fever is caused when very strong liquor is used, producing abnormal hot temperament of liver, heart and other organs

ephemeral fever due to consumption of ho.def: Short-term fever caused by excessive consumption of hot foods

ephemeral fever due to certain drugs.def: Short-term fever caused by consumption of certain drugs having hot temperament

ephemeral fever due to discontinuing bat.def: Short-term fever caused by discontinuing bath/ Turkish bath

catarrhal fever.def: Short-term fever caused by cold and coryza

ephemeral fever due to heatstroke.def: Short-term fever caused by exposure to heat as hot wave, hot bath, etc. Description

ephemeral plethoric fever.def: Short-term fever caused by excess/congestion of humours in blood vessels or excessive volume of blood

ephemeral fever due to excessive evacuat.def: Short-term fever caused by excessive evacuation of humours, as in diarrhoea

cachexic fever.def: One of three types of fever according to classifcation including low-grade emaciating fever, i.e., tubercular fever

tubercular fever.def: Specific type of fever caused in pulmonary tuberculosis, occurring when infection is attached with the organ itself; the fever is chronic, low-grade, rises in evening followed by night sweating, leading to weight loss an

putrefaction/sepsis/ infection intravasc.def: An abnormal change occurring in the humours/ body fluids caused by extrinsic or intrinsic factors; in extrinsic factors there may be morbid bodies, which come from dead bodies and stagnant waters that may be interpreted

fever with shivering.def: Fever associated with rigor and chills

acute fever; hyperpyrexia.def: Fever which occurs and rises suddenly

chronic fever.def: Fever which occurs slowly and persists for a long time

summer fever.def: Fever whose incidence is seen commonly in summer season, possibly caused by extreme exposure to heat

relapsing fever.def: Fever which relapses after a period of time

tertian fever.def: Fever in which the periodicity is every third day

bilious fever.def: Fever which is caused by infection of bilious humour Description

tertian fever/ bilious intermittent feve.def: Bilious intermittent fever caused by infection of bilious humour; in which bout occurs every third day; this fever develops due to infection of bile humour outside vessels

bilious continuous fever.def: Bilious continuous fever caused by infection of bilious humour

high-grade bilious fever.def: High-grade bilious fever which develops due to infection of bilious humour inside vessels; temperature is very high, commonly leading to complications

phlegmatic fever.def: Fever caused by infection of phlegmatic humour

quotidian fever/ phlegmatic intermittent.def: Phlegmatic intermittent fever with daily periodicity which develops due to infection of phlegm outside vessels in stomach, lungs, etc.

phlegmatic continuous fever.def: Phlegmatic continuous fever develops due to infection of phlegm inside vessels; in this condition, low-grade fever persists continuously

quartan fever/ melancholic intermittent.def: Melancholic intermittent fever occurs every fourth day due to infection of black bile outside blood vessels

melancholic continuous fever irregular f.def: Melancholic continuous fever with a peak every fourth day; develops due to infection of black bile inside blood vessels Remittent fever with irregular bouts Description

typhoid fever.def: A type of sanguineous fever in which infection/ sepsis is intravascular and involves mainly intestines

sanguineous fever.def: Sanguineous fever where infection/sepsis is intravascular

sanguineous fever with increased viscosi.def: Sanguineous fever due to excessive hotness/ viscosity of sanguine humour

sanguineous infective fever.def: Sanguineous fever due to infection of sanguine humour

sanguineous infectious increasing fever.def: Sanguineous fever due to infection of sanguine humour, with rising temperature

sanguineous infectious decreasing fever.def: Sanguineous fever due to infection of sanguine humour, with decreasing temperature

sanguineous infectious constant fever.def: Sanguineous fever due to infection of sanguine humour, with constant high temperature

cerebral fever.def: Fever with unconsciousness, caused by a morbid matter that affects the brain and heart, leading to unconsciousness

enteric fever; typhoid fever.def: This type of fever is continuous and is caused by infection in the intestine

epidemic fever.def: Fever which develops due to infected and morbid air, which affects vital organs, body fluids and the pneuma

exanthematous fever.def: This type of fever occurs with skin eruptions, e.g., measles, chicken pox, etc.

scarlet fever.def: Fever caused by a bacterium, usually associated with sore throat and skin eruptions

fever with delerium standard terminologi.def: A high-grade fever associated with patches on skin and altered sensorium, but not affecting any specific organ; characterized by confused thinking, disrupted attention, disordered speech Description

malaria.def: Intermittent fever caused by infection, commonly from stagnant water, characterized by periodicities of 24 hours, 48 hours and 72 hours

backache.def: Pain of the back can be superficial or deep; it can be due to derangement of cold temperament, predominance of phlegm, physical exertion, incorrect sitting posture, increased sexual activity, weakness of kidneys, premens

low backache.def: Pain of the lower portion of back

rheumatism.def: Inflammation and pain of joints with redness of skin which may sometimes involve heart

arthritis/arthralgia.def: Pain, inflammation, and degenerative conditions of joints may occour due to different etiologies

sanguineous arthritis.def: A type of arthritis which involves several joints and occurs due to derangement of sanguine

bilious arthritis.def: A type of arthritis which involves several joints and occurs due to the derangement of yellow bile Description

phlegmatic arthritis.def: A type of arthritis which involves several joints and occurs due to derangement of phlegm

melancholic arthritis.def: A type of arthritis which involves several joints and occurs due to the derangement of black bile

polyarthritis.def: A type arthritis which involves several joints of the body

pain of hip joint.def: Pain and inflammation of hip joint

achillodynia.def: Severe pain in heel caused by the infiltration of acrid humours

gout.def: Pain and inflammation of big toe/heel; a specific type of swelling, inflammation and pain occurring commonly in small joints, especially in great toe; but sometimes it may also affect wrist and fingers of hand

sciatica.def: Pain along sciatic nerve; a type of pain which originates from the back and runs along the sciatic nerve, the intensity of pain increasing with the quantity of matter

ankylosing arthritis.def: Fixation or restricted mobility of joints leading to locking of joints due to degenerative changes in the articular surface

kyphosis.def: A deformity in which there is backward bending of the vertebral column

lordosis standard terminologies on unani.def: Forward bending of vertebral column Description

pott’s disease / koch’s spine.def: Damage and displacement of vertebral column commonly due to tubercular pathology, in which a chronic ulcer damages the vertebral column up to the gangrenous stage, resulting in its displacement

osteoporosis/ weakness of bones.def: Weakening/softening of bones, commonly in females and old age; bones are more prone to fractures in this condition

simple fracture.def: Breakage of bone without much trauma to overlying muscles or skin

compound fracture.def: Breakage of bone with trauma to associated muscles and skin; there may also be laceration of skin by fragments of broken bone

complicated fracture.def: Breakage or discontinuity of bone together with involvement of organs, either artery or vein, either due to trauma from outside or puncture by fragments of broken bones

complete fracture.def: Complete breakage or discontinuity of bone

incomplete fracture.def: Incomplete breakage or discontinuity of bone

green-stick fracture.def: Incomplete type of fracture with discontinuation of bone at one side, remaining intact on the other side; occurs mostly in children due to incomplete ossification of bones; resembles breaking of green branch of tree, whi

comminuted fracture.def: Breaking of a bone into many pieces

avulsion fracture.def: Breaking of bone with injury by some sharp object and exposure of a piece of bone outside

perforating fracture.def: A perforating type of breakage of bone

spiral fracture.def: Breaking of bones in twisted or spiral manner

impacted fracture.def: Breaking of long bones which penetrate into soft tissue

intrauterine fracture.def: Discontinuity of bones of fetus inside uterus due to trauma

congenital fracture.def: Discontinuity of bone due to congenital causes/ mishandling of obstetric procedures

hairline fracture standard terminologies.def: Fine or thin-line breakage of bones Description

pathological fracture.def: Breaking of bone due to some pathology in bone

epiphyseal fracture.def: Breaking of bone along its edges

deep and broad fracture.def: Breaking of bone which is deep and broad

crescent fracture.def: Breaking of bone which is long and broad

fracture of vertebrae of upper part of b.def: Breaking of vertebrae of upper part of back

fracture of vertebrae of lower part of b.def: Breaking of vertebrae of lower part of back

dislocation/luxation.def: Displacement of bone from adjoining bone

traumatic dislocation.def: Dislocation of joints caused by trauma; dislocation caused by two kinds of factor, i.e., exogenous and endogenous; exogenous factors include trauma of joint; endogenous factors include weakness of joints, ligaments, etc.

congenital dislocation standard terminol.def: Dislocation of bone in fetal life; a type of dislocation which is present at birth, caused either by the abnormal position of fetus in uterus or by defect in the process of formation of structure of fetal life; the most

pathological dislocation.def: Dislocation of bone due to any pathology in joint; a type of dislocation occurring due to joint diseases, e.g., inflammation, pus formation or tubercular infection and other nerve disorders

complete dislocation.def: Subluxation of head of bone completely from its normal position

subluxation/partial dislocation.def: Subluxation of head of bone partially from its normal position

compound dislocation.def: Dislocation of bone associated with skin injury

complicated dislocation.def: Dislocation of bone associated with injury of vessels and bones

dislocation with fracture.def: Dislocation of bone associated with its fracture

dislocation of joint.def: Total deviation or displacement of joint

sprain diseases of skin and cosmetology.def: Injury of ligament without displacement or injury to bone

eruptions.def: Appearance of lesions on skin, commonly associated with inflammation Description

small eruptions.def: Small pustules are formed by accumulation of morbid fluids which after being deviated towards skin are collected between muscular layer and skin; this usually occurs in persons with thick skin

eruptions of pinna.def: Eruption on external ear or pinna; small, red- coloured eruptions caused by derangement of blood mixed with fluids

calf eruptions.def: Large black-coloured eruptions on calf muslces due to accumulation of morbid humours, characterized by black discharge

eruptions at nape of neck.def: Abnormal eruptions at nape of neck; multiple, large and painful pustules caused by acrid humour

pustular eruptions of cheeks.def: Abnormal eruptions on face and cheeks in the form of hard pustules with redness around them

persistent eruptions.def: Persistent eruptions on skin and mucous membrane

a type of ichthyosis.def: Hyperpigmented patches on skin, with itching and shedding

pityriasis alba standard terminologies o.def: Hypopigmented patches on skin with normal hair and easily reversible Description

pityriasis nigra.def: Hyperpigmented patches on skin with scaling seen on rubbing

melasma.def: Dark hyperpigmented patches on skin without roughness or itching

freckle.def: Black or reddish spots on skin, especially on face

naevus.def: Black or reddish dot-like spots or patch on skin, especially on face

mole.def: Black or blackish small plain or raised spots on skin

ecchymosis.def: Greenish black patch that develops after bleeding under skin due to trauma, etc.
/ cadaveric lividity. The purplish / bluish patch caused by extravasations of blood in the skin. Its possible English equivalent is ecchymosis.

scar.def: Marks of ulceration/wounds and scar formation after healing

redness of skin of face.def: Redness of skin of face, usually seen in winter, often associated with excoriation

discloration of skin.def: Discoloration of skin according to predominance of humour, e.g., yellowish in bilious predominance

petechial haemorrhage.def: Haemorrhage occurring beneath skin in some fevers Description

dandruff.unani: Ḥazāz / Bafā/ Hubayrā/ Qishr al-Ra’s/ Ḥibriyya
dandruff.def: Shedding of whitish scales from scalp

alopecia areata.def: Patchy loss of hair without scaling, mainly seen in scalp hair, beard, eyebrows, etc.

alopecia with loss of a layer of skin.def: Patchy loss of hair with loss of a layer of skin (disease of snake, resembling moulting of exuviae of snake)

hairfall.def: Hair fall which commonly occurs in scalp, beard, eyebrows, etc.

thinning of scalp skin with hair loss.def: Thinning of scalp skin with thinning and loss of hair (resembling loss of hair as in head of ostrich)

baldness.def: Excessive loss of scalp hair, commonly from front part

oily sticky hair pediculosis standard te.def: Oily sticky hair that sticks to cloths; mostly due to excessive oily nutrition Head lice Description

pediculosis (special type).def: A special kind of louse that remains firmly attached to hair root and can be removed by pouring on hot water

brittle nails.def: Mica-like whitish and brittle nails due to reduced blood and increased dryness

whitening of nails.def: Whitening of nails due to collection of thick morbid fluid

thickened and brittle nails.def: Thickening and drying of nails which are easily broken, mainly due to bilious black bile

splitting of nails.def: Splitting of nails due to excessive dryness

onychogryphosis/ hypertrophy of nails.def: Disfiguring/bending of nails, usually associated with thickening and yellowish discoloration

onycholysis.def: Separation of nails from nail bed due to collection of fluid under nails

elevation of nails from nail bed.def: Elevation of nails from nail bed due to collection of fluid under nails

subungual clotting.def: Clotting of blood under nail bed, usually due to trauma

yellowness of nails.def: Yellowness of nails due to anaemia or increased bile humour in blood

crushing of nails.def: Crushing of nails, usually due to trauma Description

polyhidrosis/ hyperhidrosis.def: Excessive sweating commonly due to plethora, hot diet and other causes, in order to achieve excretion of waste or morbid material

bromhidrosis.def: Foul-smelling sweat occurs due to morbid matter in body or in sweat glands

hematidrosis.def: Blood in sweat, a condition in which either blood comes instead of sweat or blood is mixed with sweat

cracks/ fissures in skin.unani: Shuqūq al-Aṭrāf wa’l Wajh / Shiqāq-i-Jild-o- Aṭrāf-o-Wajh
cracks/ fissures in skin.def: Occurrence of cracks/fissures in skin of extremities and face due to dryness

sanguineous urticaria.def: Red itchy rashes appearing suddenly due to sanguineous predominance

phlegmatic urticaria.def: Red itchy rashes appearing suddenly due to phlegmatic predominance

nocturnal urticaria.def: Fine eruptions with itching which appear at night; initially itchy but become painful

prickly heat.def: Fine-pointed millet-like eruptions which appear in summer and are very itchy and painful, like pricking of thorns

tinea/ringworm standard terminologies on.def: Fine eruptions with itching which appear in form of circle Description

detrimental ringworm.def: A type of fulminant and detrimental ringworm

tinea with scaling.def: A type of ringworm with scale formation

spreading tinea.def: A type of ringworm which spreads from one site to another

static tinea.def: A type of ringworm which is stationary on a specific site

paronychia.def: Inflammation of nail bed which is manifest as throbbing pain, swelling and, sometimes, fever

scabies.def: Small maculopapular eruptions which are initially red with severe itching and burning and involve mostly hands between fingers, legs, groin and, sometimes, whole body

senile pruritus.def: Itching of elderly people due to weakness of skin, indigestion and dryness

folliculitis.def: Ulceration of hair roots, especially in scalp

wet folliculitis.def: Ulceration of hair roots with oozing of yellowish morbid fluid

dry folliculitis.def: Dry ulceration of hair roots with scaling Description

kerion/folliculitis.def: Comparatively deep-rooted ulceration of hair roots with formation of multiple open pores filled with pus

a type of folliculitis.def: Ulceration of hair roots with fine pores filled with serous fluid oozing regularly; due to drying of the serous fluid, hair becomes hard, resembling needles
A type of folliculitis which was commonly found in Balkh (a city of west Asia)
Ulceration of scalp hair roots with intense redness

nodular folliculitis.def: Ulceration of hair roots with nodule formation

bulbar folliculitis.def: Formation of small nipple-like rashes with oozing

wrinkles of forehead.def: Spasm of skin of forehead with itching and redness

chillblain.def: Swelling and itching of fingers due to exposure to cold

foul-smelling scalp.def: Foul smell in skin of head due to contamination with oily substance

frostbite.def: Injury and death of skin and underlying tissue due to extreme cold exposure, commonly in snowfall

warts.def: Round hard eruption on external surface of skin

warts with peduncles.def: An eruption with pointed tip, may produce pain when touched

acne.unani: Buthūr Labaniyya / Ḥabb al-Shabāb/ Buthūr Duhniyya
acne.def: Eruptions due to inflammation of sebaceous glands of skin, usually in young people, commonly found on cheeks and nose, similar to drops of milk filled with oily substance

obesity.def: Excessive amount of body fat or weight gain which may interfere with routine life

emaciation due to dry- ness.def: A type of emaciation due to excessive temperamental dryness before old age

herpes zoster.def: Small papulovesicular eruptions, bilious in nature, associated with severe pain and burning; one or more small red papules/vesicles spread along one line due to preponderance of yellow bile or its abnormality, causing se
Eruptions appear on line of distribution of nerves, commonly on one side of body, which change into transparent papules and cause severe burning and itching

herpes simplex/ fever blister/ cold sore.def: Simple eruptions with mild burning/itching; red papules affecting upper surface of skin due to accumulation of yellow bile, causing burning and itching

herpes labialis / cold sores.def: A morbid condition in which red papules appear on the lips and crusting appears within six to eight days

herpes genitalis.def: A morbid condition in which red papules appear on prepuce internally or externally, leading to severe itching; they form blisters which later change to ulcers

herpes zoster ophthal- micus.def: Painful eruptions on forehead and head accompanied by inflammation of conjunctiva, occurring mostly in patients with joint pain and gout

vesicular herpes standard terminologies.def: Erythema in which vesicles occur; a type of redness with small eruptions on hands and knees that fuse together and are characterized by pus formation, itching, and burning and are devoid of neuralgic pain, usually subsid

miliary herpes.def: A morbid condition in which the skin papules are millet-shaped, white in colour with red roots, and cause burning, pain and swelling at the site

blisters.def: Fluid-filled skin eruptions; a morbid condition in which eruptions appear on skin in form of bullous, containing watery fluid and sometimes blood due to dilution of blood and collection of fluid beneath the skin

blisters in children/ impetigo.def: Blisters which occur in childhood, which rupture quickly; black crust appears on them followed by formation of cicatrix

gangrenous blisters.def: A morbid condition in which blisters are accompanied by infection and decomposition of tissues, leading to weakness and death of patient

superimposed blisters.def: A morbid condition in which a blister appears initially on the upper trunk and then small blisters appear on the initial blister

gas-filled blisters.def: Blisters containing gases in place of fluids

carbuncle.def: A morbid condition characterized by collection of flat red papules, immediately forming crust of brown colour due to preponderance of black bile, leading to severe burning sensation resembling fire burn Description

eczema.def: A morbid condition in which eruptions of the skin are encrusted immediately after appearance along with intense burning and appearance of reddish/greenish lines on skin at site resembling flame of fire

plague.def: Acute inflammation of lymph glands; swelling of lymph glands accompanied by severe pain and burning and blackish, reddish, bluish or greenish discoloration of surrounding skin

cancrum / gangrenous ulcer.def: A gangrenous ulcer of any part of body

lymphadenitis/ buboes.def: Inflammation, swelling and enlargement of lymph glands of axilla and groin due to infections and collection of morbid material from organs

boil/furuncle surgical disorders.def: A type of small abscess mostly occurring in travellers and persons who do more physical and strenuous work; initially red, causing severe pain

wounds.def: Breaches in continuity of muscles and other tissues of body

open wounds.def: Discontinuity of skin and mucous membrane which is exposed outside

incised wounds standard terminologies on.def: Wounds produced by sharp instruments or objects Description

penetrating wounds.def: Wounds caused by sharp objects that involve skin and underlying tissues

lacerated wounds.def: Wounds with irregular and jagged edges, characterized by massive destruction of tissues

contused wounds.def: Wounds caused by contusion of tissue and injured blood vessels (capillaries) leaking into surrounding tissue

poisonous wounds.def: Wounds caused by bite of poisonous animals / insects

suppurated wounds.def: Wounds that are infected and suppurated

non-suppurated wounds.def: Wounds that are neither infected nor suppurated

haemorrhage/ bleeding.def: Escape of blood from blood vessels

septicaemia.def: A systemic disease associated with presence, multiplication and formation of septic emboli in blood

toxaemia.def: A condition which results from spread of bacterial products or toxins in bloodstream

pyaemia.def: Presence of pus in blood with pyaemic abscesses Description

ulcer.def: Breach in continuity of lining of organ caused by injury, abscess or skin eruptions; this term does not cover Qarḥa mentioned under mechanism of disease

malignant ulcer.def: Fulminating malignant ulcer with delayed healing

benign ulcer.def: Benign ulcer or a type of ulcer that is easily healed

indolent ulcer/ callous ulcer.def: A type of ulcer that is difficult to heal, e.g., varicose ulcer

tuberculous ulcer.def: A grave kind of ulcer, possibly due to tuberculosis

spreading ulcer.def: A type of ulcer with tendency to spread

cutaneous ulcer with sloughing.def: Ulcer on skin surface as scar mark with dark sloughing due to accumulation of burnt black bile

decubitus ulcer/ bed sore.def: Ulcer in bedridden patient produced by pressure, injury, anaemia and wet surface of bed

infected ulcer standard terminologies on.def: Ulcer in which infection persists Description

ulcer with viscid fluids.def: Ulcer in which there are abundant viscid fluids

putrified ulcer.def: Ulcer in which there is putrefied material

rodent ulcer / sloughing ulcer.def: Ulcer in which there is sloughing with central necrosis

ulcer associated with falling of surroun.def: Ulcer associated with falling of surrounding hair

oozing ulcer / exudative ulcer.def: Ulcer with continuous discharge from it

hot ulcer / acute ulcer.def: Ulcer that is hot in nature

cold ulcer / chronic ulcer.def: Ulcer that is cold in nature

varicose ulcer / venous ulcer.def: Ulcer on legs because of stasis or stagnation of blood in veins

oriental sore.def: A morbid condition characterized by appearance of small papule on skin with redness and burning, followed by formation of difficult-to-heal ulcer with intense red papule and crusts with continuous discharge

oedema.def: Abnormal accumulation of fluid in tissue (interstitial space) due to predominance of phlegm, characterized by heaviness

dracunculosis.def: A parasitic disease that starts with eruption on skin of leg, hand, extremities or trunk, or more commonly on calf muscles Description

gangrene.def: Death of tissue but not decomposed; initial stage of death of tissue resulting in lack or absence of blood supply of organ due to gradual loss of innate heat, characterized by loss of lustre, sensitivity and blackish app

death of tissue with putrefaction / deco.def: Death of tissue with putrefaction / decomposition; a condition in which innate / natural heat is completely lost, blood supply is blocked and tissue becomes putrefied and black

dry gangrene.def: Gangrene caused by chronic obstruction of arteries; in this condition the decayed organ becomes hard, dry and wrinkled

wet gangrene.def: Gangrene caused by obstruction of veins or occlusion of arteries abruptly

infected gangrene.def: A rapidly spreading gangrene associated with infection in which decaying process is fast; the colour of the affected part changes to yellow, green, black

secondary gangrene.def: Gangrene that occurs as a result of complication of some other disease

embolic gangrene.def: Gangrene due to embolic obstruction of vessels

senile gangrene thrombotic gangrene stan.def: Gangrene that occurs in old age Gangrene due to pressure of thrombus in arteries Description

traumatic gangrene.def: Gangrene due to trauma or injury of a part

cold exposure gangrene.def: Gangrene which develops when a part is exposed to extreme cold for a long time

acutely spreading gangrene / gas gangren.def: Gangrene with acute infection, spreading very rapidly and accompanied by swelling

calf pustules.def: Black-coloured large papules which occur on calf muscles and are suppurated

mulberry nodule/ pustule.def: Ulcerated papule on cheeks and vagina which resembles mulberry and occurs beneath skin

pustules with hard roots.def: Small white-coloured pustules with hard roots; outer ends are filled with pus; much time required for maturation

acrid pustules.def: Pustules that appear at different sites due to accumulation of acrid blood in body

cysts.def: Fluctuant swellings which are more or less circular in shape and are filled with fluid or viscous material

nodule.def: Hard nodules in an organ which do not resolve even after a long time

cervical lymphadenopathy/ diphtheric lym.def: Inflamed lymph nodes commonly found around the neck (resembling neck of swine) Description

inflammation with gaseous distension.def: In this type of inflammation, gas accumulates in spaces of organs

scirrhous.def: A type of chronic inflammation or swelling due to accumulation of black bile, characterized by stony hardness and loss of sensation and pain; begins with small swelling which later grows and sometimes may transform into

tumours.def: Movable swellings of varying size, ranging from gram seed to watermelon

benign tumour.def: A tumour formed by tissues of organs; encapsulated and not malignant

malignant tumour.def: Spreading and recurring tumour that sometimes becomes ulcerated,with emergence of pus and other materials

lipoma.def: A type of tumour with fat content; white, very hard and cannot be compressed

soft tumour.def: Content of this type of tumour resembles honey

a type of benign tumour.def: Content of this type of tumour resembles a semi-solid preparation made of flour and clarified butter

a type of benign tumour standard termino.def: Content of this type of tumour resembles a curry which is white in colour and prepared with milk Description

myxoma.def: A tumour in face, urinary bladder, intestine, nerves and spinal cord, containing phlegm

chondroma.def: A benign cartilaginous tumour on inner and outer side of bones

fibroma.def: Content of this type of tumour is fibrous and hair-like; commonly found in uterus

osteoma.def: Tumour on margins of long bones, causing pain when it compresses nerve

neuroma.def: Tumour on face or neck, formed from nerve fibres along with other tissues

myoma.def: A type of tumour in uterus, prostate gland and gastrointestinal tract

haemangioma.def: Nipple-shaped benign tumour superimposed on skin in which blood vessels are present

cancer.def: Malignant black bilious tumour which can occur anywhere in body; spreads very rapidly and the roots of the swelling are deep; sometimes appears due to accumulation of burnt yellow bile and phlegm Description

An imaginary line situated at 23.5° north from the equator. When the sun reaches this line, it spells the coming of summer while fall of winter towards Capricorn. Its possible English equivalent is cancer.

ulcerated malignant tumour.def: Ulcerated malignant tumour characterized by purulent discharge

elephantiasis / filariasis.def: Collection of fluid in lymphatic channels due to their obstruction, as a result of which legs become swollen; leg and foot of patient resemble those of an elephant

varicose veins.def: Dilatation, elongation and torsion of veins which affects legs of patient, common in persons who stand for a long time

burn.def: Burn injury with dry heat, e.g., flame, heated metal, heated objects

In the context of medical jurisprudence, it is the trauma resulting from exposure of the body to dry heat. In this condition death occurs due to hypovolemia and asphyxia. Its possible English equivalent is burn.

sunburn.def: Burning of skin on exposure to sunlight

burn with semicarpus anacardium.def: Burn injury due to direct contact with secretions of Semecarpus anacardium

abrasion.def: Abrasion of skin which may be caused by friction with hard objects
When skin is rubbed with a hard object, it causes a superficial injury involving the superficial skin. It heals fast and leaves no scar after healing. Its possible English equivalent is abrasion.

shoe bite.def: Abrasion of skin caused by ill-fitting shoes and leather socks

pressure abrasion.def: Abrasion of skin caused by tightening of rope or any ligature

leprosy standard terminologies on unani.def: A chronic infectious disease which causes deformity and loss and ulceration of organs Description

lepromatous leprosy.def: Leprosy where there is no nodular formation but there is loss of sensation of organs

tuberculoid leprosy.def: Leprosy where there is nodular formation with deformity of organs

hernia.def: Protrusion of an organ or part of an organ through an abnormal opening from one space to another space/cavity; can be caused by increased abdominal pressure; this term does not cover Fatq mentioned under mechanism of dis

complete hernia.def: Protrusion of a part of intestine from the inguinal canal into the scrotum

incomplete hernia.def: protrusion of a part of intestine into inguinal canal

reducible hernia.def: Hernia which can be restored to its original position by manipulation

irreducible hernia.def: Hernia which cannot be restored to its original position by manipulation

strangulated hernia.def: Hernia in which herniated part is so tightly constricted in orifice that it disrupts blood supply of the hernial sac, leading to gangrene

inflammed hernia.def: Hernia where the herniated part becomes inflamed

obstructed hernia.def: Hernia where there is obstruction in hernial sac because of extramural cause Description

oblique/ indirect inguinal hernia.def: A type of hernia in which herniated part comes out from deep inguinal ring into inguinal canal

direct inguinal hernia.def: A type of hernia in which herniated part comes out through inguinal triangle (Hasselbach’s triangle)

congenital hydrocele.def: A type of hernia which is present since birth, with herniation into the scrotal sac with all three layers of peritoneum

umbilical hernia.def: Hernia where a part of intestine herniates through umbilicus, occurring mostly in children, old people and young multipara women

epigastric hernia.def: Hernia where the part of stomach herniates through opening in abdominal wall

femoral hernia.def: Herniation of a part of intestine through the femoral canal in thighs

umbilical hernia/ abdominal hernia.def: Displacement of abdominal muscle around umbilicus

poisoning with milk of antique spurge.def: Poisoning with milk of antique spurge (Euphorbia antiquorum)

poisoning with thorn apple.def: Poisoning with thorn apple (Datura stramonium) Description

pharmacology.def: A branch of medicine which deals with study of drugs including their origin, physicochemical properties, pharmacokinetics, toxicological characters and therapeutic uses

sources of drugs.def: Three groups that are used as sources of drugs, i.e., plants, animals and minerals

drug(s).def: Substance or product that produces any new condition in the body; used or intended to be used to modify or explore physiological system or pathological state for benefit of recipient Description

drugs of plant origin.def: Drugs obtained from plants or their products

herb(s).def: Seed-bearing plants which do not have woody stem and die down on ground after flowering

leaf.def: Expanded, blade-like structure of vascular plants, usually green in colour, which acts as basic source for nutrition of plant

flower.def: The reproductive structure of flowering plants

branch of a plant/twig.def: Secondary wood limb growing from trunk of a plant

stem/trunk.def: The part of a plant above ground which bears branches, leaves, flowers and fruits

tender leaves/ flower buds.def: Used for both newly formed flowers and leaves that have not yet unfolded

(1) fruit; (2) seed wood(s) standard ter.def: (1) The seed-bearing structure in flowering plants; (2) the unit of reproduction of a seed plant, capable of developing into another such plant.; this term does not cover Ḥabb mentioned under Unani pharmacy Hard fibrous

root.def: The part of a plant which attaches it to the ground and conveys water and nourishment to rest of plant

(1) bark; (2) rind.def: Hard outer covering of stem, branches and fruit of a tree

stem bark / aerial root.def: (1) Thin outer covering and fibres of plant stem; (2) also used for aerial roots

root peel.def: Hard outer covering of root of a tree

fruit rind / fruit peel.def: Outer protective layer of a fruit

latex.def: A milky fluid which exudes when plant is cut and coagulates on exposure to air; this term does not cover Laban mentioned under functions of organs

gum.def: A viscous secretion of some plants that hardens on drying but is soluble in water

oil.def: A combustible liquid which can not be mixed with water. Its possible English equivalent is oil. A liquid preparation which may be a

drugs of animal origin.def: Drugs of animal origin used for therapeutic purposes

rennet.def: Young goat, camel, sheep, etc., are allowed to suckle for two or three days after birth; they are then slaughtered and the milk coagulated in stomach and intestine is collected and used in management of certain diseases

birds’ eggs.def: Eggs of birds used for therapeutic purposes; eggs of sparrow, duck, hen, peacock, pigeon, etc., are used both as diet and drug

animal gizzard.def: A muscular thick-walled part of an animal, especially bird stomach, for grinding food, used for therapeutic purposes

animal flesh.def: Flesh of animals, including goat, sheep, camel, birds, etc., used for therapeutic purposes

animal droppings/dung.def: Droppings/dung of any animal used for therapeutic purposes

animal fat.def: Fat of animals used for therapeutic purposes; white fat which is not mixed with flesh

fat mixed with flesh.def: Fat of animals conflated with flesh; less white compared to Shaḥm al-Ḥaywān

a kind of sea shell.def: Found in different shapes and colours and applied internally and externally for therapeutic purposes

animal limbs.def: Bones of limbs of animals used for therapeutic purposes

stones.def: Naturally occurring precious and non-precious stones used as drugs

gems.def: Naturally occurring precious and semi-precious stones; this term does not cover Jawhar mentioned under logic and philosophy

earthy materials.def: Naturally occurring earthy materials used as drugs

clay.def: Naturally occurring earthy material, mainly clay, used as drug

pure drug.def: An agent which acts on body by its properties or qualities and is not assimilated to become part of the body and excreted

nutritional drugs.def: Substances used mainly as drugs but also contain some dietary constituents

drug temperament.def: Temperament of a drug obtained after interaction of its components

moderate drug.def: Drug that does not cause any substantial change in body even when used repeatedly or in higher dose

drug of hot temperament.def: Drug that increases heat in body

drug of cold temperament.def: Drug that increases cold in body

drug of moist tempera- ment standard ter.def: Drug that increases moisture in body Description

drug of dry temperament.def: Drug that increases dryness in body

toxic drug / poisonous drug.def: Agent that causes injurious and toxic effects which may lead to death

fatal drug.def: Drug which excessively corrupts temperament of body, leading to death

absolute poison.def: Agent which produces death even when used in small quantity

drug which is metabolized rapidly.def: Drug which is easily disintegrated and absorbed by body in a short duration

drug which is metabolized slowly.def: Drug which disintegrates slowly and takes more time in absorption

solid drug.def: Drug having structural rigidity and resistance to changes of shape or volume

liquid drug.def: Fluid drug that takes the shape of its container

viscous drug.def: Drug that has the property of stickiness, such as honey

brittle drug.def: Solid drug which disintegrates easily if gentle pressure is applied

mucilaginous drug.def: Drug which produces mucilage when soaked in water

dry drug.def: Drug which has undergone drying process

lightweight drug.def: Drug weighing relatively less than another drug of same origin

heavy drug.def: Drug weighing more as compared with another drug of same origin

slow-absorbing drug.def: Drug that has slow absorption rate

fast-acting drug.def: Drug that produces its action in body quickly

assumption by syllogism.def: Assumption about the action of an unknown drug on basis of its similarity in organoleptic properties to some known drug

mental inclination.def: One of the tools of hypothesis in which possible assumption of action of unknown drug is based on spontaneous inclination of a patient to use something in a particular diseases condition

intuition standard terminologies on unan.def: Divine or supernatural disclosure to humans of something relating to efficacy of a drug or management of a disease (one of the tools of hypothesis) Description

taste.def: Sensation of taste of drug perceived by taste buds in mouth

drug odour.def: Odour or scent of a drug perceived by olfactory nerve

drug colour.def: Colour of drug used for purpose of identification Description

grades/degrees of drug potency.def: Grades/degrees of temperament of dugs that indicate magnitude of their action and make a basis for their selection in different acute, subacute or chronic diseases; dose is also determined on basis of degree of temperame

first grade / degree of drug potency.def: First of four grades comprises the action of drugs administered orally which may produce heat or cold but which does not manifest itself until the drug has been taken repeatedly or in large dose

second grade/degree of drug potency.def: In second grade, action is more powerful than that in first but does not produce any apparent harmful effect nor does it affect physiological functions, except when a drug is taken repeatedly or in a large measure

third grade/degree of drug potency.def: In third grade, action of a drug necessarily results in apparent disturbance but does not cause death and destruction

fourth grade/degree of drug potency.def: In fourth grade, action of a drug goes to extent of causing death and destruction; this action of poisonous drugs arises from their quality, whereas the drug which is lethal by its specific form is an absolute poison

idiosyncratic drug standard terminologie.def: Drug which has definite pharmacological actions but mechanism of action is not known Description

drug action/effect.def: Resultant effect of action of a drug on human body; it indicates capacity of a drug for sufficient therapeutic effect; however, it is commonly denoted to indicate action of a drug on normal body
