primary effect.def: Effect of a drug produced while drug has not been altered by body

secondary/ subordinate effect.def: Effect of a drug that is produced after drug is altered by body

direct effect.def: Effect produced by a drug directly on organ or site where it reaches or is applied over; the effect may be immediate or delayed

indirect effect.def: Effect of a drug which is produced when drug is absorbed in body; the effect is mostly mediated through nervous system

harmful effect.def: Undesired harmful effect produced by a drug when it is used to treat a disease

corrective drug.def: A drug or substance which is added with main drug to modify or counter its disagreeable effect

drug substitute(s).def: A drug which can be used in place of another drug of more or less similar efficacy; such substitution is commonly exercised when a drug initially intended to be used in a disease condition is not available

general actions of drug.def: Actions of drug which are produced in body as a whole Description

organ-specific actions of a drug.def: Actions of drug which are produced to affect a specific system or organ of body

calorific drug.def: Drug which produces heat in body

innate heat stimulant.def: Drug which stimulate innate heat and thereby produces tonic effect on entire body

brain stimulant.def: Drug which improves functions of brain by stimulating it

delirium-causing drug.def: Drug associated with causing delirium on oral administration

exhilarant.def: Drug which acts as exhilarant and mood elevator; this term does not cover Mufarriḥ mentioned under Unani pharmacy

circulatory stimulant.def: Blood circulation stimulant. Drug which increases blood circulation through various mechanisms

attenuant.def: Drug which disintegrates morbid material into smaller particles

resolvent standard terminologies on unan.def: Drug which resolves morbid materials Description

swelling-softening drug.def: Drug which reduces swelling and makes it soft

detergent.def: Drug which cleanses surface of organs
An agent which cleans the surface of the skin. Its possible English equivalent is detergent.

roughening agent.def: Drug which causes roughening of skin surface
An agent which causes roughening of the skin surface. Its possible English equivalent is roughening agent.

cleanser.def: Drug that cleanses morbid matters from an organ

deobstruent.def: Drug that removes blockage from a luminal organ of body
An agent which dilates the blood vessels or dissolves thick / viscous matter to remove the obstruction. Its possible English equivalent is deobstruent.

deobstruent of vessels.def: Drug that removes blockage of vessels

deobstruent drug.def: Drug that dissolves thick/viscous matter to remove obstructions from any luminal organ

miotic drug.def: Drug that causes constriction of pupil

laxity-producing drug.def: Drug that produces laxity in organ

expectorant.def: An agent which facilitates expectoration. Its possible English equivalent is expectorant.

concoctive.def: Drug which modifies and prepares morbid humours for evacuation from body
An agent which matures and prepares the morbid humours for evacuation from the body. Its possible English equivalent is concoctive.

concoctive of phlegm.def: Drug which modifies and prepares phlegm for evacuation from body Description
An agent which matures and prepares the Balgham (Phlegm) for evacuation from the body. Its possible English equivalent is concoctive of phlegm.

concoctive of yellow bile.def: Drug which modifies and prepares yellow bile for evacuation from body

concoctive of black bile.def: Drug which modifies and prepares black bile for evacuation from body
An agent which matures and prepares the Sawda’ (Black bile) for evacuation from the body. Its possible English equivalent is concoctive of black bile.

alterative.def: Drug which normalizes body humours in case of any qualitative or quantitative change

blood alterative.def: Drug which normalizes blood/sanguine in case of any qualitative or quantitative change

yellow bile alterative.def: Drug which normalizes yellow bile in case of any qualitative or quantitative change

phlegm alterative.def: Drug which normalizes phlegm in case of any qualitative or quantitative change

black bile alterative.def: Drug which normalizes black bile in case of any qualitative or quantitative change

blood purifier.def: Drug which eliminates toxic, putrefied and waste products from blood through urine, stool and sweat
matters, waste products and putrefied material from the blood through urine, stool and sweat. Its possible English equivalent is blood purifier.

laxative/softener.def: Drug which relieves constipation smoothly; a substance that acts to loosen stool and prevents or treats constipation

mild laxative standard terminologies on.def: Drug of mild grade which relieves constipation smoothly Description

purgative.def: Drug which helps in expulsion of morbid humours in form of loose motions; purgation as a mode of treatment is generally adopted for evacuation of morbid humours of stomach, intestines, liver and joints

strong purgative.def: Drug of high potency that excretes morbid humours of body by inducing watery stool
An agent which causes watery evacuation of the intestinal contents. Its possible English equivalent is strong purgative.
Drugs evacuating the bowel contents rapidly. Its possible English equivalent is strong purgative. Usul-i-Ilaj In the context of this term is used for the elimination of impurities

mild purgative.def: Drug of low potency that excretes morbid humours of body by inducing diarrhoea

mild purgative / tardy purgative.def: Drug which evacuates bowel contents slowly

quick purgative.def: Drug which evacuates bowel contents rapidly

purgative by squeezing.def: Drug that excretes morbid humours by squeezing from neighbouring organs of body and inducing diarrhoea

purgative by lubrication.def: Drug which induces diarrhoea by lubricating tract and facilitating smooth passage of stool; lubricating properties of these agents impede colonic water absorption and permit greater ease of faecal passage

purgative by liquefaction.def: Drug which induces diarrhoea by thinning and liquefying morbid matter

purgative by absorption.def: Drug which excretes morbid humours after drawing them from neighbouring organs of body through purgation Description

purgative by softening.def: Drug which softens stool by impairing water absorption from intestine; in presence of excessive amount of water, stool is softened, breaks into small pieces and passes easily

purgative by dissolution.def: Drug which induces diarrhoea by softening and liquefying hard and solidified morbid matter

purgative by relaxation.def: Drug which causes diarrhoea by inducing relaxation in intestinal wall

purgative of phlegm.def: Drug which causes evacuation of phlegm through purgation

purgative of yellow bile.def: Drug which causes evacuation of yellow bile through purgation

purgative of black bile.def: Drug which causes evacuation of black bile through purgation

saline purgative.def: Saline drug which works by increasing fluid in small intestine

purgative of phlegm and yellow and black.def: Drug which causes evacuation of the three humours, i.e., yellow bile, black bile and phlegm, through purgation

appetizer.def: An agent which promotes the desire for food. Its possible English equivalent is appetizer.

digestive standard terminologies on unan.def: Drug which aids in digestion of food items Description

remover of viscous humour.def: Drug which, because of its tenuity, penetrates the surface of an organ and attached viscous humour and separates the latter from the former

diluent.def: Drug that decreases viscosity of humours and body fluids

carminative.def: Drug which expels gases from gastrointestinal tract
An agent which expels the gases from the gastrointestinal tract. Its possible English equivalent is carminative.

absorbent.def: Drug which absorbs excess fluid from surroundings

irritant drug.def: Drug which causes burning sensation or irritation in organs

excitative drug.def: Drug which causes excitement of organs

rubefacient.def: Drug which produces redness of surface after local application by causing dilatation of capillaries and an increase in local blood circulation
An agent which increases the blood circulation locally and makes the treated part red. Its possible English equivalent is rubefacient.

itch-producing drug.def: Drug which causes irritation/itching sensation on surface of skin

roughness-producing irritant.def: Drug that produces roughness and inflammatory changes at site of contact

ulcerative drug.def: Drug which produces ulceration on surface of skin and mucous membrane

vesicant drug.def: Drug which produces blisters on surface of skin Description

corrosive drug.def: Drug that removes unwanted tissue from wound for healing

depilatory drug.def: Drug which removes hair from body surface

dissipating drug.def: Drug that dissipates delicate and wet parts of organ and burns them

renal lithotriptic drug.def: Drug that breaks down renal calculi

vesical lithotriptic drug.def: Drug that breaks down vesicular calculi

putrefactive drug.def: Agent that putrefies morbid matter to expel it easily from body

suppurative drug.def: Agent which produces or causes production of pus

abscess / swelling rupturing agent.def: Drug which ruptures matured swelling in order to drain out pus

escharotic agent standard terminologies.def: Corrosive/caustic drug or substance which destroys/burns surface of tissue/skin/organ Description

sloughing agent.def: Drug which acts superficially on surface of skin and induces sloughing
An agent which acts superficially on the surface of the skin and induces sloughing. Its possible English equivalent is sloughing agent.

refrigerant.def: Drug which reduces body temperature from normal limits
An agent which reduces the body temperature from normal limits. Its possible English equivalent is refrigerant.

extinguishing drug.def: Drug that lessens heat of humours and checks their impetuosity

tonic.def: Drug which strengthens organs of body for their optimal functions

tonic for vital organs.def: Drug which strengthens vital organs of body for their optimal functions
An agent which provides strength to the vital organs of the body. Its possible English equivalent is tonic for vital organs.

brain tonic.def: Drug used for toning up brain, improving its faculties

nervine tonic.def: Drug used for toning up nerves, improving their functions

eye tonic.def: Drug used for toning up and improving eyesight

tooth- and gum- strengthening drugs.def: Drugs used for toning up gums and teeth, binding gums and strengthening teeth

cardiotonic.def: Drug used for toning up heart, improving its functions

hepatotonic.def: Drug which tones up liver cells and improves liver function
An agent which tones up the liver and improves its function. Its possible English equivalent is hepatotonic.

stomachic.def: Drug used for toning up stomach; strengthens stomach and improves its function Description

renal tonic.def: Drug used for toning up kidney; strengthens kidney and improves its function

vesical tonic.def: Drug used for toning up urinary bladder; strengthens urinary bladder and improves its function

aphrodisiac.def: Drug used for toning up sexual organs; strengthens sexual organs and improves their function

uterine tonic.def: Drug used for toning up uterus; strengthens uterus and improves its function

pleasing agent.def: Agent which increases sense of enjoyment and pleasure of sexual act

childbirth-facilitating agent.def: Agent that helps in quick childbirth, thereby preventing prolonged labour; hastens or facilitates childbirth mainly by stimulating contraction of uterus

hair-grower agent.def: Drug which promotes growth of hair

hair-elongater agent.def: Drug which increases length of hair

repellent agent.def: Drug which diverts matter from one part of body/ organ to another part

thickener/inspissant.def: Agent which increases the viscosity of humours of body to make them thicker than their moderate consistency or previous state

semen inspissant standard terminologies.def: Agent which increases viscosity of semen Description

condensing agent.def: Agent which induces or helps to induce coagulation; drugs with cold and astringent properties have grumous effect on humours

swelling-hardening agent.def: Drug that causes conversion of body swellings into hard masses

anaesthetic agent.def: Agent which causes loss of sensation in organ

soothing agent / calming agent.def: Agent which calms a patient, eases agitation and induces sleep; also used to neutralize the hotness of humours

humours-calming agent.def: Drug that cools down heat of humours, restricts their unnatural agitation and excitement

analgesic.def: An agent which relieves the pain. Its possible English equivalent is analgesic.

brain-calming agent.def: Agent which reduces irritability or excitement of brain

heart-calming agent.def: Drug that lessens abnormally increased force and frequency of heart action

broncho-relaxant.def: Drug that relieves irritation of respiratory function

stomach-calming agent.def: Drug that reduces irritability of stomach and thus diminishes pain, nausea and vomiting Description

antiemetic.def: Drug that is effective against vomiting

antinauseant.def: Drug that prevents or counteracts nausea

humidifying drug.def: Drug that generates wetness due to its excessive moisture

flatulent.def: Agent which causes accumulation of gas in alimentary tract
An agent which causes flatulence. Its possible English equivalent is flatulent.
An agent which produces abnormal gases and secretions leading to flatulence by disturbing the digestion. Its possible English equivalent is flatulent.
An agent that produces flatulence. Its possible English equivalent is flatulent.

vaporific.def: Agent which produces abnormal gases in body

abluent.def: Liquid agent which cleanses treated part due to its detergent property

wound-polluting agent.def: Agent which makes wounds dirty and delays healing

lubricating agent.def: Drug that makes wastes slip out of organ by its lubricating property

emollient.def: Greasing drug that provides lubrication to surface

drying agent.def: Dryness-producing drug that causes dryness by decreasing exudation resulting from constrictions of local blood vessels and thus helps in healing wounds

desiccant.def: Drug that produces dryness through desiccation
An agent which constricts blood vessels and decreases exudation from them and thus helps in healing of wounds. Its possible English equivalent is desiccant.

squeezing drug standard terminologies on.def: Drug that squeezes out all secretions present in organ spaces Description

astringent.def: Drug that causes contraction of body tissues and lumen

constipation-inducing drug.def: Drug which decreases peristaltic movement of intestine or its secretions, leading to constipation

vasoconstrictor.def: Drug that causes contraction of vascular tissues, leading to decreased circulation

myoconstrictor drug.def: Drug that causes contraction of muscular tissue

anastaltic drug.def: Drug that checks any abnormal secretion or excretion from body

haemostyptic drug.def: Drug that stops bleeding by decreasing blood circulation to part or by constricting vessels locally

antiperspirant drug.def: Drug that decreases production of sweat from body

sweat-altering agent.def: Drug which changes quality of sweat when excreted through perspiration Description

glueing drug.def: Mucilaginous drug which produces stickiness on mouth of bleeding vessels after internal administration and thus helps to check internal haemorrhage

vulnerary agent.def: Drug that helps in process of wound healing and scar formation

flesh/tissue growing agent.def: Drug that regenerates flesh for healing wound

cicatrizant.def: Drug that dries up wound and its secretions, eventually forming scar
An agent which dries up the wound and its secretions and thus forms scar. Its possible English equivalent is cicatrizant.

anaphrodisiac.def: Drug that causes loss of libido
An agent which suppresses the libido. Its possible English equivalent is anaphrodisiac.

nutritive.def: An agent which provides nutrition. Its possible English equivalent is nutritive.

antagonist.def: Dug that counters action of another drug
Any drug interfering with the action of the other. Its possible English equivalent is antagonist.

humoural heat increasing drug.def: Drug that increases heat in humours, causing agitation, excitement and expansion in them

haematogenic/ haematopoietic/ blood prod.def: Drug that improves production of blood

spermatogenic.def: Drug that improves production of semen
An agent which improves the production of semen. Its possible English equivalent is spermatogenic.

galactopoietic standard terminologies on.def: Agent that induces milk secretion Description

antigalactic.def: Drug that decreases production of milk

galactogogue.def: Drug that increases production of milk
An agent which enhances the output of milk. Its possible English equivalent is galactogogue.

diuretic.def: Drug that increases excretion of urine
An agent that increases the excretion of urine. Its possible English equivalent is diuretic.

anthelmintic.def: Vermicide, drug that kills intestinal worms

vermifuge.def: An agent which expels out the intestinal worms. Its possible English equivalent is vermifuge.

abortifacient.def: An agent which expels out the fetus by virtue of contracting the uterine muscles. Its possible English equivalent is abortifacient.
An agent which aids the termination of pregnancy. Its possible English equivalent is abortifacient.

hepatoprotective.def: Drug that protects liver tissues from harmful or toxic effects
An agent which protects the liver from harmful or toxic effects. Its possible English equivalent is hepatoprotective. Literally it refers to burning out but

semen-reducing agent.def: Agent which reduces production of seminal fluid

drug causing uterine debility.def: Drug that causes debility of uterus

drug causing diminished vision.def: Drug that causes decrease in vision

drug causing diminished brain function.def: Drug that causes decrease in functions of brain Description

drug causing heart weakness.def: Drug that produces weakness in heart functioning

anticonvulsant/ antispasmodic.def: Drug that decreases the contractibility of muscles by acting through CNS; this term is also used for agents having antispasmodic activity

antiseptic.def: possible English equivalent is antiseptic. An agent that reduces the body temperature during fevers. Its possible English equivalent is antipyretic.

antipyretic.def: Drug that reduces increased body temperature

diaphoretic.def: The drugs which induce perspiration. Its possible English equivalent is diaphoretic.

antiperiodic.def: Drug that prevents recurrence of periodic attacks of fever

intoxicant.def: Drug that induces intoxication or drunkenness

mouth freshener.def: Drug that makes mouth fresher and cleaner

adhesive drug standard terminologies on.def: Drug that helps to stick a material to a surface Description

penetrative.def: Absorption-enhancing drug that makes other drugs reach target organ easily

obstruent.def: Agent which obstructs or close natural openings or passages of body

adjuvant.def: Drug that is given in addition to primary treatment

conception-promoting drug.def: Drug that facilitates and increases probability of conception

semen discharge improver.def: Drug which, on account of its hot quality, stimulates production and reduces viscosity of semen and relaxes passage for its smooth discharge

soporific.def: Drug that induces drowsiness, leading to sleep

thrombogenic.def: Agent that produces coagulation of blood, which may lead to development of thrombus

vehicle.def: Drug enhancing absorption of another drug; a drug or substance which helps main drug in absorption and reaching target; it may be an inert substance or of therapeutic value in potentiating effect of main drug Description

stale unani pharmacy.def: Change in appearance and taste of a drug due to moulds

pharmacy.def: Science of preparing and dispensing drugs

druggist/pharmacist.def: Health professional who practises pharmacy or is associated with production, distribution and monitoring of drugs

general pharmacy.def: Application of major processes of preparing and dispensing drugs on large scale (industrial pharmacy)

dispensing pharmacy.def: Application of minor processes for dispensing individual dosage of drugs

binders.def: Binding materials added to powdered drugs for making pills/tablets

dough.def: Dough form of drug prepared by kneading after adding water or any other suitable liquid medium to powdered ingredients

bolus of fresh drugs.def: Fresh plant drugs crushed and mixed to form a bolus and used to cover another drug that is intended to be processed; if fresh drug is not available then bolus is formed with dry drugs by adding water

pill standard terminologies on unani med.def: Solid, small, rounded medicinal preparation for oral administration; this term does not cover Ḥabb mentioned under Unani pharmacology Description

tablet.def: A solid, flat and circular medicinal preparation of varying size and weight
A solid, flat and circular medicinal preparation of varying size and weight for oral administration. Its possible English equivalent is tablet. A drug of particular shape prepared with

suppository.def: Solid conical medicinal preparation made from thick paste of finely powdered drugs with suitable liquid media, used locally as such or after grinding; used as suppository in vaginal ailments and fine powder is used in ey
A preparation made from a thick paste of finely powdered drugs and particularly used in eye diseases. Its possible English equivalent is suppository.

tampon.def: Piece of cloth soaked or dipped in a mixture of certain drugs for vaginal or rectal use

pessary.def: Solid medicinal preparation in roughly conical or cylindrical shape, designed to be inserted into vagina
A piece of cloth soaked or dipped in a mix of certain drugs, honey, oil or fat for vaginal or rectal use. Its possible English equivalent is pessary.

medicated wick.def: Piece of cotton soaked or dipped in a liquid mixture of certain drugs for application in vagina, rectum or other body orifices

powder.def: Form of dried fine particles obtained by grinding a drug or combination of drugs
Powder of drugs obtained by grinding one or more drugs. Its possible English equivalent is powder.

tooth powder.def: Finely ground preparation of drugs used for cleaning teeth and treating certain diseases of teeth and gums

dusting powder.def: Fine powder of drugs used for dusting on wounds, ulcers or skin eruptions Description
A fine powder of drugs that is used for dusting wounds, ulcers or skin eruptions. Its possible English equivalent is dusting powder.

insufflation.def: Finely powdered drug that is blown with help of a tube into nose, throat or any other opening of body
Finely powdered drug that is blown with the help of a tube in nose, throat or any other opening of the body. Its possible English equivalent is insufflation.

chewable drug.def: A (crude or finished) chewable drug to be kept in mouth and masticated for local effects

snuff.def: Finely powdered drug that is inhaled to induce sneezing
A finely powdered drug that is inhaled to induce sneezing. Its possible English equivalent is snuff.

eye-dusting powder / eye coolant.def: (1) A microfine powder of drugs with cooling effect meant for dusting in eyes; (2) cold drugs put in a Potlī (small bag) and placed on eyes to cool them

drops.def: Liquid drug taken in doses measured by drops, natural orifices being common route of administration

eye drops.def: Liquid drug taken in doses measured by drops and used in different eye ailments

ear drops.def: Liquid drug taken in doses measured by drops and used in different ear ailments

nasal drops.def: Liquid drug taken in doses measured by drops and used in different nasal ailments
Liquid preparation used as nasal drops

throat drops collyrium standard terminol.def: Medicated liquid preparation instilled in throat drop-by-drop Microfine powder of drugs including antimony used as ophthalmic medicine/eyeliner Description

topical ophthalmic medicament / eyeliner.def: Semi-solid dosage form prepared by mixing smoke collected from burning of drugs and some oily substance, used both as eyeliner and topical ophthalmic medicament

aromatic powder / perfumed powder.def: Aromatic powder, prepared from specific aromatic herbs for local application

face powder.def: Fine powder of drugs that is applied on face to improve complexion
A fine powder of drugs that is applied on the face and body to improve complexion. Its possible English equivalent is face powder.

depilatory.def: Medicine used to remove unwanted hair

fomentation.def: Powder of drugs tied in a piece of cloth (bag) and used for local fomentation after heating/cooling it

fruit preserve.def: Fruit preserve prepared from boiled and pierced fruits mixed in sugar syrup of certain consistency; fruits of medicinal importance, such as gooseberry, are preserved for future use

dry extract.def: Solid preparation obtained by drying juice of fresh plants or fruits mixed with a base of sugar and dried by artificial heat or in sun
A semisolid preparation obtained by squeezing the juice of fresh plants or fruits and mixed with a base of sugar later on dried on heat or in sun. Its possible English equivalent is dry extract.

electuary.def: Semi-solid dosage form prepared by mixing powdered drug in base of sugar syrup or honey

antidote.def: Electuary which eliminates toxicity and preserves the life

opium electuary.def: Semi-solid medicinal preparation with opium as its main ingredient Description

electuary based on three medicinal fruit.def: Semi-solid medicinal preparation containing Phyllanthus emblica, Terminalia chebula and Terminalia bellirica as main ingredients along with other drugs

saffron-based electuary.def: Semi-solid medicinal preparation containing saffron as chief ingredient

gooseberry-based electuary.def: Electuary which contains gooseberry as main ingredient

marking-nut electuary.def: Semi-solid compound medicinal preparation containing Semecarpus anacardium as its main ingredient

kernel-based electuary.def: Electuary which contains powder of seed kernels as main ingredients along with other medicinal agents

blended whitish and frothy semi-solid pr.def: Type of Ma‘jūn obtained by mixing decoction of drugs in a base of purified honey, sugar or jaggery; mixture is continuously stirred while still hot, until it becomes thick and white; ingredients of non-plant drugs and so

flower conserve standard terminologies o.def: Semi-solid medicinal preparation of flower petals; a form of semi-solid drug in which rose or other flower petals are preserved in a thick sugar-based syrup Description

rose-petal conserve.def: Semi-solid medicinal preparation in which rose petals are preserved in a thick honey-based syrup

musk-based electuary.def: A type of semi-solid compound medicinal preparation with musk and precious stones as its main ingredients

exhilarant electuary.def: A type of semi-solid compound medicinal preparation having aromatic drugs as main ingredients, producing exhilarant effect; this term does not cover Mufarriḥ mentioned under Unani pharmacology

digestive electuary.def: Semi-solid compound medicinal preparation for diseases of digestive system

linctus.def: Electuary of low viscosity prepared to relieve cough, sore throat and other respiratory problems

vision improver.def: Drug that is used locally in form of microfine powder in management of eye problems, mainly to improve eyesight

ointment.def: Semi-solid medicine prepared by mixing fine powder of drugs with wax or other suitable base, meant for local application
A semi solid preparation which is prepared by mixing the drugs in wax or fat. It is used in skin diseases and other ulcerative diseases. Its possible English equivalent is ointment.

poultice.def: Semi-solid preparation of crude drugs meant for local application Description

liniment.def: A kind of medicated oil or a thin medicinal preparation applied locally
A kind of medicated oil or a thin medicinal preparation used externally for application. Its possible English equivalent is liniment.

a kind of ointment.def: Ointment made of oils and wax

ruby-based electuary.def: A type of semi-solid medicinal preparation with mineral drugs, ruby being the main ingredient

a type of fermented condiment.def: A liquid preparation in which salt, vinegar, ginger, black pepper and water are mixed and kept for a time until it turns sour; the strained liquid is then obtained and used

honey water.def: Liquid preparation of water and purified honey in a specific ratio; the mixture is boiled for some time and filtered

medicated honey water.def: Certain drugs are included in honey water

barley water.def: Liquid preparation obtained by boiling dehusked barley in water; the water is then drained and used

water of roasted barley.def: Liquid preparation obtained by boiling roasted and dehusked barley in water

water mixture of barley and meat.def: Liquid preparation obtained by boiling dehusked barley in liquid meat extract

distillate of meat standard terminologie.def: Extract prepared by distillation of certain drugs mixed with meat extract Description

vegetable juice.def: Juice of green vegetables or green herbs such as mako (Solanum nigrum) and kāsnī (Cichorium intybus), obtained by crushing and expressing them

fruit juice.def: Juice of fruits obtained after crushing and expressing them after removing the fruit coat
equivalent is vegetable juice. Juice of fruits obtained from crushing them after removing the fruit coat. Its possible English equivalent is fruit juice.

curd water.def: Multiple lines are drawn into curdled milk so that water retained in it is liberated; this water is collected for use in certain diseases

water repeatedly treated with iron.def: Water treated with red-hot iron until it becomes thick and black

composition reverting calx to its origin.def: Composition of selected drugs which, when applied to metal calx in a specific manner, the calx reverts to its original form

preparation of rose water and sugar.def: Liquid preparation for oral administration consisting of rose water and honey/sugar, along with other suitable drugs

emulsion.def: Liquid preparation obtained by mixing two immiscible substances with help of an emulsifier; minute droplets of one liquid are dispersed in another Description

milky emulsified product.def: (1) Liquid preparation obtained by grinding kernel of dry fruits/seeds with water or suitable liquid, making what looks like a milky product; (2) white liquid mixture in which oily substances are suspended in water base;

mixture.def: Preparation obtained by mixing two or more drugs in any form: liquid/solid/semi-solid / semi-liquid; however it is often used for a liquid mixture which contains at least one liquid ingredient
A preparation obtained by the mixing of two or more drugs in any form; liquid/solid/semisolid/semi liquid. Its possible English equivalent is mixture.

liquid product prepared by fermentation.def: Liquid preparation obtained after fermentation of drugs in a liquid by adopting a particular method

juice.def: Juice of pulpy fruits and green vegetables obtained by expression

essence.def: Purest form of volatile contents of aromatic drugs; this term does not cover Rūḥ mentioned under factors of existence

vinegar.def: Fermented liquid preparation obtained by keeping juice of carbohydrate-rich substances like sugarcane for a few days

wine standard terminologies on unani med.def: Alchohol made from fermented grapes or other carbohydrate-containing substances by distillation Description

medicinal syrup.def: Liquid preparation obtained by mixing fresh fruit juice or decoction of drugs with a base of honey or sugar

tincture.def: Liquid extract obtained by soaking a drug in liquor; the liquid collected after filtration is tincture
Its possible English equivalent is tincture. Skin dye

dye.def: Dye used to colour skin or conceal discolouration, e.g., cosmetic camouflaging in vitiligo

distillate.def: Liquid product condensed from vapour during distillation; this term does not cover ‘Araq mentioned under functions of organs
Liquid obtained by subjecting a plant drug to process of distillation using suitable apparatus
An aqueous extract of drugs. Its possible English equivalent is distillate.

solution.def: Liquid obtained after mixing some drugs which have ability to become dissolved in water or other suitable liquid

mucilage.def: Mucilaginous liquid obtained by soaking mucilage-secreting drugs in water
Mucilage which is obtained after soaking some mucilaginous drugs in water. Its possible English equivalent is mucilage.

semi-liquid preparation with high nutrie.def: Special semi-liquid dietary preparation with medicinal value, generally made with kernels of seeds, dry fruits and milk

type of non-intoxicating fermented drink.def: Kind of non-intoxicating drink prepared by fermenting grapes, dates or other fruits

infusion.def: Liquid preparation made by soaking crushed/ whole drug overnight in water or other suitable liquid and used after straining Description
possible English equivalent is infusion. A liquid preparation obtained by grinding dry fruits/ seeds/ kernels with water or suitable liquid and strained.

decoction.def: Liquid dosage form prepared by boiling one or more plant drugs

The preparation in which the crushed drugs are soaked overnight in water or suitable liquid. In the morning it is boiled, strained and the strained liquid is used. Its possible English equivalent is decoction.

extract.def: (1) Liquid obtained by grinding green herbs with water and straining; (2) collection of crude mixture extracted from different parts of plants

decanted liquid.def: Unstrained liquid obtained by soaking drugs overnight in water or suitable liquid

hair dye.def: Liquid/semi-liquid preparation used as hair dye
A liquid/ semi liquid preparation used for dyeing the hair. Its possible English equivalent is hair dye. It is a method of evacuation of waste

epithem.def: Medicated preparation for external application, such as poultice; this term does not cover ointment and plaster

adhesive medicine.def: Adhesive drug which is spread over a piece of cloth or paper and pasted at affected part of body

irrigation.def: Medicated liquid preparation poured slowly over an affected part from a distance
is solution. The pouring of medicated water slowly over an affected part from a distance. Its possible English equivalent is irrigation.

douche.def: Medicinal liquid preparation poured on affected part with force
The pouring of a liquid preparation over the affected part from a suitable distance. Its possible English equivalent is douche.

sitz bath.def: Method of treatment in which patient sits in warm water or medicated liquid obtained by boiling drugs in water

footbath standard terminologies on unani.def: Lukewarm decoction of drugs used for immersion/irrigation of affected foot Description

mouthwash.def: Medicated liquid preparation which may be a decoction, infusion or solution, used for rinsing mouth

gargle.def: Liquid preparation in form of decoction, infusion or solution, used for gargling
mouth wash. A liquid preparation which may be a decoction, an infusion or a solution etc., used for gargles. Its possible English equivalent is gargle.

inhalation.def: Inhalation of drugs which may be in dry or liquid form so that volatile substances reach nasal cavity and respiratory tubes
The inhalation of the vapours from drugs kept in a wide mouthed bottle. Its possible English equivalent is inhalation.
The term refers to inhale something / vapours through air passage. Its possible English equivalent is inhalation.

inhalation of vapour arising from fragra.def: Inhalation of fragrance of drugs kept in a wide-mouthed bottle

washing agent.def: Liquid preparation used for cleaning affected body part/organ

oil-based liniment.def: Oil or oily preparation applied over skin as a thin layer

embrocation/anointing.def: Act of moistening and rubbing a part of body with a liniment or lotion

oily-liquid preparation.def: Oil-based liquid preparation rubbed with gentle movements on the body

semi-solid oily preparation for local ap.def: Semi-solid preparation obtained by kneading powdered drugs with perfumed oil or making a paste of oily drugs, this is applied locally on skin Description

massage oil.def: Liquid/oily preparation applied on the body and massaged vigorously

liquid used to splash on affected part.def: Medicated liquid preparation which is splashed on affected part

liquid used for syringing.def: Liquid preparation used for syringing urethra, vagina, nose, ear, or any other sinus

incense.def: Aromatic smoke; any substance that is burnt for fragrance
Aromatic preparation in form of pill or stick, which produces fragrance on burning

fumigation.def: Inhalation or exposing the affected body part to fumes produced by burning drugs; also used to repel or kill insects

disc-shaped preparation for local applic.def: Dry or wet drugs are converted into a mass which is used to prepare a disc dosage form; freshly prepared semi-solid disc is applied on affected part of body and covered with leaf of a suitable plant

vapour bath.def: Exposure of a part of body or whole body to vapours obtained from decoction of drugs or boiled simple water

oiling.def: (1) Lubricating a drug in moderately hot oil; (2) application of hot or cold oil on body part

powder drug meant for sprinkling.def: Granular powder of a drug alone or mixed with sugar, used to sprinkle over decoction or infusion

(1) sugar base; (2) consistency.def: (1) Sweetened solution of particular consistency that is generally made by adding water, distillate or fruit juice to purified honey, sugar or jaggery and boiled until it attains required consistency; (2) consistency of

base of high concentration.def: Sugar/honey base of relatively high concentration (> 70%)

base of low concentration.def: Sugar/honey base of relatively low concentration (65% to 70%)

thick base.def: Base of sugar/honey in which gum acacia is added to increase consistency

crystallization.def: Artificial or natural process by which crystalline solids are formed
Process of formation of crystals from liquid solution

The process of converting some drugs into crystal form. Some drugs are collected in the form of crystals on drying, such as sulphur, whereas some drugs crystallize on precipitation. Its possible English equivalent is cry

decrystallization of a supersaturated su.def: Process of losing crystalline structure formed in a syrup or sugar base

distillation apparatus.def: Apparatus used for purpose of distillation

classical distillation apparatus with sp.def: Apparatus meant for distillation comprising four parts: (i) cauldron; (ii) a specially designed dome-shaped cover for collecting vapour and sending it to (iii) tube, then to (iv) receiver, where condensation occurs Descr

apparatus used for distillation through.def: Modified form of Nal Bhapkā; in this method of distillation, waterbath type setup is used for heating boiler container so that limited heat may be available to drug material in boiler; distillation process remains same a

apparatus used for distillation.def: Apparatus of distillation in which receiver is placed in middle of boiling pot on a support; very little distillate is collected in this way

apparatus used for purification of drugs.def: Consists of a pot half-filled with specified liquid such as milk, with a horizontal rod placed on rim from which bundle of material to be treated is immersed in such a way that it does not touch bottom and is heated

classical distillation apparatus.def: Apparatus meant for distillation comprising three parts: (i) cauldron; (ii) specially designed cover for collecting vapour and condensing it before it is collected in (iii) receiver

classical distillation apparatus with sp.def: Apparatus meant for distillation in which vapour is condensed while passing through a spiral tube before being collected in a receiver

receiver of distillate.def: One of three main components of a distillation apparatus which is used to collect distillate; this term does not cover Qābila, used for obstetrician and midwife

furnace, stove.def: Both stove and furnace are used in pharmacy to prepare drugs

partially crushed.def: Hard and fibrous plant drugs are crushed into small pieces commonly with metallic mortar and pestle; partially crushed drug material is used to prepare decoction and infusion so that maximum soluble constituents may beco

polishing.def: Polishing of tablet, pills, etc., to make them smooth and shiny

deseeded drug.def: Drug from which seeds have been removed

decaped.def: Flower from which sepal has been removed

sieved drug material.def: Drug which has been strained through a sieve

sifting.def: Process of sifting powder of drugs through fine cloth or mesh of different size to obtain powder of required particle size

peel off.def: Removing outer covering of fruits, vegetables, fish, etc.

skinned, peeled.def: Drug from which outer covering is removed Description

syringe/injector.def: Tube with a nozzle and piston or bulb for sucking in and ejecting liquid in a thin stream, used for cleaning wounds or body cavities, or fitted with a hollow needle for injecting or withdrawing fluids

(1) liquid form of drug (2) solution of.def: (1) Liquid dosage form such as syrup, decoction, liquid extract, etc.; (2) solution of some drugs, mainly those of mineral origin such as gold, iron and silver,etc., which are prepared by special techniques

adulterated drug.def: Drug rendered poor in quality by addition of another drug of low quality or of an entirely different substance

drug mixed with honey.def: Drug preparation made by mixing honey with another ingredient

amalgamation of mercury with other drugs.def: Mixing of mercury with another mineral drug to stabilize it and prepare a suitable dosage form

composition of silver and mercury.def: When silver is mixed with mercury and ground thoroughly a soft and semi-solid compound, malghama, is formed

fine powder of mercury and sulphur mixed.def: When purified mercury and sulphur are mixed and ground together a fine black powder is produced which is used to prepare certain drugs

routes of drug administration standard t.def: Routes of administration of different dosage forms Description

respiratory route.def: Administration of drugs through respiratory tract, usually by inhalation

ophthalmic route.def: equivalent is respiratory route. Administration of drugs through the eye. Its possible English equivalent is ophthalmic route.

auricular/otic route.def: Administration of drugs through ear opening

urethral route.def: The administration of drugs through the urethra. Its possible English equivalent is urethral route.

enema / colon cleansing / colonic evacua.def: Administration of warm water or liquified drugs or medicinal preparation through anal canal in the bowel; administration of liquid drugs through anal canal used to eliminate or getting rid of superfluities, vitiated humo

purgative enema.def: A type of enema used for purgation
An enema used for purpose of purgation. Its possible English equivalent is purgative enema.

strong purgative enema.def: A type of enema having strong purgative effect
An enema used for purpose of strong purgation. Its possible English equivalent is strong purgative enema.

laxative enema.def: A type of enema having laxative effect
An enema used for purpose of laxation. Its possible English equivalent is laxative enema.

moderate enema.def: A type of enema having moderate purgative effect
An enema used for purpose of moderate purgation. Its possible English equivalent is moderate enema.

constipating enema.def: A type of enema used to induce constipation

resolvent enema.def: A type of enema having resolving effect Description
An enema used for purpose of resolving inflammation. Its possible English equivalent is resolvent enema.

carminative enema.def: A type of enema having carminative effect
An enema used as a carminative. Its possible English equivalent is carminative enema.

nutritive enema.def: A type of enema having rich nutritive value

alterative enema.def: A type of enema used for normalizing temperamental deviation

emollient enema.def: A type of enema having emollient effect
An enema used predominantly as an emollient. Its possible English equivalent is emollient enema.

anaesthetic enema.def: A type of enema having anaesthetic effect

analgesic enema.def: A type of enema having analgesic effect

antiseptic enema.def: A type of enema having antiseptic effect
An enema used predominantly as an antiseptic. Its possible English equivalent is antiseptic enema.

antispasmodic enema.def: A type of enema having antispasmodic effect
An enema used predominantly for purpose of relieving spasmodic conditions. Its possible English equivalent is antispasmodic enema.

vermicidal enema.def: A type of enema having vermicidal effect

charring.def: Process by which drugs are burnt to charring stage but not reduced to ash

possible English equivalent is charring. The process of obtaining a Kushta (calyx) through a specific procedure. Its possible English equivalent is calcination. An apparatus used for making a fine paste

melting standard terminologies on unani.def: Process of melting of drugs by heating Description

defrothing.def: Process of cleaning froth collected on surface of a drug by skimming; when drugs such as honey, sugar, vegetable juice, etc., are boiled, froth is produced which contains impurities; the liquid is allowed to cool a littl

decoloration.def: Removing colour of certain drugs for purification using various physical and chemical techniques

immersion of hot items in a liquid.def: Process in which red-hot metals are dipped in cold water/juice of drugs; this term does not cover Iṭfā’ mentioned under principles of treatment

process of obtaining salts.def: Process of separating salt contained in crude drugs; either powder of drug or its ash is dissolved in water to obtain a solution; the supernatant liquid is then drained into a vessel and boiled; on boiling, water evapora

evaporation.def: Process in which liquid drug/juices of drug are boiled and their moisture is evaporated

fermentation.def: Process of converting sugar content into liquor or organic acids Description
equivalent is calyx. The chemical conversion of sugars into ethanol. Its possible English equivalent is fermentation. The basic solution of particular

drying.def: Process of removing moisture content of drugs, especially plant drugs by different means for the purpose of their storage or further processing to convert into suitable dosage form; this term does not cover Tajfīf mentio

(1) granulation; (2) pill formation.def: (1) Process in which powdered particles of a drug are made to adhere to form larger, multiparticle entities called granules; (2) process of preparation of pills

coating of gelatin.def: Pills and tablets intended for action in stomach are coated with gelatin

coating of keratin.def: Keratin is used as a coating for pills which are intended to pass through stomach and act in small intestine

dissolution torrefaction standard termin.def: (1) Process of obtaining finest powder of drugs after mixing in water and removing heavy particles and decanting; (2) process of dissolving soluble drugs in a liquid to obtain a solution; this term does not cover Taḥlīl

roasted/parched drug.def: Drug that has undergone process of torrefaction

process to make drug usable.def: Implementation of certain processes such as collecting, cleaning, purifying and washing in order to make drug/inert substance usable, to enhance its efficacy and to reduce its toxicity; drugs possessing toxic or undesire

treated drug.def: Drug subjected to certain treatments for purpose of cleaning, purification or detoxification in order to improve efficacy and reduce toxicity

sedimentation.def: Physical process in which heavy particles of a drug in a liquid base are made to settle by keeping them in the liquid for a stipulated time

filtration by dribbling.def: Process of cleaning a liquid drug/juice by sieving it through a thick cloth, cotton or piece of filter paper

filtration by defrothing.def: Process of cleaning extract of green leafy drug by boiling it and removing supernatant froth from liquid by a sieve or filter

defrothed liquid.def: Cleaned extract of green leafy drug obtained by filtration and defrothing

juice of leaves of chicory and black nig.def: Cleaned juice of leaves of two plants, viz., Cichorium intybus and Solanum nigrum obtained through Tarwīq (filtration by defrothing) Description

sprinkled.def: Decoction or infusion which has been sprinkled over with certain powdery substance before use

despumated.def: Honey and other drugs are boiled for a while so that froth containing impurities is taken from the surface and purified matter is collected

roasting in hot ash / low heat.def: Drugs with juice, like fresh fruit/vegetables, etc., are covered with a cloth and wrapped in clay and kept in an oven or buried in hot ash for some time and their juice is collected thereafter by expression which is used

roasted drug.def: Drug roasted in hot ash / low heat (Tashwiya)

sublimation.def: Process of obtaining purified form of mineral drugs through evaporation followed by cooling, resulting in their solidification

decantation standard terminologies on un.def: Process of obtaining finest powder of insoluble mineral drugs in water Description

squeezing.def: Manual process of obtaining extract/juice of selected plant drugs by squeezing crushed drugs

washing.def: Process of washing of drugs of plant, animal and mineral origin in order to clean, detoxify or get fine powder of the drug; washing is often undertaken to prepare drug of moderate properties and actions

cleaning of drugs.def: Process of cleaning drugs to remove impurities; different methods, such as washing, sieving, etc., are used for this purpose

cleaned/washed drug.def: Drug that has undergone cleaning process (Taṣfiya)

frying.def: Process of frying drugs in suitable oil

drug fried in oil.def: Drug that has been fried in oily substance

distillation.def: Process of obtaining purified liquid by heating it and collecting vapours in liquid form

calcination.def: Process of obtaining calx of metals, minerals, earthy materials and certain drugs of animal origin through specific procedures of heating

calx.def: Finely powdered form of a drug or medicinal preparation obtained by calcination of metals, minerals, earthy materials and certain drugs of animal origin

bloomed.def: Calx that has swelled up and become white and appears like flower; easily powdered Description

cutting.def: Process of cutting roots and bark into small pieces before processing

pounding and grinding.def: Process of crushing hard and dry plant drugs into small pieces or coarse powder

trituration/pulverization.def: Process of making powder of drugs by grinding
The process of powdering by grinding. Its possible English equivalent is trituration/pulverization. The process in which the hard and dry

cooking/boiling.def: Process of boiling drugs in water

purified mica.def: Abrak (mica) is cut into small pieces or coarsely powdered; it is laid into a bolus of cotton or jute which contains whole rice grains or small irregular pieces of stone and tied loosely; bolus is dipped in a relatively

grooved.def: Made hollow; hard woody material of a drug such as turpeth is removed and grooved part is used as drug

sheared/scissored.def: Some drugs are cut into small pieces before further processing,e.g., silk cocoon

unpierced pearl.def: Full round pearl bead without hole; such a pearl is considered better for medicinal use

scorched drug standard terminologies on.def: Drug after putting in a vessels is roasted until it starts to smell and changes its colour Description

pounder.def: Round or boat-shaped apparatus along with a pestle used to make fine powder of drugs

porphyry.def: Hard stone used to prepare mortar; considered hardest of stones, carved to form mortar in which hard drugs are ground to prepare powder

quartzite.def: Stone almost as hard as porphyry used to form mortar and pestle

touchstone.def: Black stone used to prepare mortar and pestle, of lesser hardness than porphyry

grindstone.def: Apparatus used for grinding or making a fine paste of drug manually; sil is a big flat piece of stone on which drug is placed and crushed with help of battā, an approximately cylindrical piece of stone used for crushing

mortar and pestle.def: Cylindrical apparatus made of iron/brass with metallic pestle, used for crushing and pounding of hard drugs

earthen crucible.def: Bowl made with a mixture of clay mixed with some other ingredients, which is then pasted with a special semi-liquid preparation

specially prepared sealing clay.def: Preparation of clay and cotton wool used for sealing vessels during process of calcination

cow-dung cake.def: Dried cow-dung cake made manually in shape of a pancake

cow dung.def: Dried cow dung collected from fields and forests Description

kept in pouch.def: Form of inclusion of drugs in a compound preparation in which drugs are kept in a pouch and then added to other drugs

purse/bag/pouch.def: Bag or pouch used to preserve or air-dry drug; sometimes drug is placed in a pouch and boiled

shelf-life.def: Length of time for which a drug in crude or finished form remains usable, effective and fit for consumption

sawdust.def: Dust obtained by filing hard drugs of plant origin

four seeds.def: Four seeds, viz., aspghol (seed of Plantago ovata), tukhm-i-rayḥān (seed of Ocimum sanctum), tukhm-i-kanocha (seed of Phyllanthus maderaspatensis) and tukhm-i-bārtang (seed of Plantago major),collectively known as chahār

three fruits standard terminologies on u.def: Three fruits, viz., āmla (Emblica officinalis), halayla (Terminalia chebula) and balayla (Terminalia bellirica), collectively known as athmār thalātha; this combination is used in a number of diseases and included in a n

three pungent drugs.def: Sonth (Zingiber officinale), mirch siyāh (Piper nigrum) and pīpal kalān (Piper longum), collectively known as ḥirrīfāt thalātha

five salts.def: Combination of five salts collected from five different sources: 1. Namak lāhavrī; 2. Namak siyāh; 3. Namak sānbhar; 4. Namak sonchar; 5. Namak biryārī

two drugs named centaurea behen and salv.def: Bahman safayd (Centaurea behen) and bahman surkh (Salvia haematodes), together known as bahmanayn

two tūdrī.def: Tūdrī zard (Matthiola incana) and tūdrī surkh (Cheiranthus cheiri), together known as tūdariyayn

two peppers.def: Two peppers, viz., Piper longum and Piper nigrum

small and large cardamoms.def: Two cardamoms, i.e., Amomum subulatum and Elettaria cardamomum

white and black musli.def: Two muslis, i.e., mūṣlī safayd (Chlorophytum borivilianum) and mūṣlī siyāh (Curculigo orchioides), collectively known as mūṣliyayn

white and red sandal.def: Ṣandal safayd (Santalum album) and ṣandal surkh (Pterocarpus santalinus), together known as Ṣandalayn

weight of a rice grain.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 15 mg Description

125 mg weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 125 mg

500 mg weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 500 mg

1 gram weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 1 g

3.5 gram weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 3.5 g

4.5 gram weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 4.5 g

5 gram weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 5 g

12 gram weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 12 g

21 gram weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 21 g

60 gram weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 60 g

240 gram weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 240 g

960 gram weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 960 g

2900 gram weight standard terminologies.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 2900 g Description

32 gram weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 32 g

20 gram weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 20 g

420 gram weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 420 g

250 mg weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 250 mg

1620 gram weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 1620 g

60 mg weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 60 mg

27 gram weight.def: A classical weight approximately equivalent to 27 g

therapeutics principles of treatment.def: A branch of medicine that deals with principles of treatment and management of diseases to restore health

management of disease.def: Treatment of diseases by different methods which may include regimens, diet, drugs and surgical intervention

rectification of disease- causing factor.def: Eradication or alleviation of any factor hindering restoration of health

diagnosis.def: To determine and find disease and its nature Description
To determine the nature of a disease. Its possible English equivalent is diagnosis.

dietotherapy.def: Treatment of diseases by manipulating and maintaining the dietary pattern depending upon requirement of treatment of diseases

principles of dietotherapy.def: Guidelines for use of dietary articles according to nature of illness and requirement of body for restoring health

stoppage of dietary intake.def: Complete abstinence from intake of food temporarily to facilitate evacuation of morbid matter from body

reducing dietary intake.def: Reducing the intake of diet in order to conserve power of digestive faculty

quantitative reduction of dietary intake.def: Reducing quantity of dietary intake but maintaining the nutritional requirement of body

qualitative reduction of dietary intake.def: Quantity of diet in terms of nutritional value is reduced, which decreases the production of humours and provides favourable medium for concoction of morbid matters

quantitative and qualitative reduction o.def: Diet both in terms of quality and quantity is reduced, particularly when faculties of body are not markedly disturbed

pharmacotherapy.def: Treatment of disease with help of medicines/drugs
equivalent is concoction. It is the cure of disease with the help of drugs. Its possible English equivalent is pharmacotherapy.

treatment with single drugs standard ter.def: Treatment of disease with single medicines, e.g., herbal or mineral drugs or drugs of animal origin Description

treatment with compound drugs/ formulati.def: Treatment of disease with compound drugs/ formulations, e.g., tablets, syrups, powders and other semi-solid dosage forms

principles of selection of medicine acco.def: Principles regarding selection of drugs according to their temperament/quality, whether hot, cold, moist or dry

heterotherapy.def: Method of treatment through heteropathy; one of the basic principles of treatment in Unani medicine in which physician chooses drugs, diets or any other mode of treatment having qualities opposite to quality /temperament

principles regarding dosage of a drug.def: Principles of advising/prescribing amount of drug to be given, i.e., dose according to age, weight, severity of disease, etc.

principles regarding time of administrat.def: Principles regarding time of administration commonly depend on action of drug as well as stage and severity of the disease

psychotherapy.def: Treatment of patients with psychiatric measures

principles/line of treatment.def: Line of treatment to select appropriate treatment including choice of drugs, changes in diet and regimen and surgical intervention, if needed

extensive treatment/ potent therapy.def: Treatment to remove diseases and restore health through strong and potent drugs/measures Description

principles of treatment for simple morbi.def: Line of treatment for abnormal nonsubstantial temperament that occurs without morbid matter in which just alteration of temperament is needed, usually without concoction and evacuation

principles of treatment for morbid tempe.def: Line of treatment for abnormal substantial temperament that occurs with morbid matter, in which concoction and evacuation are usually needed

correction of morbid temperament.def: Principles of treatment for correction of an abnormal temperament that leads to morbidity

concoction.def: Process by which morbid matter/humour matures and is made easily evacuable from body; drugs for concoction or the duration of concoction are specific to humoral morbidity

concoction /maturation of humours.def: Process by which humours mature and are made evacuable from body

concoction/maturation of phlegmatic humo.def: Process by which phlegmatic humour matures and is made evacuable from body

concoction/maturation of bilious humour.def: Process by which yellow bile matures and is made evacuable from body Process by which black bile matures and is made evacuable from body Description

cleansing of morbid matter/humour from b.def: Induced elimination of morbid material from the body, usually done after proper concoction

removal of pus.def: Principle of treatment of removal of pus from ulcers with help of drugs

diverting morbid matter.def: Diversion of morbid matter/humour from affected site to another site, or increase in flow of humours towards a specific site

diverting morbid matter to an adjacent o.def: Diversion/attraction of morbid matter/humour from affected site towards adjacent organs or parts of body

diverting morbid matter to a distant org.def: Diversion/attraction of morbid matter/humour from affected site towards a distant organ or part of body

pain induction to shift disease standard.def: Process of inducing pain by any means, like pressing or tying organ or cupping organ, etc., performed to divert morbid matter To shift morbid matter from diseased part to distant non-vital part Description

moderation of morbid matter.def: Moderation of morbid matter to bring it to normal/balanced condition

moderation/ attempering of blood.def: To bring morbid blood to normal/ balanced condition

moderation/attempering of yellow bile.def: Process to bring yellow bile to its normal condition

moderation of abnormal temperament.def: Moderation of temperament or bringing abnormal temperament to normal temperament

removal of cause.def: Removal of cause of disease or treatment of cause

stoppage of infiltration or effusion of.def: To stop infiltration or effusion of humours from one organ to another

calefaction of body or its part.def: Warming / heat production in body / part of body; a method of treatment in which heat is produced/generated in body by drugs or regimens

producing hotness in temperament.def: Producing heat in case of predominance of cold morbid temperament; a method of treatment in which heat is produced/generated in body by drugs or regimens to manage predominance of cold morbid temperament Description

warming extremities standard terminologi.def: Principle of treatment in which extremities are warmed through various means to divert disease-causing matter from affected organs towards them Description

increasing temperature of surrounding ai.def: Increasing room temperature of patient; principle of treatment advocated in case of cold morbid temperament

cooling of body or part of body.def: Cooling of body/part of body; a method of treatment in which coldness is produced/ generated or heat is reduced in body by drugs or regimen

producing cold in case of predominance o.def: A method of treatment in which cold is produced/ generated in body by drugs or regimen

decreasing temperature of surrounding ai.def: Principle of treatment advocated in case of hot morbid temperament Description

moistening of body or part of body.def: Process of moistening body or part of body by drugs or regimen

producing moistness in case of predomina.def: A method of treatment in which moistness is produced/ generated in body by drugs or regimen

inducing dryness.def: Process of producing dryness in body or part of body; the term does not cover Tajfīf mentioned under Unani pharmacy

extinguishing.def: Process of reducing increased heat of humours and organs; the term does not cover Iṭfā’ mentioned under Unani pharmacy

stopping production of vapours.def: Stopping production of vapours accumulated in an organ or body part

alleviation of morbid heat.def: Alleviation of morbid heat of body

inducing dryness by fluid absorption.def: Process of producing dryness through absorption of fluids and other material from any part of body, especially towards intestine, as in case of Ishāl (purgation) by action of drugs

process of refining / attenuation.def: Act of refining of any thick viscid matter

softening of swellings.def: Process of softening inflammation of hard organ/ inflammation associated with black bile

inducing rupture of swellings.def: Inducing rupture of swellings through local application of drugs/regimen

inducing deobstruction.def: Process of removing an obstruction by use of deobstruents Description

inducing deobstruction / dilatation of b.def: Process of dilatation of blood vessels either by using drugs having hot and wet temperament or drugs that dissolve thick substance blocking vessels

blood purification.def: Purification/cleansing of blood; elimination of anything which is an addition in quantity or quality to physiological composition of blood and considered as an impurity, by drug or regimen

disintegrating morbid matter.def: Cutting out morbid matter to make it easily evacuable

diluting morbid matter absorbing morbid.def: Making disease-causing matter dilute Attracting morbid matter from affected site to another site, one of the objectives of Imāla Description

thickening morbid matter.def: Making thin morbid matter thick to facilitate its evacuation and prevent its absorption in other organs

repelling morbid matter.def: Process that repels morbid matter from affected part

dissolving morbid matter.def: Dispersion of disease-causing matter accumulated in an organ or body part

dissolving flatus.def: Dissolution of flatus; process which expels flatus

dispersion of vapours.def: Dispersion of vapours accumulated in an organ or body part

condensation of vapours.def: Condensation of vapours accumulated in an organ or body part

inducing relaxation.def: Relaxation of muscles, commonly resulting in pain reduction

strengthening/toning up of organs.def: Process of toning up and strengthening organs and body to enhance functions and maintain health of body by stimulating metabolism, strengthening internal organs and improving body functions

toning up of vital organs.def: Process that strengthens vital organs, i.e., heart, brain and liver

toning up of nerves.def: Process which provides strength to nerves

toning up of vision.def: Toning up and strengthening vision; process which improves eyesight

toning up of heart.def: Process which tones up heart and improves its function

toning up of liver standard terminologie.def: Process which tones up liver and improves its function Description

toning up of stomach.def: Process which strengthens stomach and improves its function

toning up of kidney.def: Process which strengthens the kidney and improves its function

toning up of urinary bladder.def: Process which strengthens the urinary bladder and improves its function

toning up of sexual power / libido.def: Process which strengthens the genitals and improves their function

toning up of uterus.def: Process which strengthens uterus and improves its function

toning up of skin to grow hair.def: Process which strengthens the skin to grow hair

advising easily digestible nutritious di.def: Advising easily digestible nutritious diets for producing humours of good quality Description

trichogenesis.def: Process which increases circulation of blood in skin and helps growth of hair

anaesthesia.def: To induce loss of consciousness; a method of treatment in which whole body or part of it is reversibly anaesthetized by drugs
A method of treatment in which the whole body or a part of it is reversibly made numb or anaesthetized through drugs. Its possible English equivalent is anaesthesia.

hypnosis.def: Inducing sleep/causing hypnosis with help of drugs or regimens

alleviation.def: (1) To relieve pain; (2) to produce soothing effect; (3) to reduce peristaltic movements; the term is used for (1) relieving any type of pain; (2) producing soothing effect in any local lesion; (3) causing antidiarrhoeal

sedating brain.def: To cause sedation of the brain; process that reduces irritability or excitement of brain

sedating heart alleviation of morbid hum.def: Process of reducing abnormally increased function of heart Description

analgesia.def: To relieve pain by using drugs, changing temperament of pain site or some other means

reducing perspiration/ sweating.def: Process of reducing perspiration/sweating, required when there is excessive sweating

blistering/vesication.def: Process to induce blister formation by hot fluid or some drugs to increase blood supply of a particular organ, e.g., liniments used in male sexual debility

wound cleansing.def: Cleaning of infected wound with drugs

formation of flesh.def: Promotion of wound healing by use of desiccant drugs

producing milk.def: Process of increasing production of milk by drugs

producing semen.def: Process of increasing formation of semen by quality diet and drugs

thickening semen.def: Process of increasing consistency of semen by some drugs/diets Description

delaying ejaculation.def: Process by which ejaculation is delayed by using some drugs to treat premature ejaculation

diuresis.def: Process of increasing urination by some drugs/diets

to increase menstrual flow.def: Process of increasing menstrual flow in case of amenorrhoea, dysmenorrhoea, etc., through drugs/regimens

litholysis; lithotripsy.def: Process of crushing stone (usually renal/ ureteric/vesicular), by some drugs/regimens

killing of intestinal worms.def: Process of killing intestinal worms by vermicidal drugs

expulsion of intestinal worms.def: Process of expelling intestinal worms by vermifuges

induction of abortion.def: This term does not cover Isqāṭ mentioned under diseases

checking putrefaction.def: Process of checking or eradicating putrefaction

reducing temperature.def: Process to reduce temperature of body, usually by sweating or application of cooling agents like cold water or by some drugs, etc.

reducing morbid matter standard terminol.def: Reducing quantity of disease-causing matter Description

reducing weight.def: Process to shed body weight or reduce bulk of any part of body/organ

increasing weight.def: Process to increase body weight or bulk of any part of body/organ

inducing laxation regimenal therapy.def: Process to soften intestinal content or to evacuate excrement from intestine

regimenal therapy.def: Regimenal therapy is modification in Asbāb Sitta Ḍarūriyya (six essential factors) and application of regimens for maintenance of health as well as for management of diseases

inducing purgation.def: Evacuation of morbid matter from body through intestines as a regimen; the term does not cover Ishāl mentioned under diseases

inducing emesis.def: To induce vomiting as a regimen in order to evacuate gastric contents; the term does not cover Qay’ mentioned under diseases

inducing increased flow.def: To induce increased flow of urine/menstruation/ sweat/milk, etc.

expectoration.def: Induced expectoration/ expulsion of sputum from lungs; regimen/drugs by which sputum is expelled / expectorated from lungs Description

venesection.def: Bloodletting through venesection is a mode of regimenal therapy for complete evacuation of morbid matter, leading to moderation of all humours

ideal time for venesection.def: Ideal time of day for venesection for preventive purpose is forenoon, when digestion and evacuation processes have completed

time of mandatory / obligatory therapeut.def: Time of venesection for therapeutic purpose, irrespective of contraindications

preventive venesection.unani: Faṣd al-Mutahayyi’ / Faṣd al-Musta‘id li’l Amrāḍ
preventive venesection.def: Venesection indicated as a preventive measure for persons who are prone to certain diseases

therapeutic venesection.def: Venesection indicated for persons who are suffering from diseases in which venesection is indicated as therapeutic tool

permissibility of venesection.def: A range of conditions for which venesection may be conducted or avoided

venesection repeated after a few days.def: Repeating the process of venesection at the same site, ideally after two to three days

repeated venesection.def: Venesection that is repeated daily in selected conditions

blood vessels for venesection.def: Vessels, especially veins, from which blood- letting is done for therapeutic and preventive purposes

hijama, cupping, cupping therapy.def: Mode of regimenal therapy in which horns (nowadays cups) are used with or without scarification for diversion and evacuation of morbid matter from blood; may be without scarification or with scarification (blood-letting)

wet cupping; cupping with scarification.def: Cupping therapy with scarification for blood- letting to achieve local evacuation of morbid matter

dry cupping; cupping without scarificati.def: Cupping therapy without scarification; only application of cup with vacuum creation within it for diversion of morbid matter

fire cupping.def: Dry cupping, where vacuum is created with fire inside cup

non-fire cupping water cupping.def: Cupping therapy where vacuum is created without fire Cupping therapy along with warm water/ medicinal decoction while cupping

obligatory cupping standard terminologie.def: Cupping carried out for a diseased condition Description

optional cupping.def: Cupping carried out for prevention and promotion of health

cupping over right shoulder.def: Application of cupping procedure over right shoulder

cupping over left shoulder.def: Application of cupping procedure over left shoulder

cupping over inner side of forearm.def: Cupping with scarification over inner side of forearm

cupping over breast.def: Application of cupping procedure over breast

dry cupping over liver.def: Application of cupping procedure without scarification over liver

dry cupping over spleen.def: Application of cupping procedure without scarification over spleen

dry cupping around umblicus.def: Application of cupping procedure without scarification around umbilicus

dry cupping over region of kidney.def: Application of cupping procedure without scarification over region of kidney

dry cupping over abdomen.def: Application of cupping procedure over abdomen without scarification Description

dry cupping over region of acetabulum of.def: Application of cupping procedure without scarification over region of acetabulum of femur

cupping over interscapular region.def: Cupping over interscapular region, i.e,. between shoulders; equivalent to venesection of basilic vein

extent and number of incisions to be mad.def: Term applicable to number and depth of incisions, depending on quality and consistency of morbid fluid, physique, season, etc.

cup used for cupping therapy.def: Cup (horn, glass or plastic, etc.) used to apply with vacuum for cupping therapy; earlier, horn of animal was used for this purpose; some people also used bamboo vessel

leech therapy.def: Leech application as a regimen is one of the methods of evacuation; it is specially useful at a site where venesection or Ḥijāma is not done properly

inducing diaphoresis.def: Sweating as a regimen is usually done to remove morbid matter through skin Description

therapeutic bath/ turkish bath.def: Turkish bath, constructed as per specific guidelines, where temperatures of different rooms are different for therapeutic purposes like cleansing, reducing viscosity of matter and elimination and diversion of morbid matt

hot bath.def: In this type of bath, the person has to stay in a hot dry room as well as a hot wet room for specified periods of time

Hammam in which hot water is used. This Hammam, kind of softens skin, increases blood circulation, relaxes muscles and reduces pain. It is beneficial in diseases such as arthralgia, hepatalgia. Duration of this bath is 5

medicated bath.def: In this type of bath, medicated water is used
is sand bath. Hammam, A type of in which medicated water (hot or cold) is used. It is recommended in various diseases. Its possible English equivalent is medicated bath.

borax bath.def: In this type of bath, a little borax is mixed in water
Hammam, A type of in which borax mixed water, is used for bathing. It is useful in skin disorders. Its possible English equivalent is borax bath.

sulphur bath.def: In this type of bath, a little sulphur is mixed in water

Hammam in which, the water containing sulphur is used. It is beneficial for skin diseases, purifies the nerves, and relieves the pain due to distension and convulsion. Its possible English equivalent is sulphur bath.

sand bath.def: In this type of bath, part of body or whole body up to neck is covered with sand

sea bath.def: Hammam, in which sea water is used for bath. As the sea water contains various salts, it is beneficial in certain skin diseases. Its possible English equivalent is sea bath.

sun bath.def: Exposure to sunlight is often called sun bath

oil bath.def: In this type of bath, some oil or some drugs dissolved in olive oil are massaged over body

astringent bath exercise standard termin.def: In this type of bath, water is used in which some strong astringent like alum is dissolved Activity involving physical effort, to maintain or improve health Description

passive exercise.def: Exercise is not intentionally done but carried out as a part of profession, e.g., blacksmith
Exercise done without the effort of the person. Its possible English equivalent is passive exercise.

active exercise.def: Exercise which is done purely and intentionally in order to get benefits of exercise

exercise for a shorter duration.def: Exercise for a shorter duration, stopped soon after starting

exercise for a prolonged duration.def: Exercise that is not stopped soon but continued for a longer duration

rapid exercise.def: Rapid/fast exercise, where movement is done rapidly at a fast pace
Exercise, done with rapid body movements. Its possible English equivalent is rapid exercise.

slow exercise.def: Slow exercise, where movement is done at a slow pace
Exercise, done with slow body movements. Its possible English equivalent is slow exercise.

relaxing exercise.def: Exercise which is done slowly and without much force

moderate exercise.def: Moderate exercise, where force as well as movement is moderate
The exercise, moderate in all aspects such as duration, strength, quantity etc. Its possible English equivalent is moderate exercise.

therapeutic massage.def: Massage with techniques ranging from light, moderate, and deep pressure; regimen involving manual manipulation of muscles Description

massage with firm / strong pressure.def: Deep, strong and extensive pressure massage

moderate massage.def: Moderate massage in terms of force as well as time
