A type of massage, in which the body is rubbed moderately in every aspect, such as strokes and duration. It helps in the growth and development of the body. Its possible English equivalent is moderate massage.

rough massage.def: Rough massage, usually done with a rough cloth
A type of massage done with the help of rough cloth. It draws the blood rapidly to the body surface. Its possible English equivalent is rough massage. A type of gentle massage done for a

soft/smooth massage.def: Soft or smooth massage usually done with oil or soft cloth

preparatory massage.def: Massage done before exercise as a preparatory measure

restorative massage surgical interventio.def: Massage done after exercise to relax body/ organ and restore its normal condition

treatment with surgical interventions st.unani: Al-‘Amal bi’l Yad / ‘Ilāj bi’l Yad/Ṣanā‘a al-Yad/ Ṣanā‘a al-Jirāḥa/ Al-‘Amal bi’l Ḥadīd
treatment with surgical interventions st.def: Treatment of diseases through surgical procedures Description

cautery.def: Procedure of burning skin or flesh to stop bleeding or avoid further putrefaction with help of heated instrument or caustic substance

actual cautery.def: Procedure of cauterization with heated metals

cauterization by iron.def: Procedure of cauterization with heated iron

cauterization by gold.def: Procedure of cauterization with heated gold

cauterization with caustics.def: Procedure of cauterization with caustic substances

site of cauterization.def: Site/part of body which is subjected to cauterization

incision.def: Making of cut into body during surgical intervention

incision and drainage of abscess.def: Process of making incision over abscess to drain pus

blepharoplasty.def: Removal of superfluous lashes grown on eyelid

excision of excrescence of canthus.def: Surgical removal or reconstructive surgical intervention of medial or lateral canthus Description

excision of pterygium.def: Surgical removal of pterygium for a therapeutic purpose

excision of reddish growth of eyelids.def: Cutting red granulation growth which blocks pupil or grows over eyelids

excision of pannus.def: Removal of network of blood vessels spreading over eye

couching of cataract.def: Couching is a technique whereby lens is dislodged, thus removing opacity

nasal polypectomy.unani: Qaṭ‘ al-Laḥm al- Nābit fi’l Anf
nasal polypectomy.def: Removal of superfluous fleshy growth in nose

excision of wart growing on end of nose.unani: Qaṭ‘ al-Tha’ālīl al-Nābita fī Ṭaraf al-Anf
excision of wart growing on end of nose.def: Removal of wart grown on tip of nose by cutting it

suturing of nose, lip and ear.unani: Khiyāṭa al-Anf wa’l Shafa wa’l Udhun
suturing of nose, lip and ear.def: Procedure of bringing edges of wounds in nose, lip and ear together with a suture

extraction of nodules of lips.def: Removal of hard swelling of inner side of lips

excision of superfluous growths on gums.unani: Qaṭ‘ al-Laḥm al- Zā’id fi’l Litha
excision of superfluous growths on gums.def: Superfluous growth is commonly removed by semilunar incision

extraction of tooth extraction of roots.def: Removal of tooth from dental alveolus Removal of roots of teeth retained in gums Description

scraping teeth.def: Procedure of removal of crusts and gritty substances collected on inner and outer surfaces of teeth

interlacing loose teeth.def: Interweaving loose front teeth with gold or silver wire

removal of ranula.def: Procedure of complete removal of ranula

laryngeal incision.def: Procedure of making a cut into larynx under third or fourth ring of trachea

extraction of fish-bone from throat.def: Procedure of removal of bone or fish-bone or anything stuck in throat

removal of leech sticking in throat.unani: Ikhrāj al-‘Alaq al- Nāshib fi’l Ḥalq
removal of leech sticking in throat.def: Procedure of removal of leech sticking in throat

amputation of extra finger.def: Surgical removal of supernumerary finger or toe

separation of webbed fingers.def: Procedure of cutting web away in case of webbed fingers

extraction of thorn.def: Procedure in which a thorn is removed from body

extraction of stone.def: Surgical procedure carried out to remove stone from urinary bladder

extraction of dead fetus.def: Procedure of extraction of dead fetus from womb Description

perforation of haemorrhoids.def: Surgical procedure of perforation of internal and external haemorrhoids

ligation of haemorrhoids.def: Surgical procedure of ligation of haemorrhoids to make them fall off eventually

haemorrhoidectomy.def: Surgical procedure of removal of internal and external haemorrhoids

amputation of limbs.def: Procedure involving amputation of limbs affected by gangrene

sawing off bone.def: Cutting a bone or piece of bone

circumcision.def: Surgical procedure of removal of skin of prepuce

excision of varices.def: Procedure of removal of twisted veins surgically

extraction of guinea worm.def: Procedure of removal of guinea worm (Dracunculus medinensis) through surgical intervention

extraction of arrow.def: Procedure of removal of arrow from body

amputation standard terminologies on una.def: Cutting or removal of part of body to control disease process; the term does not cover Batr mentioned under mechanism of disease Description

bone-setting.def: Art of setting of fracture or dislocation

reduction of dislocation.def: Procedure of reduction of dislocated joints of body

suturing.def: Surgical procedure which involves stitching edges of wound with help of sutures

operative instruments.def: Instruments used to carry out surgery

gold cautery.def: Instrument made of gold, used for cauterization

iron cautery.def: Instrument made of iron, used for cauterization

claviform cautery.def: Nail- or boat-shaped instrument for cautery

edged cautery.def: A simple iron with an edged extremity at right angle to shaft

cautery of two blades.def: Instrument for cautery provided with two blades

crescent-shaped cautery.def: Instrument for cautery having crescentic end

hollow cautery.def: Instrument for cautery similar to vulture’s quill Description

cautery with two spits.def: Instrument for cautery having two spits

cautery with three spits.def: Instrument for cautery having three spits

thin cautery.def: Executed by an instrument which looks like a ball pen, intended for coagulating tissue or arresting bleeding from small vessels by heat conducted through wire tip

cannula/tube.def: A plain straight tube made of metal, through which cautery is passed to treat certain disorders

razor.def: Tool used for removal of hair before surgical procedure

needle.def: Instrument employed for suturing during surgery

scissor.def: Instrument used for dissecting and cutting during surgical procedures

scalpel/lancet.def: Sharp-bladed instrument used during surgical procedures

probe.def: A flexible slender surgical instrument with a blunt end that is used to explore wounds or body cavities

small probe standard terminologies on un.def: A small flexible slender surgical instrument with a blunt end that is used to explore wounds or body cavities Description

exploring needle.def: An exploring needle utilized in procedure carried out to treat sebaceous cysts

forceps.def: An instrument used for grasping and holding firmly

forceps/pincers.def: Powerful jointed-type forceps or pincers especially used for extracting teeth

hook.def: Instrument used for picking up and holding tissues

couching needle.def: Instrument for cataract surgery, used during couching procedure

cephalotribe.def: Instrument used to crush head of stillborn fetus

speculum.def: Metal instrument used to dilate an orifice or canal in body to allow inspection

surgical pad.def: Packing material used during surgical procedures

bandage.def: Piece of material commonly used to hold dressing in place

splint.def: Device used to stabilize fractured or injured bones and joints

scraper.def: Instrument used for scraping diseased tissues of body Description

saw.def: Instrument used to cut or remove bones

drill.def: Instrument for making hole in bone

chisel.def: Instrument for cutting and shaping bones

urinary catheter dietetics.def: A hollow tube used to drain urinary bladder

dietary regimen / modification.def: Maintenance of health and management of diseases through diets

absolute diet.def: Dietary substance which becomes part of body and does not produce any qualitative change such as heat, cold, etc.; the term does not cover Ghidhā’ (diet) mentioned under preservation and promotion of health

single diet.def: Diet used without addition of other substances

compound diet.def: Diet prepared by combination of many other substances

diet of hot temperament.def: Dietary substance which produces heat in body

diet of cold temperament.def: Dietary substance which produces cold in body

diet of wet temperament standard termino.def: Dietary substance which produces wetness in body Description

diet of dry temperament.def: Dietary substance which produces dryness in body

beneficial diet.def: Dietary substance producing healthy effect in body

harmful diet.def: Dietary substance producing unhealthy effect in body

diet with potential of becoming part of.def: Diet with potential to become part of body organ
Dietary substance with potential to become part of body organ after being affected by innate heat of body

diet ready to become part of organ.def: Dietary substance ready to become part of body organ, such as secondary fluid

diet becoming part of organ.def: Dietary substance which departs from its specific form and becomes part of body organ

dietary drug.def: Substance which is primarily used as diet but produces some pharmacological actions

diet having effect through its matter, q.def: Dietary substance which produces effect in body through its specific form, matter and quality

diet having effect through its matter an.def: Dietary substance which produces effect in body through its matter and specific form Description

easily digestible diet.def: Dietary substance which is spontaneously absorbed in organs without leaving behind considerable amount of residue in stomach and vessels

difficult-to-digest diet.def: Dietary substance which is not spontaneously absorbed in organs and a considerable amount of residue is left behind in stomach and vessels

completely digestible diet / freely abso.def: Dietary substance which is completely digested and does not remain immature

partially digestible diet / partially ab.def: Dietary substance which is not completely digested

diet whose wastes are quickly removable.def: Dietary substance whose waste products are quickly evacuated from body

diet whose wastes are difficult to remov.def: Dietary substance whose waste products are not quickly evacuated from the body

diet remaining in stomach for short peri.def: Dietary substance which does not stay in stomach for a longer period

diet remaining in stomach for longer per.def: Dietary substance which stays in stomach for a longer period

low-nutrient diet.def: Dietary substance low in nutrition and producing more waste products

high-nutrient diet.def: Dietary substance having more nutrition and producing fewer waste products

dietary substance producing humours of g.unani: Ghidhā’ Ṣāliḥ al- Kaymūs/ Ghidhā’ Ḥasan al-Kaymūs/ Ghidhā’ Jayyid al-Kaymūs

dietary substance producing humours of b.unani: Ghidhā’ Radī’ al- Kaymūs / Ghidhā’ Fāsid al-Kaymūs

diet of rich nutritional value producing.unani: Ghidhā’ Laṭīf Kathīr al-Ghidhā’ Ḥasan al-Kaymūs
Ghidhā’ Laṭīf Kathīr al-Ghidhā’ Radī’ al-Kaymūs
Ghidhā’ Laṭīf Kathīr al-Ghidhā’ Mu‘tadil al-Kaymūs

diet of poor nutritional value producing.unani: Ghidhā’ Laṭīf Qalīl al-Ghidhā’ Ḥasan al-Kaymūs
Ghidhā’ Laṭīf Qalīl al-Ghidhā’ Radī’ al-Kaymūs
Ghidhā’ Laṭīf Qalīl al-Ghidhā’ Mu‘tadil al-Kaymūs

instant nutrition providing diet.def: Diets providing instant but small quantity of nutrition and easily becoming part of body

delayed nutrition providing diet.def: Diet providing more nutrition in a delayed manner and not easily becoming part of body organs

sweet-smelling diet.def: Sweet aroma diets, usually possessing hot temperament

foul-smelling diet.def: Diet having foul odour, usually providing poor nutrition to body

tastes of diets.def: Tastes of various kinds of food materials

sweet-tasting diet.def: Sweet-tasting diet with hot and wet temperament and heat of moderate degree

bitter-tasting diet.def: Bitter-tasting diet with hot temperament to greater degree than salty diet, causing desiccation

pungent diet standard terminologies on u.def: Diet which has hot temperament to greater degree than bitter diet Description

salty diet.def: Diet which has hot and dry temperament

sour astringent diet.def: Diet which has cold temperament to lesser degree and dry temperament to greater degree than sour diet

sour diet.def: Diet which has cold and dry temperament

fatty diet.def: Diet which has hot and wet temperament but heat to lesser degree and wetness to greater degree than sweet diet

tasteless diet.def: Diet which has moderately cold temperament

black-bile-producing diets.def: Diets that increase production of black bile

weight-gaining diets.def: Diets helpful in gaining body weight

diets causing flatulence.unani: Al-Aghdhiya al- Muwallida li’l Riyāḥ/ Al-Aghdhiya al-Nāfikha

laxative diets.unani: Al-Aghdhiya al- Mulayyina li’l Ṭabī‘a / Al-Aghdhiya al- Mulayyina li’l Baṭn

constipative diets.unani: Al-Aghdhiya al-Ḥābisa li’l Baṭn/ Al-Aghdhiya al-Mumsika li’l Baṭn

concentrated grape juice.def: Preparation made by mixing honey or sugar with concentrated grape juice

bread.def: Bread, prepared with flour of different cereals

white bread.def: Bread made of refined wheat flour

triangular pastry.def: Fried or baked food preparation of flour base with a filling of seasoned meat

dessert made of unleavened dough and dry.def: Fried dessert made of moderately cooked unleavened dough, almond, pistachio, sugar or honey

starch-containing preparation.def: A sweet made of rice, starch and some other ingredients

soup.def: Gravy of meat prepared with water and salt

preparation of meat containing sumac as.def: Food preparation having cold and dry temperament

preparation of meat containing indian be.def: Food preparation having cold and dry temperament

preparation of meat containing spinach a.def: Food preparation having moderately hot temperament

preparation of meat containing turnip as.def: Food preparation having hot and moist temperament

preparation of fried chopped meat prepar.def: Food preparation having hot and moist temperament Description

preparation containing pomegranate as on.def: Food preparation having cold and dry temperament

preparation containing lemon juice as on.def: Food preparation having cold and dry temperament

preparation containing apple as one of i.def: Food preparation having cold and dry temperament

preparation containing carrot as one of.def: Food preparation having hot and moist temperament

unleavened flat bread.def: Bread made with wholewheat flour, which is kneaded with water to make dough, and then thoroughly rolled into flattened mass and cooked on a flat skillet (Tavā)

frumenty/porridge.def: A semi-liquid dish prepared with hulled and slightly ground wheat cooked in milk

preparation of sābū dāna.def: Preparation of Sābū Dāna and milk, often added with sugar

dessert made of mainly rice and milk.def: Preparation of ground aromatic rice cooked in milk, with some sugar

knowledge of preservation of health.def: Branch of medical science which deals with maintenance, preservation and promotion of health as well as prevention of diseases through various regimens and practices

six essential factors.unani: Al-Asbāb al-Sitta al-Ḍarūriyya/ Asbāb Sitta Ḍarūriyya
six essential factors.def: Six essential factors for maintaining physical, mental, social and spritual health which include: air; foods and drinks; bodily movement and repose; mental activity and repose; sleep and wakefulness; retention and evacua

inhaled air.def: Air which is inhaled through breathing and is one of the constituents of pneuma

quality air.unani: Al-Hawā’ al-Jayyid / Al-Hawā’ al-Ṣāfī / Awfaq al-Ahwiya
quality air.def: Air free from pollutants like smoke, gases, foreign bodies as well as other hazardous matters; essential/effective in preserving and maintaining health

hot air.def: Air which attracts blood towards skin; when moderately hot it may dissolve some body fluids and may cause relaxation in body; when extremely hot it may increase perspiration and thirst, decrease urination, weaken digesti

cold air standard terminologies on unani.def: Air which increases appetite, improves digestion, constricts body pores and increases urination Description

north winds.def: Air which blows from north to south has cold and dry properties, which, however, vary according to geographical location

south winds.def: Air which blows towards the north has hot and wet properties, which, however, vary according to geographical location

east winds.def: Air which blows towards the west has hot and dry properties, which, however, vary according to geographical location

west winds.def: Air which blows towards the east has wet properties, which, however, vary according to geographical location

four seasons.def: The four seasons, i.e., spring, summer, autumn and winter each has its own temperament which influences human health; the term does not cover Al-Faṣl mentioned under logic and philosophy
Kharif, Fasl-i-Sayf, Fasl-i-Shita’ of a year and all of these have their own temperament which influences the human health. Its possible English equivalent is four seasons.

spring.def: One of the four seasons with moderate temperament, optimally suitable for human health

autumn.def: One of the four seasons with hot and wet temperament, not considered suitable for optimal health

summer.def: One of the four seasons with hot and dry temperament, not considered suitable for optimal health in its extreme condition Description

winter.def: One of the four seasons with cold and wet temperament, suitable for hot temperament, not suitable for aged people

hot habitats.def: Regions with hot climate; indigenous people generally have dark or frizzy hair, weak digestion and early ageing; may also have dark complexion

cold habitats.def: Regions with cold climate; indigens are generally robust, strong, bold, courageous and with better digestion

humid habitats.def: Humid regions where summers are not extremely hot and winters are not extremely cold; indigens generally have soft/lighter complexion; such people are more succeptible to epilepsy, protracted fever, pustular stomatitis,

dry habitats.def: Regions with extreme summers and extreme winters; indigens may have dry temperament as well as dry and dusky skin

foods and drinks.def: One of the six essential factors, referring to all foods and drinks consumed by people, essential for life and maintenance of health through replacement of used up and broken down components of body

essential factors. Asbab Daruriyya. One of the Sitta It refers to all the foods and drinks which the human being consumes. Proper balance is required in this factor. It acts on the body in three ways; by their quality or

diet(s) standard terminologies on unani.def: Food(s) / nutritious item(s) consumed regularly; produce effect through their quality and replenish the body; the term does not cover Ghidhā’ Muṭlaq (absolute diet) mentioned under dietetics Description

nourishment.def: Process of providing or obtaining food necessary for health and growth

ideal water.def: Sweet, light-in-weight and odourless water, which easily becomes hot and cold, passes quickly down from stomach and does not cause renal and vesicular calculi

boiled water.def: Water purified by boiling, which does not cause flatulence and passes quickly out of body

distilled water.def: Water obtained through condensation of vapours produced after boiling
The water purified by distillation. Its possible English equivalent is distilled water.

soft water.def: Water that is odourless, tasteless, free from contamination and light in weight, which passes quickly down from stomach and can easily be rendered hot or cold
possible English equivalent is soft water. Ma’-i-Yusr. Same as Unhealthy and hard water due to

saline water.def: Water that contains natural salts in appreciable amount and induces diarrhoea, makes skin thin and dry and causes emaciation
Water that contains natural salts (chlorides) in appreciable amounts. Its possible English equivalent is saline water.

hard water.def: Water that contains an appreciable amount of minerals
English equivalent is hot water. The water that contains salts of calcium or magnesium resists the action of soap and reduces lather formation Its possible English equivalent is hard water.

stagnant water.def: Water which is hard and unhygienic; it contains soil material and other contaminants and is liable to cause various ailments

hot water.unani: Al-Mā’ al-Ḥārr / Mā’ Sukhn / Āb Garm
hot water.def: Water which is suitable for persons of cold temperament Description

tepid water.def: Water which produces more moisture than cold water, helps to expel thick and viscid material through emesis

cold water.def: Water obtained from melting ice or cooled by other means; when of moderate temperature is suitable for healthy persons

rain water.def: Rain water is considered to be of good quality, especially if not mixed with soil

spring water.unani: Mā’ al-‘Ayn/ Mā’ al- ‘Uyūn/ Mā’ al-Nab‘
spring water.def: Water obtained from springs and free from contaminants, is considered to be the best for use

well water.def: Water which is inferior to spring water due to its confinement, prolonged contact with soil and possibilities of contamination

marsh water.def: Water found in wetland, which is considered inferior in quality to well water due to its long stagnation in sodden ground and among herbaceous plants

tank water.def: Water stored in a tank is considered better than well water, especially when rain water has been stored in it

karez water / underground tunnel water s.def: Water which is stored by karez technique is considered better than well water but inferior to spring water because of the possibility of becoming polluted, as it does not come out of earth by its own force but is collect

water surrounded by reeds and thicket.def: Water surrounded by reeds and thicket is not good for health; it may cause diseases of spleen and ascites, etc.

hail and ice water.def: Hail and ice water is suitable for persons of hot temperament and during hot season

water containing minerals.def: Water that contains appreciable amounts of certain salts, used for therapeutic purposes but not suitable for regular use

water containing iron.def: Water that contains certain amount of iron

water containing ammonium chloride.def: Water that contains certain amount of ammonium chloride

alum-containing water.def: Water that contains certain amount of alum

sulphur-containing water.def: Water that contains certain amount of sulphur

copper-containing water.def: Water that contains certain amount of copper

bodily movement and repose.def: One of six essential factors for maintenance of health; it may also be used for managing certain disease conditions; bodily movements may increase innate heat and are necessary for digestion and metabolism and also help

psychic movement and repose.def: One of six essential factors for maintenance of health; it may also be used for managing certain disease conditions; psychic movement increases heat, while repose increases cold; a balance between the two ensures optimum

sleep and wakefulness.def: One of six essential factors for maintenance of health; it may also be used for managing certain disease conditions; moderate sleep is necessary for preserving pneuma and vitality, replenishing the body, improving digest

retention.def: One of six essential factors for maintenance of health; it is necessary for digestion and metabolism as well as maintaining reserves of nutrients in body; in case of any imbalance, various disorders may occur, e.g., obst
massage. The physiological process which retains essential nutrients in the body for the maintenance of health. Its possible English equivalent is retention. The health care of the traveller. It

evacuation standard terminologies on una.def: One of six essential factors for maintenance of health; it is necessary for removal of waste products/metabolites through defecation, micturition, menstruation, coitus, sweating and spittle; in case of any imbalance, var

preventive measures.def: Preventive measures adopted to counter disease-causing factors and to maintain healthy state

preservation of health through air.def: Promotion and restoration of healthby ensuring provision of good quality air and adopting measures to minimize the effect of polluted air

preservation of health with respect to f.unani: Ḥifẓ al-Ṣiḥḥa bi Ḥasabi’l Awqāt al-Arba‘a
preservation of health with respect to f.def: Promotion and restoration of health with respect to four seasons; adopting regimens and life style modifications with respect to four seasons as per the six essential factors doctrine and nonessential factors as well; th

preservation of health through water int.def: Promotion and maintenance of health by following measures related to quality, quantity and timing of water intake

preservation of health through diet.unani: Ḥifẓ al-Ṣiḥḥa bi’l Ghidhā’ / Tadbīr al-Ṣiḥḥa bi’l Ghidhā’
preservation of health through diet.def: Preservation of health by following measures related to quality, quantity and timings of food intake, e.g., daily requirement of food, sequencing of different kinds of food, etc.

preservation of health through sleep and.unani: Ḥifẓ al-Ṣiḥḥa bi’l Nawm wa’l Yaqẓa
preservation of health through sleep and.def: Promotion and maintenance of health through sleep and wakefulness; preservation of health by following measures to maintain balance between sleep and wakefulness, e.g., duration, timing, posture during sleep, etc. Descri

preservation of health through exercise.def: Promotion and maintenance of health through exercise; a regimenal therapy, which revives innate heat, disperses and expels accumulated harmful material from body, keeps organs fit for their functions, improves nutrition

preservation of health through massage.def: a regimenal therapy which tones up body, resolves waste products, increases innate heat, helps open skin pores and relaxes muscles

preservation of health through purgation.def: a regimenal therapy which evacuates waste products and morbid humours from body

preservation of health through emesis.def: a regimenal therapy, which evacuates waste products from stomach and also some morbid humours

preservation of health through moderate.def: Preservation of health through moderation in coitus, which produces exhilaration, increases positivity, subsides sorrow, grief and anger, increases thinking capacity, rejuvenates innate heat of body, resolves waste produ

antenatal care.def: Preventive healthcare of pregnant woman and fetus through various regimes

preventive healthcare of lactating woman.def: Measures of promotion of health of lactating woman, e.g., dietary regimens, exercise, massage, cleanliness, management of common problems associated with breastfeeding, etc. Factors taken into consideration for selection

guidance for breastfeeding.def: Guidance which recommends preference to mother’s milk, breastfeeding two or three times a day, giving a little honey to baby before breastfeeding, cradle song, etc.

neonatal care.def: Advice on modalities for newborns to keep them healthy, including proper cutting of umbilical cord, care of skin of baby, proper bathing, etc.

post-weaning care of infant.def: Post-weaning care of infant for promotion and maintenance of health and growth through regimens / regimenal therapies, which include measures like replacement of mother’s milk with dietary substances, gentle exercise, ap

preservation of health of child.def: Preservation and promotion of health and growth of child through regimens/regimenal therapies, which include measures like providing suitable dietary substances which produce humours of good quality, developing ability t

preservation of health of young adult.def: Preservation and promotion of health and growth of young adult through regimens / regimenal therapies, which may include measures like intake of suitable dietary substances which produce humours of good quality, exercise

preservation of health during age of dec.def: Preservation and promotion of health during age of decline through regimens/regimenal therapies; these may include measures like cleansing of body from excessive phlegmatic humour, intake of diet having hot and moist tem

preservation of health in old age.def: Preservation and promotion of health in elderly people through regimens / regimenal therapies; these may include rehabilitative measures and measures like intake of suitable diet and drinks, clearing of bowels regularly,

preservation of health of convalescent.def: Care of convalescent for promotion and maintenance of health; health restoration in a person recovering from illness through regimens / regimenal therapies; these may include use of certain formulations, strengthening in

preservation of health through selection.def: Factors to be taken into account when selecting place of residence for promotion and maintenance of health include quality of soil, altitude, quality of construction, ventilation, amount of light, temperature, climate, q

guidance for traveller.def: Guidance for traveller for promotion and maintenance of health, including certain precautionary measures

preservation of health of land traveller.unani: Ḥifẓ Ṣiḥḥa al- Musāfir fi’l Barr
preservation of health of land traveller.def: Preservation and promotion of health of traveller during travel over land; preventive measures may include special dietary regimens, quality of water, adjusting to sleep and food timings, avoidance of heat, fatigue, suns

preservation of health of sea traveller.unani: Ḥifẓ Ṣiḥḥa al- Rākib fi’l Baḥr
preservation of health of sea traveller.def: Preservation and promotion of health of traveller by sea; specific preventive measures may include special dietary regimens, measures to prevent sea sickness, management of vertigo, nausea, vomiting, etc.

preservation of health through therapeut.def: Preservation and promotion of health through use of therapeutic bath / Turkish bath, which prevents accumulation of morbid humours and waste products, increases innate heat and has digestive and excretory effects, etc.

preservation of health of organs.def: Preservation and promotion of health of organs by adopting certain preventive measures / regimenal therapies

preservation of health of hair.def: Preservation and promotion of health of hair through various preventive measures, e.g., application of suitable oils and washing of hair with suitable liquid preparations to keep it healthy; evacuation of predominant mat

preservation of health of head.def: Preservation and promotion of health of head through various preventive measures

preservation of health of eye.def: Preservation and promotion of ophthalmic health / eye care through various preventive measures, e.g., local application of eye tonic and avoiding prolonged exposure to bright objects and eyestrain

preservation of nasal health.def: Preservation and promotion of health of nose through various preventive measures

preservation of health of ear.def: Preservation and promotion of health of ear through various preventive measures

preservation of health of tongue.def: Preservation and promotion of health of tongue through various preventive measures

preservation of health of teeth.def: Preservation and promotion of health of teeth through various preventive measures

preservation of health of lungs and trac.unani: Ḥifẓ Ṣiḥḥa al-Ri’a wa Qaṣaba al-Ri’a
preservation of health of lungs and trac.def: Preservation and promotion of health of lungs and trachea through various preventive measures, e.g., talking with moderate tone, expulsion of morbid matter coming from sinuses, lungs and trachea and application of suitab

preservation of cardiac health standard.def: Preservation and promotion of cardiac health through various preventive measures, e.g., use of exhilarants and cardiac tonics and avoidance of distress and anxiety Description

preservation of health of stomach.def: Preservation and promotion of health of stomach through various preventive measures, e.g., intake of good quality food and water in suitable quantity, healthy eating habits, etc.

preservation of health of intestine.def: Preservation and promotion of health of intestine through various preventive measures, e.g., avoidance of flatulent foodstuffs

preservation of health of liver.def: Preservation and promotion of health of liver through various preventive measures

preservation of health of organs of the.def: Preservation and promotion of health of organs of urinary system through various preventive measures

preservation of health of joints.def: Preservation and promotion of health of joints through various preventive measures

epidemic.def: Pestilential change occurring in air, water or environment leading to putrefaction in humour of body
possible English equivalent is epidemic. A method of removal of waste products of the body through urination. Its possible English equivalent is diuresis. Asbab Daruriyya The first of the six Sitta

refrainment.def: Refraining from use of certain things that can modify course of treatment or attainment of health under guidance of a physician

abstinence/ restriction.def: Avoidance of a substance or act that is not suitable for health of a person in a particular condition
Avoidance from a substance, harmful to the health of a person. Its possible English equivalent is abstinence/ restriction.

contraception.def: equivalent is family planning. Prevention of conception or impregnation. Its possible English equivalent is contraception.

coitus interruptus.def: Method of preventing unwanted pregnancy in which a man withdraws his penis from a woman’s vagina before ejaculation

weaning.def: Withdrawal of breastfeeding for a baby in a gradual manner to accustom him or her to food
Withdrawal of child from breast feeding. The withdrawal is done in gradual manner and is started with giving supplement food along with mother’s milk. Its possible English equivalent is weaning.

ice zone.def: A zone stretching beyond the neutral zone up to north and south poles. Its possible English equivalent is ice zone.

tropic zone.def: A zone lying from the equator at 23.5° north and south on both sides. Its possible English equivalent is tropic zone.

noon/mid day.def: An imaginary point which divides the day equally into two parts. Its possible English equivalent is noon/mid day. An imaginary line situated at 23.5° south

capricorn.def: from the equator. When the sun reaches this line, the days in north get longer and nights shorter and the winter season get intense. Its possible English equivalent is capricorn.

inference.def: The state of an object being in a particular form naturally or otherwise leading to the knowledge of other unknown things is called inference e.g. the presence of smoke indicates the presence of fire. Its possible Englis

race.def: A whole, whose different units bear individual identities e.g. animal. The humans, cattle etc. are different but belong to the same Jins i.e. animal. Its possible English equivalent is race.

character.def: An exclusive feature or character of a community which distinguishes it from others, for instance laughing which is hallmark of humans. Its possible English equivalent is character.

common feature.def: A character shared by different entities of the same bearing e.g. walking on foot, a common trait of humans and cattle. Its possible English equivalent is common feature.

substance.def: An entity which has a permanent existence and is able to retain itself against all odds. Its possible English equivalent is substance.

judicial contest.def: equivalent is plea. A statement which may be confirmed or rejected; for instance, Zayd (name of a person), is standing. Its possible English equivalent is judicial contest. Branding a group by a title on the basis of

exemplification.def: equivalent is inductive reasoning. Labeling or categorization of something on the basis of the specific trait. Its possible English equivalent is exemplification.

medicine.def: The science by which we learn the various states of the human body, in health and when not in health and the means by which health is likely to be lost, and when lost, is likely to be restored. Its possible English equiv

material causes.def: The first cause to create a being. In the human beings it includes elements, humours, spirits. Its possible English equivalent is material causes.

formal causes.def: The causes which include the constitutions and compositions of the matter. Its possible English equivalent is formal causes.

efficient causes.def: The essential and non-essential factors which bring about a change or maintain the states of the body. Its possible English equivalent is efficient causes.

final causes.def: The causes that are responsible for the actions and functions of the body. Its possible English equivalent is final causes.

moisture/wetness.def: One of the four physical properties naturally associated with matter. Its possible English equivalent is moisture/wetness.

the single element.def: view that all the things in this world were made of only one element. Some called it water and some named it earth, fire, air etc. Its possible English equivalent is the single element.

four elements.def: The four primary substances found in nature i.e. fire, air, water and earth. Each of them is ever lasting but could be mixed in different proportions. Its possible English equivalent is four elements.

firy elements.def: The substances bearing the natural properties of heat and dryness that perform the activities like fire. Its possible English equivalent is firy elements.

watery elements.def: The substances bearing the natural properties of cold and moistness that perform the activities like water. Its possible English equivalent is watery elements.

airy elements.def: These are the substances bearing the n a tu ra l p ro pe rt i e s o f h e a t a n d m o i s t n e s s th a t p e r fo rm t h e a c ti v i ti e s l ik e a i r . I ts possible English equivalent is airy elements.

earthy elements.def: The substances bearing the natural properties of cold and dryness that perform the activities like earth. Its possible English equivalent is earthy elements.

abundant elements.def: A group of philosophers headed by Anaxagoras of Ionia (460 BC) put forth the concept of unlimited number of elements. According to them primary elements are plenty and may not be counted. Its possible English equivalent

simple intermixture.def: more elements or components are mixed in a manner that their previous properties remain unchanged. Its possible English equivalent is simple intermixture.

compound.def: A type of intermixture in which two or more elements or components are mixed in a manner that their previous qualities change. Its possible English equivalent is compound.

moderate temperament.def: and quantities of all the participating elements in a compound are in accordance with what that compound is made for. Its possible English equivalent is moderate temperament.

steady intemperament.def: An abnormal temperament which continues in a steady mode. Its possible English equivalent is steady intemperament.

abrupt intemperament.def: An abnormal temperament which occurs abruptly and in contrast to the normal temperament. Its possible English equivalent is abrupt intemperament.

persistent intemperament.def: An abnormal temperament which persists in an everlasting manner. Its possible English equivalent is persistent intemperament.

morbid heat.def: When the innate heat exceeds the normal limit disturbing all the body functions. Its possible English equivalent is morbid heat.

hypothermia.def: The abnormal deficit of the heat of body which disturbs its normal functioning. Its possible English equivalent is hypothermia.

regional temperament/ habitable temperam.def: The temperament based on climatic conditions of a region and recognized fit or unfit for the inhabitants. Its possible English equivalent is regional temperament/ habitable temperament.

infancy.def: The age which comes under and extends from birth to four years of age. Its possible English equivalent is infancy.

childhood.def: Sinn-i-Numu The age which comes under and extends from four years of age till nine years i.e. onset of the permanent teeth. Its possible English equivalent is childhood.

last stage of growth.def: extends from twenty five to thirty five years of age. After this no growth of the body is possible. Its possible English equivalent is last stage of growth.

temperament of sexes.def: The term used to explain the difference between masculine and feminine genders. Its possible English equivalent is temperament of sexes.

ten features/indicators of temperament.def: possible English equivalent is ten features/indicators of temperament. Ajnas Same as ‘Ashara. Fasl-i-Rabi‘, Fasl-i- The four seasons i.e.

spring season.def: One of the four seasons of a year with cold and wet climatic temperament. This is suitable for human health. In India it usually spans from January to March period. Its possible English equivalent is spring season.

autumn season.def: One of the four seasons of a year with hot and wet climatic temperament. This is not suitable for human health. In India it usually spans from July to September period. Its possible English equivalent is autumn season.

summer season.def: One of the four seasons of a year with hot and dry climatic temperament. This is not suitable for human health in extreme conditions. In India it usually spans from April to June period. Its possible English equivalent i

winter season.def: One of the four seasons of a year with cold and dry climatic temperament. This is not suitable for human health in extreme conditions. In India it usually spans from October to December period. Its possible English equiv

moist organs.def: The organs which are moist in nature. They are very soft with relatively high amount of water. The most wet organs are membranes, glands, nerves etc. Its possible English equivalent is moist organs.

humour.def: equivalent is humours. Akhlat. The singular of Its possible English equivalent is humour. A view postulated by the father of Medicine-Hippocrates. According to him,

intercellular fluid/protoplasm.def: theory. The moist part of an organ, essential to bind it together. When this moisture is lost, the particles of the organs are disintegrated. Its possible English equivalent is intercellular fluid/protoplasm.

four digestions.def: The four different types of digestion i.e. Hadm Mi‘di, Hadm Kabidi, Hadm ‘Uruqi, Hadm ‘Udwi which the food particles have to go through for the purpose of assimilation in the body. Its possible English equivalent is four

digestion and metabolism.def: The term used for the process of digestion and transformation of the food particles to become the parts of the body. Its possible English equivalent is digestion and metabolism.

humoral residencies.def: The places where the humours reside or stay in the body chiefly blood vessels. Its possible English equivalent is humoral residencies.

catabolism&anabolism.def: The process of replacement of the fluids and energy that are consumed in the functioning and growth of the body with the new ones. Its possible English equivalent is catabolism&anabolism.

sanguineous fluid.def: The liquid part of sanguine humour which keeps it in flow. Its possible English equivalent is sanguineous fluid.

blood clotting.def: Coagulation of sanguine humour in a jelly like substance. Its possible English equivalent is blood clotting.

gallic phlegm.def: One of the abnormal types of phlegm which is gallic in taste. It is more inclined towards heat and dryness. Its possible English equivalent is gallic phlegm.

serous phlegm.def: An abnormal type of phlegm which has watery consistency and contains more fluid. Its possible English equivalent is serous phlegm.

limy phlegm.def: An abnormal type of phlegm which has extremely thick consistency. Its possible English equivalent is limy phlegm.

mucoid phlegm/raw phlegm.def: An abnormal type of phlegm which has mucoid consistency. Its possible English equivalent is mucoid phlegm/raw phlegm.

pathogenic organisms.def: abnormally found in the body and responsible for infection. These organisms grow better in phlegm. Its possible English equivalent is pathogenic organisms.

vitelline bile.def: An abnormal type of bile which occurs due to the mixing of thick phlegm in it. Its possible English equivalent is vitelline bile.

serous bile.def: An abnormal type of bile which occurs due to the mixing of thin phlegm in it. Its possible English equivalent is serous bile.

burnt bile.def: An abnormal type of bile which is produced either by the mixing of burnt melancholic humour in it or due to its own burning. Its possible English equivalent is burnt bile.

shallot bile/oxidized bile.def: An abnormal type of bile which is produced by the oxidation of bile itself and its colour changes to green or shallot coloured. Its possible English equivalent is shallot bile/oxidized bile.

erogenous bile.def: An abnormal type of bile which is produced by the extreme oxidation of bile itself and its colour changes to bluish green. Its possible English equivalent is erogenous bile.

enclosed fluid.def: fluid. A type of secondary fluid, located at the orifices of the minute channels near the tissues thus irrigating them. Its possible English equivalent is enclosed fluid.

foreign humour.def: The moistness of an organ which does not remain in equilibrium, qualitatively and quantitatively, in accordance to the temperament of that organ. Its possible English equivalent is foreign humour.

putrid humour/septic humour.def: An abnormal humour which is prone to putrefy. Its possible English equivalent is putrid humour/septic humour. The ailments which arise due to the

sanguineous ailments.def: abnormal quantity or quality of the sanguine humour. These ailments are considered to be of hot and moist temperament. Its possible English equivalent is sanguineous ailments.

phlegmatic ailments.def: The ailments which arise due to the abnormal quantity or quality of the phlegmatic humour. These ailments are considered to be of cold and moist temperament. Its possible English equivalent is phlegmatic ailments.

bilious ailments.def: The ailments which arise due to the abnormal quantity or quality of the bilious humour. These ailments are considered to be of hot and dry temperament. Its possible English equivalent is bilious ailments.

melancholic ailments.def: The ailments which arise due to the abnormal quantity or quality of the melancholic humour. These ailments are considered to be of cold and moist temperament. Its possible English equivalent is melancholic ailments.

organs.def: The solid parts of the body which are derived primarily from the coarse humours. These are the essential tools to accomplish vivid functions. Its possible English equivalent is organs.

compound organ type i.def: The organs which are composed of two or more simple organs e.g. muscle. Its possible English equivalent is compound organ type I.

compound organ type ii.def: The organs which are composed of simple organs and other matter linked to air for the function e.g. ear has air in its middle part, necessary for its function. Its possible English equivalent is compound organ type II.

compound organ type iii.def: The organs which are composed of simple organs and other matter linked to water for the function e.g. eye has fluids, necessary for its function. Its possible English equivalent is compound organ type III.

compound organ type iv.def: The organs which are composed of two or more compound organs e.g. the head which is composed of many compound organs i.e. ear, eyes, nose etc. Its possible English equivalent is compound organ type IV.

membrane.def: possible English equivalent is membrane. Ghisha’. The plural of

artery.def: One of the simple organs as listed by Avicenna. Its possible English equivalent is artery.

vein.def: One of the simple organs as listed by Avicenna. Its possible English equivalent is vein.

donor organ.def: The organ that provides power to other organ. Its possible English equivalent is donor organ.

recipient organ.def: The organ that receives power from other organ. Its possible English equivalent is recipient organ.

pre serving organ.def: The helping organ which acts prior to the action of the principal organ for the completion of a particular function. Its possible English equivalent is pre serving organ.

post serving organ.def: The organ which serves after the act of the principal organ for fulfilling the needs of the function. Its possible English equivalent is post serving organ.

principal organs.def: The superior organs which are served by other organs for their functioning. Its possible English equivalent is principal organs.

gonads.def: One of the vital organs. It is the seat of reproductive or generating faculties. Its possible English equivalent is gonads. The organs which execute the functions of

vital organs.def: the vital faculties. Heart is the chief organ while other organs e.g. arteries, veins, capillaries etc come under this heading. Its possible English equivalent is vital organs.

organs of nervous system.def: The organs which execute the functions of the mental faculties. Brain is the chief organ while other organs e.g. spinal cord, cranial nerves, spinal nerves etc come under this heading. Its possible English equivalent is

digestive organs.def: The organs which execute the functions of the nutritive faculties. Liver is the chief organ while other organs e.g. oesophagus, stomach, gall bladder etc come under this heading. Its possible English equivalent is digest

weak organ.def: An organ that becomes weak due to unhealthy causes. Its possible English equivalent is weak organ.`

glandular organs.def: secretion. They release their secretions in two ways i.e., exocrine and endocrine. Its possible English equivalent is glandular organs.

natural pneuma.def: Ruh The (pneuma) which is found in the organs of natural faculty and its centre is liver. Its possible English equivalent is natural pneuma.

natural faculties.def: is furnished in an individual for digestion and assimilation of food and evacuation of waste from the body. Its possible English equivalent is natural faculties.

psychic power/mental faculties.def: The power which is furnished in an individual for the intellectual, sensory and motor function of the body. Its possible English equivalent is psychic power/mental faculties.

vital power.def: The power which is furnished in an individual to receive other powers essential for the survival. Its possible English equivalent is vital power.

collective faculty.def: The power or faculty of the body which selects and collects the matter for digestion. Its possible English equivalent is collective faculty.

power of resemblance.def: Ghadhiya which transforms the matter Mulassiqa attached by Quwwat with the organ in such a way that it resembles the nourished organ in all respects and becomes a part of that organ. Its possible English equivalent is po

expulsive faculty.def: One of the sub serving powers of Quwwat Ghadhiya which excretes wastes formed after the process of digestion. Its possible English equivalent is expulsive faculty.

implantation.def: equivalent is homologous compound. It is the process of implantation of fertilized material in the uterus. Its possible English equivalent is implantation.

foetus formation.def: The process of transformation of conceived material into foetus. Its possible English equivalent is foetus formation.

intellectual faculties.def: The main powers of the brain which analyze the information perceived by sensory powers. Its possible English equivalent is intellectual faculties.

power of vision.def: One of the sub serving powers of motor faculty. Its function is the perception of light and image. Its possible English equivalent is power of vision.

power of hearing.def: One of the sub serving powers of motor faculty. Its function is the perception of sounds. Its possible English equivalent is power of hearing.

power of smell.def: One of the sub serving powers of motor faculty. Its function is the perception of smell. Its possible English equivalent is power of smell.

power of touch.def: One of the sub serving powers of motor faculty. Its function is the perception of touch. Its possible English equivalent is power of touch.

eye.def: The organ which perceives light and images. It works for one of the sensory powers i.e. power of vision. Its possible English equivalent is eye.

ear.def: The organ which perceives sounds. It works for one of the sensory powers i.e. power of hearing. Its possible English equivalent is ear.

nose.def: The organ which perceives smell. It works for one of the sensory powers i.e. power of smell. Its possible English equivalent is nose.

composite sense/common sense.def: Perception power which receives all sensations, composes them into percepts and enables proper sensory appreciations Its possible English equivalent is composite sense/common sense.

faculty of awareness.def: One of the intellectual faculties which preserve the knowledge perceived by Hiss Mushtarak. It is that sense with which new sensory experiences of same or similar types can be compared. Its possible English equivalent is

faculty of apprehension.def: One of the intellectual faculties which decodes the meanings of those particular Hiss forms perceived by Mushtarak. It decides what is in favour of the individual and what is against. Its possible English equivalent is f

faculty of modification.def: various sensory information in various ways and gives new dimensions to the preserved knowledge. Its possible English equivalent is faculty of modification.

cognitive faculty.def: is power of speech. The power of thinking or intellect which is exclusively found in human beings. Its possible English equivalent is cognitive faculty.

natural functions.def: The functions which are essential to energize all the powers and develop the organs. These Quwa Tabi‘iyya are performed by and A‘da’ Tabi‘iyya. executed by Its possible English equivalent is natural functions.

intellect function.def: The function which is performed by the intellectual faculty and works according to intellect level and differs from person to person. Its possible English equivalent is intellect function.

act of imagination.def: The function which is performed when intellectual powers like Quwwat Mutasarrifa gives new dimensions to the perceived sensations. Its possible English equivalent is act of imagination.

act of thinking.def: When intellectual powers like Quwwat Wahima and Quwwat Mufakkira establish the meaning of the perceived sensations, that exercise is known as Fi‘l-i-Fikr. Its possible English equivalent is act of thinking.

act of preservation.def: When intellectual powers like Quwwat Hafiza preserves the knowledge Wahima, forwarded by Quwwat this act of Fi‘l-i-Hifz. preservation is termed as Its possible English equivalent is act of preservation.

act of assimilation.def: The function which is performed by the digestive faculty which digests and transforms the food to become a part of the body. Its possible English equivalent is act of assimilation.

act of growth.def: The function of nourishment and growth Quwa of the body performed by Tabi‘iyya. Its possible English equivalent is act of growth.

act of reproduction.def: The function of reproduction performed Quwa Tanasuliyya, by for the continuation of the species. Its possible English equivalent is act of reproduction.

act of desire.def: The function of mind awakening to fulfill the needs of the body under the influence Shahwaniyya. of Quwwat Its possible English equivalent is act of desire.

act of absorption.def: The process of absorption of food Jadhiba. controlled by Quwwat Its possible English equivalent is act of absorption.

act of retention.def: The function of retaining the food at all sites of digestion controlled by Quwwat Masika. Its possible English equivalent is act of retention.

act of digestion.def: The function of digestion and transformation of food controlled by Hadima. Quwwat Its possible English equivalent is act of digestion.

act of expulsion.def: The function or act of expulsion of undigested and waste matter from the Dafi‘a. body controlled by Quwwat Its possible English equivalent is act of expulsion.

vapours.def: due to extra retention of the matter leading to abnormal state. Their impact may be on organs to cause disease. Its possible English equivalent is vapours.

potential matter or active substance.def: The matter which has a potentiality to transform from one form to another. Its possible English equivalent is Potential matter or active substance.

optic pneuma.def: Ruh Nafsaniyya A kind of which makes the organ able to perceive, perceptions of light and images. Its possible English equivalent is optic pneuma.

flatus.def: word air/wind. Its possible English equivalent is flatus. Colostrum The secretion of sweat glands. Watery in

red blood corpuscles.def: possible English equivalent is red blood corpuscles. Agglutination

thyroid hormones.def: essential for normal metabolic processes, mental and physical developments. Its possible English equivalent is thyroid hormones.

testosterone.def: is hormones secreted by thymus gland. Hormones secreted by testis and adrenal cortex regulate the male secondary sexual activities. Its possible English equivalent is testosterone. The hormones secreted from Adrenal
The hormone derived from testis and responsible for the development of secondary male characters. Its possible English equivalent is testosterone.

body fluids.def: The fluids in the body which are found in tissues, intracellular spaces, cavities, lymphatic and blood vessels. Its possible English equivalent is body fluids.

phosphoproteins.def: A variety of conjugate proteins having protein molecules with phosphoric acid. Its possible English equivalent is phosphoproteins.

glycoproteins.def: A group of compounds consisting of a protein combined with a carbohydrate (in quantity ≤ 4 %). Its possible English equivalent is glycoproteins.

conjugate proteins.def: A group of proteins whose molecules are combined with non proteinous matter. Its possible English equivalent is conjugate proteins.

collagen.def: Lahmiyyat Sulbiyya A variety of (Scleroprotein) found in bones and cartilages etc. Its possible English equivalent is collagen.

keratin.def: Lahmiyyat Sulbiyya A variety of (Scleroprotein) found in hair, nails, claws and horns. Its possible English equivalent is keratin.

scleroproteins.def: A group of proteins noted for their insolubility. It is found in skeletal tissue, cartilage, hair, nails, claws and horns. Its possible English equivalent is scleroproteins.

mucoproteins.def: A variety of conjugate proteins in which globulins are combined with carbohydrate group. Its possible English equivalent is mucoproteins.

protean.def: Proteins consisted of globulins but are insoluble in light salty medium. Its possible English equivalent is protean.

metalloproteins.def: A variety of proteins containing metals like Iron, copper, magnesium and cobalt etc. Its possible English equivalent is metalloproteins.

mucoid.def: A variety of glycoproteins, obtained from tendons, bones, cartilages and egg albumin. Its possible English equivalent is mucoid.

mucin.def: A mixture of glycoproteins found in or secreted by mucous membrane and mucus glands. Its possible English equivalent is mucin.

lipoproteins.def: A group of proteins found in blood plasma, lymph, cell membrane and egg yolk are combined with fats and other lipids. Its possible English equivalent is lipoproteins.

prostatic fluid.def: An alkaline fluid secreted from prostate gland that forms part of the semen. Its possible English equivalent is prostatic fluid.

derived proteins.def: A variety of proteins not found in natural form but derived by their hydrolysis. Its possible English equivalent is derived proteins.

succus entericus.def: The mild acid or alkaline fluid of the glands of small intestines which contains digestive enzymes. Its possible English equivalent is succus entericus.

gastric juice.def: The liquid, secreted by the gastric glands of the stomach, containing several enzymes and hydrochloric acid. Its possible English equivalent is gastric juice.

intercellular fluid.def: pineal gland. The fluid which is found in between cells i.e. intercellular spaces. Its possible English equivalent is intercellular fluid.

synovial fluid.def: The thick lubricating fluid that surrounds a joint, secreted by synovial membrane. Its possible English equivalent is synovial fluid. Secretions from a small endocrine gland

pituitary hormones.def: attached to hypothalamus. These include several hormones like ACTH, Thyroid stimulating hormone, lactogenic hormones, melanocyte stimulating hormones etc. Its possible English equivalent is pituitary hormones.

blood groups.def: possible English equivalent is blood groups. If the Rh factor is present in blood, it is called Rhezus Positive. Its possible English equivalent is rhesus positive. People who lack the Rh factor are termed

rhezus factor.def: negative. The antigens that may or may not be present in blood (R.B.Cs), form the basis of Rhesus blood group. These antigens produce the corresponding antibody in the serum. Its possible English equivalent is rhezus fac

bilirubin.def: A pigment largely derived from the breakdown of haemoglobin from red blood cells. Its possible English equivalent is bilirubin.

sucrose.def: juice. A disaccharide present in many plants like cane, beet etc. Its possible English equivalent is sucrose.

carbohydrates.def: A compound that contains carbon, hydrogen and oxygen, particularly present in plants and also in animals. Its possible English equivalent is carbohydrates.

dextrin.def: mucopolysaccharides. A soluble polysaccharide formed during the hydrolysis of starch. Its possible English equivalent is dextrin.

glycogen.def: Animal starch that is stored in liver and muscles and is soluble in water. Its possible English equivalent is glycogen.

glycolipids.def: present in the brain. In Gaucher’s disease, this lipid is found to occur in liver and spleen. Its possible English equivalent is glycolipids.

phospholipids.def: The lipids which contain Phosphoric Acid and nitrogenous base in the molecule. Its possible English equivalent is phospholipids.

gangliosides.def: A type of Shahmiyyat Murakkaba (compound lipids). These are present in nerve cells, spleen and red blood cells. Its possible English equivalent is gangliosides.

vascular fluids.def: sulphurolipids. The fluids which are present in vessels blood or lymph. Its possible English equivalent is vascular fluids.

essential amino acids.def: The amino acids that are not synthesized in the body but obtained from diet and essential for normal physiological functions and growth. Its possible English equivalent is essential amino acids.

lipids.def: A group of naturally occurring compounds, insoluble in water, but soluble in chloroform or alcohol. Its possible English equivalent is lipids.

polysaccharides.def: Carbohydrates containing a large number of monosaccharides, soluble in water but generally not sweet in taste. Its possible English equivalent is polysaccharides.

lecithin.def: A group of phospholipids which is present in egg-yolk. It is an important constituent of cell membrane and is involved in fat metabolism. Its possible English equivalent is lecithin.

compound lipids.def: non-fatty substance. It includes lecithin, Cephalin, sphingomyelin and Plasmogen etc. Its possible English equivalent is compound lipids.

triglycerides.def: A lipid or neutral fat consisting of glycerol combined with three or five different fatty acid molecules. These are synthesized from the product of digestion of dietary fat. They are the form in which fat is stored in th

simple lipids.def: Simple lipids are compound of true fats and wax. These are esters of fatty acids and glycerol. Its possible English equivalent is simple lipids.

simple proteins.def: Simple proteins are pure proteins, uncombined with anything else. These are classified into many types and are found in milk, muscles, eggs, maize, barley, bone and cartilage etc. Its possible English equivalent is simpl

proteins.def: Highly complex nitrogenous compounds found in all animal/ vegetable tissues. They are built up of amino acids and are essential for growth and repair of body tissues. They are equally important to regulate functions as e

visual purple/ rhodopsin.def: equivalent is respiration. The visual purple contained in retinal rods. Its colour is preserved in darkness, bleached by day light. Its possible English equivalent is visual purple/ rhodopsin.

urobilinogen.def: A pigment formed from bilirubin in intestine by action of bacteria. It may be reabsorbed and returned to the liver or excreted in the urine. Its possible English equivalent is urobilinogen.

stercobilin.def: A brown pigment formed during the metabolism of bile pigments, which are derived from hemoglobin. Its possible English equivalent is stercobilin.

biliverdin.def: The green pigment of bile formed by oxidation of bilirubin. Its possible English equivalent is biliverdin.

urobilin.def: A pigment present normally in a minute amount in the urine, derived from the Bawli oxidation of Muwallid-i-Lawn (urobilinogen). Its possible English equivalent is urobilin.

haem.def: A pigment present in RBCs comprising 4% of haemoglobin and gives red color to the blood. Its possible English equivalent is haem. A substance contained within the RBCs

alkalinity of blood.def: equivalent is haemoglobin. It is due to vomiting, excessive use of alkalis and asphyxia. Its possible English equivalent is alkalinity of blood.

acidosis.def: The condition in which blood pH is low due to excessive loss of alkalis or retention of acids. Its possible English equivalent is acidosis.

interstitial pressure.def: The pressure relating to spaces or interstices in any structure. It also influences regulation of rate of lymph flow. Its possible English equivalent is interstitial pressure.

anoxia.def: The decreased amount of oxygen in organs and tissues. This may result from hemorrhage or anemia of various types. Its possible English equivalent is anoxia.

albumin.def: A variety of protein found in egg which coagulates on heating. Its possible English equivalent is albumin.
A variety of simple proteins found in animal and vegetable matter. It is soluble in water and coagulates on heating. Its possible English equivalent is albumin.

globulin.def: A v a ri e t y o f si m p l e p r o t e i n , c o a g ul a t e s o n he a t in g a n d i n s ol u b l e i n d i s t i ll e d w a t e r . It s possible English equivalent is globulin.

lipochrome.def: A fat soluble pigment which gives colour to fatty material. Its possible English equivalent is lipochrome.

buffer.def: A solution whose hydrogen ion concentration (pH) remains unchanged by dilution or by addition of acid or alkali. Its possible English equivalent is buffer.

urea.def: The main breakdown product of protein metabolism. It is excreted in the urine as the main constituent. Its possible English equivalent is urea.

uric acid.def: An acid formed in the breakdown of nucleoproteins in the tissues and excreted in the urine. Its possible English equivalent is uric acid.

resorption.def: equivalent is lacrimal secretion. Loss of substance through physiological or pathological means. Its possible English equivalent is resorption.

intra pleural pressure.def: The pressure in pleural cavity, that is negative normally and amounts to 2.5 mm Hg. Its possible English equivalent is intra pleural pressure.

nucleoproteins.def: A compound consisting of nucleic acid and protein tightly bound together. Its possible English equivalent is nucleoproteins.

thrombocytes /blood platelets.def: A circular or oval disk shaped structure, present in the blood. It plays a role in blood coagulation, contraction of clot, thereby in homeostasis and thrombosis. Its possible English equivalent is thrombocytes /blood pla

pentose.def: corpuscles. A monosaccharide containing five carbon molecules. Its possible English equivalent is pentose.

lipolysis.def: The process by which lipids particularly triglycerides are broken down into fatty acids and cholesterol in the body by enzyme lipase. Its possible English equivalent is lipolysis.

acetylcholine.def: A chemical substance released at nerve endings and acts as neurotransmitter. Its possible English equivalent is acetylcholine.

villikinin.def: A hormone present in mucous membrane of small intestine stimulates the movements of villi. Its possible English equivalent is villikinin.

prostaglandins.def: A group of hormone like substances present in body fluids and tissues. It has many physiological functions. Its possible English equivalent is prostaglandins.

glucagon.def: A hormone mainly secreted from endocrine pancreas raises blood sugar level. Its possible English equivalent is glucagon.
A hormone produced by Islets of pancreas, which causes an increase in blood sugar level. Its possible English equivalent is glucagon.

cholecystokinin.def: A hormone from small intestine that causes contraction of gall bladder and expulsion of bile into intestine and thus helps in digestion. Its possible English equivalent is cholecystokinin.

hyperglycaemia.def: An excess of glucose in blood stream. Its possible English equivalent is hyperglycaemia. The precocious sexual development and

adrenogenital syndrome.def: apparent masculinisation in girls, caused by overproduction of hormones by adrenal cortex in infancy. Its possible English equivalent is adrenogenital syndrome.

cushing’s syndrome.def: A disorder caused due to excess amount of corticosteroid hormones in the body resulting into weight gain, red face, excess growth of body and hair etc. Its possible English equivalent is Cushing’s syndrome.

progesterone.def: A steroid hormone secreted by corpus luteum of ovary, placenta and in small amount by adrenal cortex. It prepares uterus for pregnancy. Its possible English equivalent is progesterone.

oestrogen.def: The hormone that controls female sexual development and female secondary sex characters, synthesized in ovary and in small amount by adrenal cortex. Its possible English equivalent is oestrogen.

relaxation.def: In the context of physiology, it is the diminution of tension in a muscle. Its possible English equivalent is relaxation.

contraction.def: In the context of physiology, it is the shortening of muscle in response to stimulus. Its possible English equivalent is contraction.

mineralocorticoids.def: The hormones released by adrenal cortex and necessary for regulation of salt and water balance. Its possible English equivalent is mineralocorticoids.

gastrin.def: equivalent is glucocorticoids. A hormone produced in the mucous membrane of stomach. It stimulates the gastric juice secretion. Its possible English equivalent is gastrin.

secretin.def: A hormone secreted from small intestine stimulates the secretion from pancreas. Its possible English equivalent is secretin.

ovum.def: The mature female cell released from ovary having half of the chromosomes. It is fertilized by sperm. Its possible English equivalent is ovum.

ovoplasm.def: The fluid inside the unfertilized ovum. The nucleus and germinal vesicle are found in it. Its possible English equivalent is ovoplasm.

corpus luteum.def: equivalent is pituitary gland. The yellow endocrine body formed in the ovary at the site of a ruptured ovarian follicle. Its possible English equivalent is corpus luteum.

sex hormones.def: The hormones produced by testis or ovaries are responsible for development of sex organs and their functions. Its possible English equivalent is sex hormones.

placenta.def: A vascular structure developed in uterus during the third month of pregnancy by means of which the embryo is attached to the wall of uterus. Its possible English equivalent is placenta.

growth hormone/somatotrophic hormone (st.def: The hormone secreted by anterior lobe of Pituitary gland which stimulates body growth. Its possible English equivalent is growth hormone/somatotrophic hormone (STH).

erythrocyte sedimentation rate.def: The rate at which erythrocytes settle out of suspension in blood plasma. Its possible English equivalent is erythrocyte sedimentation rate.

eosinophils.def: English equivalent is granulocytes. A v a r i ety o f w h it e b l oo d ce l ls t h a t c o n ta i n co a r s e g r a n u l es i n it s c y t o p l a s m . I t s possible English equivalent is eosinophils.

dwarfism.def: The abnormally short stature due to deficiency of thyroid hormone. Its possible English equivalent is dwarfism.

haemolysin.def: A substance that may be antibody or bacterial toxin, capable of bringing about destruction of Red Blood Cells. Its possible English equivalent is haemolysin.

oxytocin hormone.def: A hormone, secreted by neurohypophysis, causes contraction of uterus during labour and stimulates milk secretion from breasts. Its possible English equivalent is oxytocin hormone.

placental gonadotrophins.def: A hormone produced by placenta during pregnancy and it maintains the secretion of progesterone by corpus luteum. Its possible English equivalent is placental gonadotrophins.

interstitial cells.def: The cells interspersed between the seminiferous tubules of testis. These cells secrete androgens. Its possible English equivalent is interstitial cells.

spermine.def: hormone. A nitrogenous base found in seminal fluid, secreted by prostate gland. Its possible English equivalent is spermine. A smooth transparent membrane that lines

serous membrane.def: peritoneum, pleura and pericardium. Its inner surface is moistened by a fluid that allows frictionless movement of organs within their cavities. Its possible English equivalent is serous membrane.

mucous membrane.def: The moist membrane that lines the respiratory tract, gastrointestinal tract, biliary and pancreatic systems. Its possible English equivalent is mucous membrane.

glandular epithelium.def: A type of simple epithelium found in alveoli and ducts of glands. Its function is manufacturing new substances and passing them out into their respective secretions. Its possible English equivalent is glandular epitheliu

ciliated epithelium.def: The type of epithelial tissue that has hair like processes on cells. It is found in respiratory passages, fallopian tubes and testes. Its possible English equivalent is ciliated epithelium.

columnar epithelium.def: The type of epithelial tissue that has elongated cells found in stomach, intestine, alveoli and ducts of many glands. Its possible English equivalent is columnar epithelium.

cubical epithelium.def: The type of epithelial tissue that is composed of cubical cells found in small terminal, respiratory bronchioles, salivary glands, thyroid and covering of ovary. Its possible English equivalent is cubical epithelium.

compound epithelium.def: epithelium. The type of epithelial tissue which has more than one layer of cells. Its possible English equivalent is compound epithelium.

muscular tissue.def: produce movement by its force of contraction and relaxation. It also maintains the position of the body. Its possible English equivalent is muscular tissue.

connective tissue.def: The tissue that supports binds or separates specialized tissues and organs and functions as packing of the body. Its possible English equivalent is connective tissue.

epithelial tissue.def: The tissue that forms a limiting and a lining membrane, and covers the free surfaces. Its possible English equivalent is epithelial tissue.

adipose tissue.def: The fibrous connective tissue packed with fat cells. It forms a thick layer under the skin around the kidneys and serves as an insulating layer and an energy store. Its possible English equivalent is adipose tissue.

antibodies.def: A special kind of blood protein produced as a reaction to an antigen. It is synthesized in lymphoid tissue and circulates in plasma to attack antigen. Its possible English equivalent is antibodies.

diffusion.def: The process whereby gases and liquids of different densities intermingle when brought into contact until the density becomes equal throughout. Its possible English equivalent is diffusion.

white fibrous tissue.def: They are thin and non branching tissues. This tissue is present in tendon, ligament, articular capsule and deep fasciae of the body. Its possible English equivalent is white fibrous tissue.

alveolar tissue.def: The loose connective tissue consisting of a m e s h w o r k o f e l a s t ic t is s u e, c o ll a g e n a n d re tic u l a r f i b r e s . I t b in d s th e s k i n to t h e muscles. Its possible English equivalent is al

myeloid tissue.def: A tissue in the bone marrow that produces various blood cells erythrocytes, granular leucocytes and platelets. It has also phagocytic action. Its possible English equivalent is myeloid tissue.

simple tubular gland.def: The glands found in mucous membrane of small intestine. These glands secrete intestinal juice through the openings between villi. Its possible English equivalent is simple tubular gland.

pineal gland.def: gland. A small reddish grey conical structure on the dorsal surface of midbrains. It secretes a hormone like substance melatonin. It becomes calcified as age advances. Its possible English equivalent is pineal gland.

gut glucagon.def: A hormone produced in small intestine as a result of digestion of glucose. It stimulates islets of Langerhans in pancreas to secrete insulin. Its possible English equivalent is gut glucagon.

bile salts.def: The salts present in bile which are necessary for emulsification of fats. Its possible English equivalent is bile salts.

fatty acid.def: Fatty acids are fundamental constituents of many lipids including triglycerides. Some of them are synthesized in the body and some are obtained from diet. Its possible English equivalent is fatty acid.

relaxin.def: A hormone, secreted by ovaries in earlier stage of pregnancy that dilates the cervix and prepares the uterus for the action of oxytocin during labour. Its possible English equivalent is relaxin.

amniotic fluid.def: The fluid, contained within the amniotic cavity that surrounds the foetus and protects it from external pressure. Its possible English equivalent is amniotic fluid.

nor adrenaline.def: causes an increase in blood pressure, increases blood flow through coronary arteries, slows heart rate and relaxes intestinal muscles. Its possible English equivalent is nor adrenaline.

bile.def: A thick yellowish brown or green fluid secreted by liver and stored in gall bladder. It helps in fat digestion and stimulates the peristalsis in duodenum. Its possible English equivalent is bile.

melanocytes.def: The cells within the epidermis of skin that produce the dark brown pigment. Its possible English equivalent is melanocytes.
