mitochondrion.def: A structure occurring in cytoplasm of cell and the site of cell’s energy production. It contains enzymes involved in metabolic activities. Its possible English equivalent is mitochondrion.

golgi apparatus.def: A collection of vesicles and folded membranes in a cell which control the synthetic process of cell i.e., secretions. Its possible English equivalent is Golgi apparatus.

phagocytosis.def: In the context of physiology it refers to the engulfment and digestion of bacteria and foreign particles by phagocytes. Its possible English equivalent is phagocytosis.

epithelial cells.def: The cells forming the epithelial tissue cover the external surface of the body and line hollow structures. Its possible English equivalent is epithelial cells.

plasma cells.def: The large oval cells with non granular basophilic cytoplasm These cells have abundant granular endoplasmic reticulum. Its possible English equivalent is plasma cells.

antigens.def: Any substance that is regarded as foreign particle by the body and the body produces antibody against it. Its possible English equivalent is antigens.

blood corpuscles.def: The cells of blood may be classified into Red cells and white cells and blood platelets. White cells include granulocytes, lymphocytes, monocytes and blood platelets. Its possible English equivalent is blood corpuscles.

heparin.def: An anticoagulant produced in liver cells and white blood cells, which acts by inhibiting the action of thrombin during blood coagulation. Its possible English equivalent is heparin.

neutrophils.def: A variety of white blood cells which have bilobed nucleus and their main function is to provide defence against infection. Its possible English equivalent is neutrophils.

basophils.def: A variety of white blood cells having coarse granules in its cytoplasm stained with basophilic dyes. Their main function is ingestion of foreign particles. Its possible English equivalent is basophils.

lymphocytes.def: A variety of white blood cells also found in spleen, lymph nodes and bone marrow, and involved in immunity. Its possible English equivalent is lymphocytes.

leucocytosis.def: English equivalent is thrombin. Increased number of leucocytes in the blood, often in response to infection. Its possible English equivalent is leucocytosis.

osteoblast.def: A type of cells originating in the mesoderm of embryo and is responsible for formation of bone. Its possible English equivalent is osteoblast.

osteocytes.def: It is called as bone cell. The osteoblast that has ceased activity and embedded in bone matrix. Its possible English equivalent is osteocytes.

osteoclast.def: The large multinucleated cell that breaks down, dissolves or removes the calcified bone matrix. These cells are found on the bone surface. Its possible English equivalent is osteoclast.

neuroglia.def: The supportive structure of nervous tissue composed of different cells. It has various supportive and nutritive functions and makes up a greater portion of total volume of brain and spinal cord. Its possible English equi

cardiac muscle.def: The specialized muscle that compose the heart wall. It is involuntary in nature and innervated by autonomic nerves. Its possible English equivalent is cardiac muscle.

neuron.def: The morphological unit of nervous system. It is the cell specialized to transmit the nerve impulses, so in this way it caries the information from one part to another. Its possible English equivalent is neuron.

synapse.def: The place where a nerve impulse is transmitted from one neuron to another at the end of nerve fibre. Its possible English equivalent is synapse.

nerve endings.def: The terminal part of one of the branches of a nerve fibre where a neuron contacts either with another neuron or with a muscle or gland cell. Its possible English equivalent is nerve endings.

medullary sheath.def: The white covering of a nerve fibre, composed of layers of mixed lipids and neurokeratogenic protein. This sheath insulates the nerve fibre. Its possible English equivalent is medullary sheath.

osteon/ haversian system.def: The structural and functional unit of the bone. The longitudinal canals are found in it, forming a central tube. Its possible English equivalent is osteon/ haversian system.

endosteum.def: english equivalent is periosteum. The lining membrane of marrow cavity that extends into the canal system of the compact bone. Its possible English equivalent is endosteum.

bone marrow.def: The tissue present within the internal cavities of the bone. It has osteogenic and haemopoietic functions. Its possible English equivalent is bone marrow.

metaphysis.def: The growing portion of the long bone lying between the ends and shaft. Its possible English equivalent is metaphysis.

medullated nerve fibres.def: The nerve fibre that has a sheath of myelin surrounding and insulating its axon. Its possible English equivalent is medullated nerve fibres.

non medullated nerve fibre.def: Any nerve fibre that does not have the myelin sheath, has directly invested with neurolemma. Its possible English equivalent is non medullated nerve fibre.

nerve impulse.def: The electrical activity in the membrane of a neurone, that spreads from one region to another. It is the means by which information is transmitted within the nervous system. Its possible English equivalent is nerve impul

mucinogen.def: A glycoprotein that produces mucin. These granules are found in mucous cells of salivary glands. Its possible English equivalent is mucinogen.

intrinsic factor.def: A protein secreted by gastric glands, essential for absorption of extrinsic factor vitaminB12. Its possible English equivalent is intrinsic factor.

bacteriolytic enzymes.def: The enzymes that catalyze the cell wall of certain bacteria. It is found in saliva and lacrimal secretion. Its possible English equivalent is bacteriolytic enzymes.

organelles.def: The membranous structures within the cell are cytoplasmic organelles such a plasma membrane, endoplasmic reticulum, golgi apparatus and lysosomes etc. Its possible English equivalent is organelles.

carotene.def: possible English equivalent is carotene. A group of conjugated proteins. Its possible English equivalent is flavoprotein.

pepsin.def: An enzyme found in gastric juice which initiates the digestion of proteins by splitting them into peptones. Its possible English equivalent is pepsin.

cholesterol.def: A crystalline substance of fatty nature found in brain, nervous tissues, bile, and blood. Its possible English equivalent is cholesterol.

cytochrome.def: A compound consisting of a protein linked with haem. Its possible English equivalent is cytochrome.

myoalbumin.def: A variety of simple protein found in muscles. Its possible English equivalent is myoalbumin.

serum globulin.def: A variety of simple proteins found in serum and blood plasma. Its possible English equivalent is serum globulin.

enteropeptidase.def: An enzyme secreted by the glands of intestine that converts trypsinogen into trypsin. Its possible English equivalent is enteropeptidase.

lipase.def: An enzyme produced by pancreas, also known as fat splitting enzyme, breaks down fats into glycerol and fatty acid. Its possible English equivalent is lipase.

vitelline membrane.def: A thick membrane surrounding the ovum. Its possible English equivalent is vitelline membrane.

dipeptidase.def: nucleosidase. A n en z y m e p r e s e n t in d i g e s ti v e j u i c e s t h a t co n v er t s p ro d u c t o f p r o t e in d i g e s t i o n i n t o their constituent amino acid. Its possible English equivalent is di

lactase.def: The enzyme occurring in small intestine that converts lactose into glucose and galactose. Its possible English equivalent is lactase.

sucrose invertase.def: An enzyme secreted by glands in small intestine and digests cane sugar. Its possible English equivalent is sucrose invertase.

chromosomes.def: One of the thread like structures in cell nucleus that carry hereditary characters from parent cells to daughter cells. Its possible English equivalent is chromosomes.

peptone.def: A substance produced by the action of enzyme pepsin on proteins in the first stage of protein digestion. Its possible English equivalent is peptone.

hydrochloric acid.def: It acts as antiseptic and causes hydrolysis of all food stuffs. Its possible English equivalent is hydrochloric acid. The clear fluid filling the ventricles of

iron.def: An element essential to life. It is contained in haemoglobin in red blood cells. It deficiency may lead to anaemia. Its possible English equivalent is Iron.

sebum.def: possible English equivalent is sebum. An enzyme present in saliva digests the starch and converts it into maltose. Its possible English equivalent is ptyaline.

melanin.def: Black pigment occurring in iris and choroid layer of eye, skin and hair, and synthesized in the body. Its possible English equivalent is melanin.

blood transfusion.def: Injecting blood from a healthy source into the circulation of an individual whose blood is deficient in quantity or quality. Its possible English equivalent is blood transfusion.

plasmogen.def: A kind of phospholipid which is present in brain, liver and muscle tissues. Its possible English equivalent is plasmogen. The monosaccharide that contains three

triose.def: carbon molecules. Out of these, two molecules, Glyceric Aldehyde and Dihydroxy Acetone are more important. It plays a role in sugar metabolism. Its possible English equivalent is triose.

fructose.def: A simple sugar found in honey and sweet fruits. In human body it is not found in free state and rapidly converted into glucose in liver and intestine. Its possible English equivalent is fructose.

glucose.def: The form of monosaccharide that contains six carbon atoms. This form is an important source of energy in the body. Its possible English equivalent is glucose.

lactose.def: A compound of one molecule of glucose and one of galactose, found in mammal’s milk. Its possible English equivalent is lactose.

hexose.def: A simple sugar with six carbon atoms. Hexose sugars are the sugars most frequently found in the food. Glucose,fructose,Galactose and mannose are hexoses. Its possible English equivalent is hexose.

maltose.def: Maltose is composed of two glucose molecules. It is formed from the digestion of starch and found in germinating cereal seeds. Its possible English equivalent is maltose.

histones.def: Simple proteins found in thymus gland and haemoglobin. Its possible English equivalent is histones.

saliva.def: possible English equivalent is saliva. Lu‘ab-i-Dahan. Same as

glycosphingosides.def: English equivalent is hormones. Glycolipids that are especially found in brain. They contain galactose. Its possible English equivalent is glycosphingosides.

pericardial fluid.def: The fluid in pericardial cavity which prevents friction as two layers slide over one another. Its possible English equivalent is pericardial fluid.

agglutinins.def: Antibodies that cause the sticking of bacteria or blood cells, are called agglutinins. Its possible English equivalent is agglutinins.

vasodilator.def: A drug that causes relaxation or widening of blood vessels and, thus, increases the blood flow. Its possible English equivalent is vasodilator.

fibrinogen.def: A protein present in blood plasma that is converted into insoluble protein fibrin with the help of thrombin. Its possible English equivalent is fibrinogen.

genes.def: The basic unit of genetic material and inheritance occupies a certain location on chromosome. Its possible English equivalent is genes. A change in genetic material of a cell

tissue thromboplastin.def: mutation. A substance, found in tissues in most parts of the body as an intracellular substance, accelerates the clotting of blood. Its possible English equivalent is tissue thromboplastin.

neurotransmitter.def: The chemical substance released from nerve endings to transmit the impulses to other nerves and across the minute gaps between nerves that supply to the muscles and glands etc. Its possible English equivalent is neurotra

immunity.def: Thyroid stimulating hormone (TSH). The ability of the body to resist diseases. Its possible English equivalent is immunity.
English equivalent is quarantine. The resistance of the body against the diseases. Its possible English equivalent is immunity. A type of immunity which is present or

faeces/stool.def: The waste material formed after digestion and absorption of food that is eliminated through the anus. Its possible English equivalent is faeces/stool.

adrenal gland.def: Two triangular structures situated at the superior surface of kidneys, produce the epinephrine, nor-epinephrine and corticoid hormones. Its possible English equivalent is adrenal gland.

adrenaline/epinephrine.def: A hormone, secreted by adrenal gland that prepares the body for fighting and has effect on circulation. Its possible English equivalent is adrenaline/epinephrine.

insulin.def: A hormone, produced in the pancreas by islets of Langerhans, important for regulating the amount of sugar. Its possible English equivalent is insulin.

parathyroid gland.def: Four small endocrine glands lying close to or embedded in the posterior surface of thyroid gland. Its possible English equivalent is parathyroid gland.

hypoparathyroidism.def: A condition due to diminution or absence of secretion of parathyroid gland causing muscular spasm due to fall in calcium level in blood circulation. Its possible English equivalent is hypoparathyroidism.

involution.def: is parathormone. The shrinking of uterus to its normal size after childbirth. Its possible English equivalent is involution.

blood pressure.def: The lateral pressure of blood on the walls of arteries during contraction and relaxation of ventricles. Its possible English equivalent is blood pressure.

serum.def: blood. Its composition is similar to plasma but lacks fibrinogen and other coagulating substances. Its possible English equivalent is serum.

anabolism.def: thyrotoxicosis. The synthesis of complex molecules from simpler ones by living things. Its possible English equivalent is anabolism.

catabolism.def: The breakdown in the body of chemical compounds into simpler ones. Its possible English equivalent is catabolism.

catalysis.def: A n in c r ea s e in r at e o f c h em i c a l a c tio n th ro u g h t h e m e d i u m o f ca t a l y s t. It s possible English equivalent is catalysis.

thyroglobulin.def: A protein in the thyroid gland, from which thyroid hormones are synthesized. Its possible English equivalent is thyroglobulin.

myoepithelial cells.def: The cells of epithelial tissue having a contractile cytoplasm. These cells play a role to encourage the secretions of substances into ducts. Its possible English equivalent is myoepithelial cells.

polypeptide.def: A molecule containing the amino acids linked together by peptide bonds. Its possible English equivalent is polypeptide.

adrenal cortex.def: The outer part of adrenal gland that is stimulated by ACTH to produce cortico- steroid hormones. Its possible English equivalent is adrenal cortex.

thyrotrophin releasing f actor (trf).def: A factor released from hypothalamus controlling adenohypophysis for secretion of thyroid stimulating hormone (TSH). Its possible English equivalent is thyrotrophin releasing f actor (TRF).

spermatogenesis.def: blood coagulation factors. The process by which mature spermatozoa are produced in the testis. Its possible English equivalent is spermatogenesis.

respiratory quotient.def: The ratio of the volume of carbon dioxide transferred from the blood into the alveoli to the volume of oxygen absorbed into the alveoli. Its possible English equivalent is respiratory quotient.

haemolysis.def: The destruction of red blood cells as a result of poisoning, infection, action of antibodies in mismatched blood or from defects within the red blood cells. Its possible English equivalent is haemolysis.

adrenocorticotrophic hormone (acth).def: A hormone, released by the adenohypophysis of pituitary gland, controls the activity of adrenal gland for synthesis of glucocorticoids. Its possible English equivalent is adrenocorticotrophic hormone (ACTH).

center.def: myxoedema. The middle point of a body. Its possible English equivalent is center.

lateral.def: A position pertaining farther from the mid plane of the body. Its possible English equivalent is lateral.

medial.def: A position, pertaining to middle. Its possible English equivalent is medial.

inferior.def: surface of an organ or other structure or to the lower of two or more similar structures. Its possible English equivalent is inferior.

internal surface.def: is superior. The interior surface of an organ or object. Its possible English equivalent is internal surface.

external surface.def: The exterior surface of an organ or object. Its possible English equivalent is external surface.

cavity.def: A hollow space in which an organ or object lies for its proper functioning. Its possible English equivalent is cavity.

laminae.def: The flattened part of either side of the arch of a vertebra. Its possible English equivalent is laminae.

pedicle.def: A backward projection of bony process from the body of a vertebra and connected with the lamina on each side. Its possible English equivalent is pedicle.

notch.def: The depression or indention on the edge of a bone or the organ Its possible English equivalent is notch.

groove.def: A shallow linear depression of any structure. Its possible English equivalent is groove.

canal.def: A narrow tube like structure or channel for the passage of an object viz., arteries, veins, vessels, nerves etc. Its possible English equivalent is canal.

foramen.def: An orifice or hole in a bone for the passage of vessels or nerves. Its possible English equivalent is foramen.

epicondyle.def: An eminence at the articular end of a bone lying above a condyle. Its possible English equivalent is epicondyle.

tubercle.def: In the context of anatomy, it is a rounded elevation or eminence on a bone or circumscribed solid elevation of the skin or mucous membrane. Its possible English equivalent is tubercle.

spine.def: A sharp thorn or spine shaped process of a bone. It is also referred to as spinal column. Its possible English equivalent is spine.

process.def: A projection or outgrowth of bone or tissue. Its possible English equivalent is process.

fossa.def: A furrow or shallow depression for articulation of an object. Its possible English equivalent is fossa.

trochlea.def: A ring or hook like structure having a function to pull a muscle or tendon. Its possible English equivalent is trochlea.

condyle.def: A rounded protuberance at the end of a bone forming an articulation with another bone. Its possible English equivalent is condyle.

layer.def: Thin sheet like structure composed of less or more uniform thickness. Its possible English equivalent is layer.

shaft.def: The principal portion of any cylindrical body or diaphysis of a long bone. Its possible English equivalent is shaft.

diaphysis.def: equivalent is epiphysis. The shaft or middle part of a long cylindrical bone. Its possible English equivalent is diaphysis.

proximal.def: The nearest point of attachment or center of the body of any object. Its possible English equivalent is proximal.

distal.def: The farthest point from the center or medial line or trunk of any object. Its possible English equivalent is distal.

transverse process.def: A process that extends laterally and dorsally from the arch of a vertebra. Its possible English equivalent is transverse process.

articular process.def: The four processes (Two superior and Two inferior) through which vertebrae articulate with each other. Its possible English equivalent is articular process.

median plane.def: A vertical plane through the head and trunk, which divide the body into right and left halves. Its possible English equivalent is median plane.

dissection.def: A demonstrational facet of Anatomy, which deals with cutting of body parts for the purpose of their separation and study. Its possible English equivalent is dissection.

anterior.def: anatomy. A position, that lies in front of any object. Its possible English equivalent is anterior.

posterior/dorsal.def: A position, that lies towards the rear side. Its possible English equivalent is posterior/dorsal.

transverse plane.def: It is a plane that divides the body into a top and bottom portion. Its possible English equivalent is transverse plane.

coronal plane.def: The plane that divides the body into front and back portions. Its possible English equivalent is coronal plane.

parietal bone.def: Skull bones, numbering two and situated laterally on each side. Its possible English equivalent is parietal bone.

maxilla.def: English equivalent is zygomatic bone. The bone of upper jaw. Its possible English equivalent is maxilla.

tarsal bone.def: Seven bones found in each foot. Its possible English equivalent is tarsal bone.

ulna.def: The medial bone of the forearm. Its possible English equivalent is ulna.

meta carpal.def: hand. Five long bones found in each Its possible English equivalent is meta carpal.

carpal bone.def: A composition of eight bones in each hand. These are arranged in proximal and distal rows containing four bones in each row. Its possible English equivalent is carpal bone.

vomer bone.def: A bone found in the middle of nose that makes a septum between two bones of nasal cavity. Its possible English equivalent is vomer bone.

nasal bone.def: Two bones joined with each other that form the nasal bridge. Its possible English equivalent is nasal bone.

lacrimal bone.def: Two small bones situated at medial side of orbits. Its possible English equivalent is lacrimal bone.

inferior nasal conchae.def: Bones situated in the lateral wall of the nose two in number. Its possible English equivalent is inferior nasal conchae.

palatine bone.def: A pair of bones which forms hard palate. Its possible English equivalent is palatine bone.

true ribs.def: The upper seven ribs connected on each side through their cartilage at the sternum. Its possible English equivalent is true ribs.

false ribs.def: The lower three ribs, which are not connected with sternum. Its possible English equivalent is false ribs.

floating ribs.def: The last two ribs which are free anteriorly. Its possible English equivalent is floating ribs.

malleus.def: The largest of the three auditory ossicles in the middle ear resembles to a hammer and attached to the ear drum. Its possible English equivalent is malleus.

incus.def: A bone situated in the middle of the three ossicles in the tympanum and resembles to a molar tooth. Its possible English equivalent is incus.

stapes.def: The smallest bone. It articulates with the incus. Its possible English equivalent is stapes.

lumber vertebrae.def: vertebrae. They are five in number. Its possible English equivalent is lumber vertebrae.

sacral vertebrae.def: Five vertebrae that fuse to form a single bone called Sacrum. Its possible English equivalent is sacral vertebrae.

coccygeal vertebrae.def: Four small triangular vertebrae that fuse to form the Coccyx. Its possible English equivalent is coccygeal vertebrae.

origin.def: The end of muscle attachment which is relatively fixed. Its possible English equivalent is origin.

insertion.def: In the context of anatomy, it is the end of muscle attachment which is relatively mobile. Its possible English equivalent is insertion.

cardiac muscles.def: A Special type of involuntary muscles. Its possible English equivalent is cardiac muscles.

occipitofrontalis.def: muscles. The muscle situated on the head. It is supplied by the facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is occipitofrontalis.

levator palpabrae superioris.def: One of the muscles of eyelids. It is supplied by Oculomotor nerve. Its possible English equivalent is levator palpabrae superioris.

orbicularis oculi.def: One of the muscles of the orbit. It is supplied by zygomatic and temporal branches of facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is orbicularis oculi.

procerus.def: O n e o f t h e m u s c le s o f n o s e . I t i s su p p l i e d by b u c c a l b ra n c h o f t h e f a c i a l n e rv e . I t s possible English equivalent is procerus.

nasalis.def: One of the muscles of nose. The nerve supply is through buccal branch of the facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is nasalis.

depressor septi.def: One of the muscles of the nose. The nerve supply is through buccal branch of the facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is depressor septi.

levator labii superoris alaeque nasi.def: One of the muscles of mouth. It is supplied by the facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is levator labii superoris alaeque nasi.

levator labii superioris.def: One of the muscles of the mouth. It is supplied by facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is levator labii superioris.

zygomaticus minor.def: One of the muscles of the mouth. It is supplied by the facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is zygomaticus minor.

zygomaticus major.def: One of the muscles of the mouth. It is supplied by facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is zygomaticus major.

levator anguli oris.def: One of the muscles of upper lip. It is supplied by the facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is levator anguli oris.

risorius.def: A muscle common to both lips. It is supplied by buccal branch of the facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is risorius.

mentalis.def: One of the muscles of lower lip. It is supplied by mandibular branch of facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is mentalis.

depressor labii inferioris.def: One of the muscles of lower lip. It is supplied by facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is depressor labii inferioris.

depressor anguli oris.def: One of the muscles of lower lip. It is supplied by facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is depressor anguli oris.

buccinator.def: One of the muscles of cheek. It is supplied by buccal branch of facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is buccinator.

masseter.def: One of the muscles of mastication. It is supplied by the mandibular nerve. Its possible English equivalent is masseter.

lateral pterygoid.def: One of the muscles of mastication. It is supplied by the mandibular nerve. Its possible English equivalent is lateral pterygoid.

medial pterygoid.def: One of the muscles of mastication. It is supplied by the mandibular nerve. Its possible English equivalent is medial pterygoid.

platysma.def: One of the lateral superficial muscles of the neck. It is supplied by cervical branch of the facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is platysma.

trapezius.def: One of the muscles of the upper limb. It is supplied by spinal part of accessory nerve. Its possible English equivalent is trapezius.

sternocleidomastoid.def: One of the muscles of the neck. It is supplied by spinal part of accessory nerve. Its possible English equivalent is sternocleidomastoid. One of the suprahyoid muscles, having

stylohyoid.def: equivalent is diagastric muscle. A small and thin muscle. It is supplied by facial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is stylohyoid. The muscle of floor of mouth. It is

geniohyoid.def: mylohyoid. One of the muscles of chin, supplied by fibers from C1 via hypoglossal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is geniohyoid.

sternohyoid.def: One of the infrahyoid muscles. It is supplied by ansa cervicalis. Its possible English equivalent is sternohyoid.

sternothyroid.def: A muscle that lies posterior to sternohyoid. It is supplied by ansa cervicalis. Its possible English equivalent is sternothyroid.

thyrohyoid.def: A short, quadrangular muscle. It is supplied by first cervical nerve via hypoglossal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is thyrohyoid.

longus colli.def: omohyoid. One of muscles of neck. It is supplied by anterior primary rami of C3, 4, 5 and 6. Its possible English equivalent is longus colli.

longus capitis.def: One of the anterior vertebral muscles. It is supplied by anterior primary rami of C1, 2, 3. Its possible English equivalent is longus capitis.

rectus capitis anterior.def: One of the muscles of neck. It is supplied by C1 and C2. Its possible English equivalent is rectus capitis anterior.

rectus capitis lateralis.def: A muscle of neck. It is supplied by C1 and C2. Its possible English equivalent is rectus capitis lateralis.

scalenus anterior.def: One of the lateral vertebral muscles. It is supplied by C4, 5 and 6. Its possible English equivalent is scalenus anterior.

scalenus medius.def: One of the lateral vertebral muscles. It is supplied by ventral rami of the third to eighth cervical nerves. Its possible English equivalent is scalenus medius.

scalenus posterior.def: One of the lateral vertebral muscles. It helps during inspiration. Its possible English equivalent is scalenus posterior.

splenius capitis.def: It is one of the posterior vertebral muscles. It is supplied by dorsal rami of cervical nerves. Its possible English equivalent is splenius capitis.

splenius cervices.def: One of the posterior vertebral muscles. It is supplied by dorsal rami of cervical nerves. Its possible English equivalent is splenius cervices.

erector spine.def: A muscle found on both sides of vertebral column and consists of several parts. Its possible English equivalent is erector spine.

multifidus.def: A muscle made up of several muscular and tendenous fibers. Its possible English equivalent is multifidus.

rotator muscles.def: They are eleven in number on each side. They are supplied by posterior branches of thoracic nerve. Its possible English equivalent is rotator muscles.

interspinalis.def: Muscles situated along the vertebral column and supplied by posterior branches of spinal nerves. Its possible English equivalent is interspinalis.

intertransversalis.def: Muscles that are more prominent in the neck as compared to the thoracic and lumber region. Its possible English equivalent is intertransversalis.

rectus capitis posterior major.def: One of the posterior vertebral muscles. It is supplied by suboccipital nerve. Its possible English equivalent is rectus capitis posterior major.

rectus capitis posterior minor.def: One of the posterior vertebral muscles. It is supplied by sub occipital nerve. Its possible English equivalent is rectus capitis posterior minor.

oblique capitis inferior.def: One of the posterior vertebral muscles. It is supplied by dorsal ramus of C1. Its possible English equivalent is oblique capitis inferior.

oblique capitis superior.def: Muscle supplied by dorsal ramus of C1. It extends the head on the neck and rotates the head to the same side. Its possible English equivalent is oblique capitis superior.

external intercostal muscles.def: Muscles found in between the two ribs. They are supplied by intercostal nerves. Its possible English equivalent is external intercostal muscles.

internal intercostal muscles.def: Muscles found in between the two ribs. They are supplied by intercostal nerves. Its possible English equivalent is internal intercostal muscles.

intercostalis intimi.def: Muscles found in between the two ribs. They are supplied by intercostal nerves. Its possible English equivalent is intercostalis intimi.

sternocostalis.def: One of the muscles of thorax. It is supplied by intercostal nerves. Its possible English equivalent is sternocostalis.

serratus posterior superior.def: One of the muscles of back. It is supplied by second to fifth intercostals nerves. Its possible English equivalent is serratus posterior superior.

serratus posterior inferior.def: One of the muscles of back. It is supplied by ninth to 12th intercostals nerves. Its possible English equivalent is serratus posterior inferior.

obliquous externous abdominis.def: A flat muscle which forms the anterior and lateral wall of the abdomen. It is supplied by lower six intercostal nerves. Its possible English equivalent is obliquous externous abdominis.

obliquous internous abdominis.def: The muscle that lies below the obliquous externous abdominis. It is supplied by six lower thoracic and first lumber nerves. Its possible English equivalent is obliquous internous abdominis.

cremaster.def: A muscle supplied by genital branch of genitofemoral nerve. It raises the scrotum when the muscles of inner part of thigh act. Its possible English equivalent is cremaster.

transversus abdominis.def: A muscle supplied by lower six thoracic and first lumber nerve. It compresses the ribs and viscera. Its possible English equivalent is transversus abdominis.

pyramidalis.def: A small muscle supplied by twelfth thoracic nerve. Its possible English equivalent is pyramidalis.

psoas major.def: One of the posterior abdominal muscles. It is supplied by L1, L2, L3. Its possible English equivalent is psoas major.

psoas minor.def: A muscle that lies in front of psoas major. It is supplied by L1. Its possible English equivalent is psoas minor.

iliacus.def: A muscle supplied by femoral nerve (L2, L3). It acts with psoas major. Its possible English equivalent is iliacus.

quadratus lumborum.def: A muscle supplied by the anterior primary rami of the subcostal nerve and the upper three or four lumbar nerves (T12, L1, 2, 3 and 4). Its possible English equivalent is quadratus lumborum.

obturator internus.def: One of the pelvic muscles. It is supplied by L5, S1. It rotates the thigh laterally. Its possible English equivalent is obturator internus.

piriformis.def: One of the pelvic muscles. It is supplied by L5 and S2. Its possible English equivalent is piriformis. A muscle that takes part in the formation of the

levator ani.def: possible English equivalent is levator ani. A tr i a n g u l a r m u s c l e . I t i s s u p p l ie d b y S 4 an d S 5 . It s p o s si b l e E n g l i s h e q u iv a l e n t i s coccygeus.

sphincter ani externous.def: One of the muscles of perineum. It is supplied by S4 and inferior rectal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is sphincter ani externous.

bulbospongiosus.def: Muscle that empties the urethra in males and clenches the vagina in females. Its possible English equivalent is bulbospongiosus.

ischiocavernous.def: Muscle that provides erection to the penis and clitoris. Its possible English equivalent is ischiocavernous.

transversus perinei superficialis.def: A muscle which along with transversus perinei profundus keeps the perineum tense. Its possible English equivalent is transversus perinei superficialis.

transversus perinei profundus.def: A muscle which along with transversus perinei superficialis keeps the perineum tense. Its possible English equivalent is transversus perinei profundus.

sphinctor urethrae.def: A muscle that has superficial and deep fibers. It is supplied by S2, 3, 4 and perineal branches of pudendal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is sphinctor urethrae.

latissimus dorsi.def: One of the muscles which connects the shoulder with vertebral column. It is supplied by thoracodorsal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is latissimus dorsi.

rhomboid major.def: A muscle that lies below the rhomboid minor. It is supplied by dorsal scapular nerve. Its possible English equivalent is rhomboid major.

rhomboid minor.def: A muscle that lies above the rhomboid major. It is supplied by dorsal scapular nerve. Its possible English equivalent is rhomboid minor.

levator scapulae.def: A muscle that lies at the back and also forms the lateral part of the neck along with other structures. It is supplied by ventral rami of C3 and 4 and dorsal scapular nerve (S5). Its possible English equivalent is levato

pectoralis major.def: A flat, triangular muscle lying on the upper half of the anterior surface of the thoracic wall. It is supplied by the medial and the lateral pectoral nerves. Its possible English equivalent is pectoralis major.

pectoralis minor.def: A small, triangular muscle, lying below the pectoralis major. It is supplied by the medial pectoral nerve. Its possible English equivalent is pectoralis minor.

subclavius.def: A muscle that lies below the clavicle. It is supplied by C5 and 6. Its possible English equivalent is subclavius.

serratus anterior.def: A muscle found between the ribs and scapula. It is supplied by long thoracic nerve. Its possible English equivalent is serratus anterior.

deltoid.def: One of the muscles of shoulder supplied by axillary nerve. Its possible English equivalent is deltoid.

subscapularis.def: One of the muscles of scapular region supplied by upper and lower subscapular nerve. Its possible English equivalent is subscapularis.

supraspinatus.def: One of the muscles of shoulder. It is supplied by suprascapular nerve. Its possible English equivalent is supraspinatus.

teres minor.def: One of the muscles of shoulder. It is supplied by axillary nerve. Its possible English equivalent is teres minor.

teres major.def: One of the muscles of shoulder. It is supplied by lower subscapular nerve. Its possible English equivalent is teres major.

coracobrachialis.def: One of the muscles of the arm, supplied by musculocutaneous nerve. Its possible English equivalent is coracobrachialis.

biceps.def: One of the muscles of shoulder. It is supplied by musculocutaneous nerve. Its possible English equivalent is biceps.

brachialis.def: One of the muscles of shoulder. It is supplied by the musculocutaneous nerve. Its possible English equivalent is brachialis.

triceps.def: A muscle that lies in the arm posteriorly. It is supplied by radial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is triceps.

pronator teres.def: One of the superficial muscles of the forearm. It is supplied by the median nerve. Its possible English equivalent is pronator teres.

flexor carpi radialis.def: One of the superficial muscles of the forearm. It is supplied by the median nerve. Its possible English equivalent is flexor carpi radialis.

palmaris longus.def: One of the superficial muscles of the forearm. It is supplied by median nerve. Its possible English equivalent is palmaris longus.

flexor carpi ulnaris.def: One of the superficial muscles of the forearm. It is supplied by the ulnar nerve. Its possible English equivalent is flexor carpi ulnaris.

flexor digitorum superficialis.def: A muscle that lies in the anterior compartment of the forearm. It is supplied by the median nerve. Its possible English equivalent is flexor digitorum superficialis.

flexor digitorum profundus.def: One of the muscles of the deep layer of the forearm. It is supplied by anterior interosseous and ulnar nerve. Its possible English equivalent is flexor digitorum profundus.

flexor pollicis longus.def: One of the muscles of the deep layer of the forearm. It is supplied by the anterior interosseous branch of the median nerve. Its possible English equivalent is flexor pollicis longus.

brachioradialis.def: quadratus. A muscle supplied by radial nerve. It extends the elbow joint. Its possible English equivalent is brachioradialis.

extensor carpi radialis longus.def: One of the muscles of the posterior compartment of the forearm, supplied by radial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is extensor carpi radialis longus.

extensor carpi radialis brevis.def: A muscle that lies below the extensor carpi radialis longus. It is supplied by posterior interosseous branch of the radial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is extensor carpi radialis brevis.

extensor digitorum.def: A muscle supplied by the posterior interosseous branch of the radial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is extensor digitorum.

extensor digiti minimi.def: One of the muscles of the posterior compartment of the forearm. It is supplied by postesior interosseous nerve. Its possible English equivalent is extensor digiti minimi.

anconeus.def: equivalent is extensor carpi ulnaris. A small, triangular muscle. It is supplied by the radial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is anconeus.

supinator.def: A muscle is supplied by the posterior interosseous branch of the radial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is supinator.

abductor pollicis longus.def: A muscle that is supplied by posterior interosseous branch of the radial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is abductor pollicis longus.

extensor pollicis brevis.def: A muscle that is supplied by the posterior interosseous branch of the radial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is extensor pollicis brevis.

extensor pollicis longus.def: A muscle that is supplied by the posterior interosseous branch of the radial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is extensor pollicis longus.

extensor indicis.def: A muscle that is supplied by the posterior interosseous branch of the radial nerve. It extends Its possible English equivalent is extensor indicis.

abductor pollicis brevis.def: A muscle that is supplied by the median nerve. Its possible English equivalent is abductor pollicis brevis.

opponens pollicis.def: pollicis brevis. A muscle supplied by the median nerve. Its possible English equivalent is opponens pollicis.

adductor pollicis.def: A muscle supplied by the deep branch of the ulnar nerve. It adducts the thumb. Its possible English equivalent is adductor pollicis.

abductor digiti minimi.def: A muscle supplied by the deep branch of the ulnar nerve. Its possible English equivalent is abductor digiti minimi.
A muscle found on the lateral side of the plantar aspect of foot. It is supplied by the lateral plantar nerve. Its possible English equivalent is abductor digiti minimi.

flexor digiti minimi brevis.def: A muscle supplied by the ulnar nerve. It flexes the little finger towards the palm. Its possible English equivalent is flexor digiti minimi brevis.
A muscle supplied by lateral plantar nerve. It flexes the fifth toe. Its possible English equivalent is flexor digiti minimi brevis.

opponens digiti minimi.def: A muscle supplied by the deep branch of the ulnar nerve. Its possible English equivalent is opponens digiti minimi.

palmaris brevis.def: A muscle supplied by the superficial branch of the ulnar nerve. Its possible English equivalent is palmaris brevis.

musculi lumbricales manus.def: Four small, round muscles found in the palm. Its possible English equivalent is musculi lumbricales manus.

palmar interossei.def: Muscles four in number are supplied by the deep branch of the ulnar nerve. Its possible English equivalent is palmar interossei.

dorsal interossei.def: Muscles supplied by the deep branch of the ulnar nerve and are four in number Its possible English equivalent is dorsal interossei.
Dorsal interossei, four in number. They are supplied by lateral plantar nerve. Its possible English equivalent is dorsal interossei.

tensor fascia lata.def: It is supplied by superior gluteal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is tensor fascia lata.

sartorius.def: The most superficial muscle of the anterior compartment of the thigh. It is supplied by the femoral nerve. Its possible English equivalent is sartorius.

quardriceps femoris.def: A large muscle of the anterior surface of the thigh. It is composed of four main parts. Its possible English equivalent is quardriceps femoris.

rectus femoris.def: One of the four parts of quadriceps femoris. Its possible English equivalent is rectus femoris.

vastus lateralis.def: One of the four parts of Quadriceps femoris. Its possible English equivalent is vastus lateralis.

vastus medialis.def: One of the four parts of Quadriceps femoris. Its possible English equivalent is vastus medialis.

articularis genus.def: A muscle that lies deep to Vastus intermedius. It is supplied by the femoral nerve. Its possible English equivalent is articularis genus.

gracilis.def: A muscle supplied by anterior division of obturator nerve. Its possible English equivalent is gracilis.

adductor longus.def: pectineus. A muscle supplied by the obturator nerve. It is an adductor of thigh. Its possible English equivalent is adductor longus.

adductor brevis.def: A muscle supplied by the obturator nerve. It adducts the thigh. Its possible English equivalent is adductor brevis.

adductor magnus.def: A large, triangular muscle, supplied by obturator nerve and branches of the sciatic nerve. Its possible English equivalent is adductor magnus.

gluteus maximus.def: The largest of the gluteal muscles, supplied by inferior gluteal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is gluteus maximus.

gluteus medius.def: The muscle supplied by the superior gluteal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is gluteus medius.

gluteus minimus.def: The muscle supplied by the superior gluteal nerve. Its actions are similar to gluteus medius. Its possible English equivalent is gluteus minimus.

gamellus superior.def: A muscle that arises from the gluteal surface of the ischial spine. It rotates the thigh laterally. Its possible English equivalent is gamellus superior.

gamellus inferior.def: A muscle that arises from the upper part of the ischial tuberosity. It rotates the thigh laterally. Its possible English equivalent is gamellus inferior.

quadratus femoris.def: A quadrilateral muscle. It rotates the hip laterally. Its possible English equivalent is quadratus femoris.

obturator externus.def: A muscle supplied by the obturator nerve. It rotates the femur laterally. Its possible English equivalent is obturator externus.

biceps femoris.def: One of the hamstring muscles. The long head is supplied by the sciatic nerve and the short head is supplied by the common peroneal portion. Its possible English equivalent is biceps femoris.

semitendinosus.def: One of the hamstring muscles. It is supplied by the tibial division of the sciatic nerve. Its possible English equivalent is semitendinosus.

semimembranosus.def: One of the hamstring muscles. It is supplied by the tibial division of the sciatic nerve. Its possible English equivalent is semimembranosus.

tibialis anterior.def: A muscle that lies on the front of the leg. It is supplied by the deep peroneal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is tibialis anterior.

extensor hallucis longus.def: One of the muscles of leg. It is supplied by the deep peroneal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is extensor hallucis longus.

extensor digitorum longus.def: One of the muscles of leg. It is supplied by the deep peroneal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is extensor digitorum longus.

peroneus tertius.def: A muscle situated on the lower lateral aspect of the leg. It is supplied by the deep peroneal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is peroneus tertius.

peroneus longus.def: A muscle situated on the lateral side of the leg. It is supplied by the superficial peroneal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is peroneus longus.

peroneus brevis.def: A muscle situated on the lateral side of the leg. It is supplied by superficial peroneal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is peroneus brevis.

gastrocnemius.def: One of the muscles of posterior part of the leg. It is supplied by the tibial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is gastrocnemius.

tendo-calcaneus.def: soleus. A muscle formed by the tendons of gastrocnemius and soleus. Its possible English equivalent is tendo-calcaneus.

flexor hallucis longus.def: The muscle which flexes all of the joints of the great toe. It is supplied by the tibial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is flexor hallucis longus.

flexor digitorum longus.def: A muscle that lies medial to the flexor hallucis longus. It is supplied by the tibial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is flexor digitorum longus.

tibialis posterior.def: The deepest muscle on the back of leg. It is supplied by the tibial nerve. Its possible English equivalent is tibialis posterior.

extensor digitorum brevis.def: A muscle situated on the dorsum of the foot. Its possible English equivalent is extensor digitorum brevis.

abductor hallucis.def: A muscle found on the medial side of the plantar aspect of the foot. It is supplied by medial plantar nerve. Its possible English equivalent is abductor hallucis.

flexor digitorum brevis.def: A muscle situated in the sole of the foot. It is supplied by medial plantar nerve. Its possible English equivalent is flexor digitorum brevis.

flexor accessorius.def: A muscle supplied by lateral plantar nerve. Its possible English equivalent is flexor accessorius. A group of four muscles among whomthe

flexor hallucis brevis.def: equivalent is musculi lumbricales pedis. A muscle situated deep in the sole of the foot. It is supplied by medial plantar nerve. Its possible English equivalent is flexor hallucis brevis.

adductor hallucis.def: A muscle supplied by lateral plantar nerve. It adducts the great toe. Its possible English equivalent is adductor hallucis.

infraspinatus.def: A triangular muscle, lies in scapular region and is supplied by suprascapular nerve. Its possible English equivalent is infraspinatus.

rectus superior.def: One of the extra ocular muscles. Its possible English equivalent is rectus superior.

rectus inferior.def: One of the extra ocular muscles. Its possible English equivalent is rectus inferior.

rectus medialis.def: One of the extra ocular muscles. Its possible English equivalent is rectus medialis.

superior oblique.def: One of the extra ocular muscles. Its possible English equivalent is superior oblique.

inferior oblique.def: One of the extra ocular muscles. Its possible English equivalent is inferior oblique.

constrictor pharyngis inferior.def: One of the muscles of pharynx. Its possible English equivalent is constrictor pharyngis inferior.

constrictor pharyngis medius.def: One of the muscles of pharynx. Its possible English equivalent is constrictor pharyngis medius.

constrictor pharyngis superior.def: One of the muscles of pharynx. Its possible English equivalent is constrictor pharyngis superior.

stylopharyngeus.def: One of the muscles of pharynx. Its possible English equivalent is stylopharyngeus.

palatopharyngeus.def: One of the muscles of pharynx. Its possible English equivalent is palatopharyngeus.

levator palati muscle.def: One of the muscles of palate. Its possible English equivalent is levator palati muscle.

tensor palati muscle.def: One of the muscles of palate. Its possible English equivalent is tensor palati muscle.

musculus uvulae.def: One of the muscles of palate. Its possible English equivalent is musculus uvulae.

palatoglossus muscle.def: One of the muscles of palate. Its possible English equivalent is palatoglossus muscle.

cricothyroideus.def: One of the muscles of larynx, supplied by external laryngeal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is cricothyroideus.

cricoarytenoideus posterior.def: One of the muscles of larynx. It is supplied by Recurrent laryngeal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is cricoarytenoideus posterior.

cricoarytenoideus lateralis.def: One of the muscles of larynx. It is supplied by Recurrent laryngeal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is cricoarytenoideus lateralis.

arytenoideus.def: thyroarytenoideus. One of the muscles of larynx. Its possible English equivalent is arytenoideus.

thyroepiglotticus muscle.def: One of the muscles of larynx. It is supplied by Recurrent laryngeal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is thyroepiglotticus muscle.

aryepiglotticus muscle.def: One of the muscles of larynx. It is supplied by Recurrent laryngeal nerve. Its possible English equivalent is aryepiglotticus muscle.

elastic cartilage.def: The cartilage in which cells are surrounded with territorial capsular matrix and interterritorail matrix having elastic fiber network. Its possible English equivalent is elastic cartilage.

hyaline cartilage.def: The cartilage having a frosted glass appearance. Its possible English equivalent is hyaline cartilage.

semilunar cartilages.def: means lunate shaped. These are the moon shaped cartilages present in the knee joints. Its possible English equivalent is semilunar cartilages.

floating cartilage.def: Loose pieces of cartilage with in a joint cavity detached from the menisci. Its possible English equivalent is floating cartilage.

accessory ligament.def: equivalent is ligamentum teres. The ligament that strengthens or supports another one. Its possible English equivalent is accessory ligament.

capsular ligament.def: The fibrous layer of a joint capsule. Its possible English equivalent is capsular ligament.

simple joint.def: A joint composed of two bones with their articular surfaces joined with each other. Its possible English equivalent is simple joint.

compound joint.def: A joint composed of more than two bones with their articular surfaces making a single capsule. Its possible English equivalent is compound joint.

temporo mandibular joint.def: A synovial joint of the condylar variety, found between temporal bone and mandible. Its possible English equivalent is temporo mandibular joint.

sterno clavicular joint.def: A joint that lies between medial end of the clavicle, the clavicular notch of the manubrium sterni and the upper surface of the first costal cartilage. Its possible English equivalent is sterno clavicular joint.

acromio clavicular joint.def: A joint formed by the articulation of small facets at the lateral end of the clavicle and the medial margin of the acromion process of the scapula. Its possible English equivalent is acromio clavicular joint.

interphalangeal joint.def: tarsal joints. The joints present between phalanges of foot. Its possible English equivalent is interphalangeal joint.

carpometacarpal joints.def: The joint is made in between the distal surface of Trapezium and the proximal surface of the base of the first Metacarpal bone. Its possible English equivalent is carpometacarpal joints.

interphalangeal joints.def: The joints belonging to hinge type of joints. They are formed between the head of the more proximal phalanx with the base of the next distal phalanx in the series. Its possible English equivalent is interphalangeal joint

metatarso phalangeal joints.def: The joints found between metatarsal and proximal Phalanges. Its possible English equivalent is metatarso phalangeal joints.

intermetatarsal joints.def: In these types of joints the bases of the lateral four metatarsal articulate through synovial joints between facets on their adjacent sides. Its possible English equivalent is intermetatarsal joints.

dura mater.def: The outer thickest membranous covering of the brain. Its possible English equivalent is dura mater.

arachnoid mater.def: A membrane that lies between dura and pia mater and loosely surrounds the brain without dipping into its sulci. Its possible English equivalent is arachnoid mater.

spinal nerves.def: The nerves arising from spinal cord. Its possible English equivalent is spinal nerves.

optic nerve.def: olfactory nerve. It is the nerve of sight. It is a sensory nerve and is about 1.6 inches long. Its possible English equivalent is optic nerve.

oculomotor nerve.def: A motor nerve. It supplies all the extrinsic muscles of the eye except lateral rectus and superior oblique. Its possible English equivalent is oculomotor nerve.

trochlear nerve.def: A motor nerve. It supplies the superior oblique muscle of the eyeball. Its possible English equivalent is trochlear nerve.

trigeminal nerve.def: The largest cranial nerve. It is mixed, sensory and motor both. Its possible English equivalent is trigeminal nerve.

ophthalmic nerve.def: A sensory nerve that supplies the forehead, scalp, frontal sinus, lacrimal gland etc. Its possible English equivalent is ophthalmic nerve.

maxillary nerve.def: A purely sensory nerve. It leaves the skull through the foramen rotundum and is distributed to the skin of the face overlying the maxilla. Its possible English equivalent is maxillary nerve.

abducent nerve.def: nerve. A motor nerve that supplies the lateral rectus muscle of eyeball. Its possible English equivalent is abducent nerve.

facial nerve.def: A mixed nerve that supplies the muscles of the face, cheek, scalp etc. Its possible English equivalent is facial nerve.

vestibulo-cochlear nerve.def: A nerve that consists of two sets of sensory fibers, vestibular and cochlear. The vestibular part is concerned with balance and the cochlear part is concerned with hearing. Its possible English equivalent is vestibulo-co

glossopharyngeal nerve.def: A motor and sensory nerve which descends through the upper part of the neck to the back of the tongue. Its possible English equivalent is glossopharyngeal nerve.

accessory nerve.def: and a spinal root. The two roots unite and leave the skull through the jugular foramen. Its motor part supplies sternocleidomastoid and the trapezius. Its cranial part joins vagus nerve. Its possible English equivalent i

hypoglossal nerve.def: A motor nerve. It passes downward and forward in the neck to reach the tongue. It innervates the muscles of the tongue and controls the shape and movements of the tongue. Its possible English equivalent is hypoglossal ne

pharyngeal branch of the vagus.def: A nerve which arises from the inferior ganglion and contains motor fibres from the cranial part of the accessory nerve. Its possible English equivalent is pharyngeal branch of the vagus.

superior laryngeal nerve.def: A nerve which arises from the inferior ganglion and runs downwards and medially behind the internal carotid artery. Its possible English equivalent is superior laryngeal nerve.

cervical plexus.def: four cervical nerves join together to form a plexus, which supplies the structures in head and neck region. Its possible English equivalent is cervical plexus.

brachial plexus.def: The plexus formed by the union of the th th th th anterior rami of the 5 , 6 , 7 , and 8 st thoracic nerves, and cervical and 1 supplies the upper limb. Its possible English equivalent is brachial plexus.

lumbar plexus.def: The anterior rami of the upper four lumbar nerves join together to form a plexus for supplying the lower limb. Its possible English equivalent is lumbar plexus.

lacrimal duct.def: A pair of two ducts. Both of which begin at the lacrimal punctum and open into the lacrimal sac. Its possible English equivalent is lacrimal duct.

lacrimal sac.def: The upper, wider part of the nasolacrimal duct. Both the lacrimal ducts open into this sac. Its possible English equivalent is lacrimal sac.

lacrimal apparatus.def: The structures associated with the secretion and drainage of the lacrimal fluid. Its possible English equivalent is lacrimal apparatus.

eyeball.def: The ball of the eye, about 2.5cm in diameter and situated into the orbit. Its possible English equivalent is eyeball.

orbit.def: The two bony sockets which protect the eyeballs from external injuries. Its possible English equivalent is orbit.

sclera.def: The outer tough layers of the eyeball. Its possible English equivalent is sclera. At the entrance of the optic nerve into the

cornea.def: equivalent is lamina cribrosa. The transparent anterior one sixth of the eyeball. Its possible English equivalent is cornea.

conjunctiva.def: A membrane that covers the anterior surface of the eyeball and the posterior surface of the lids. Its possible English equivalent is conjunctiva.

choroid.def: The middle highly vascular layer of the eyeball. Its possible English equivalent is choroid.

iris.def: A membrane placed between the cornea and the lens. It has the property of contraction and dilation. Its possible English equivalent is iris.

pupil.def: A hole made by central free margin of the iris, and the light enters the eye through it. Its possible English equivalent is pupil.

ciliary muscle.def: A ring of unstriped muscle in the eye. It has radial and circular fibres. Its possible English equivalent is ciliary muscle.

retina.def: The innermost nervous coat of eye ball, responsible for vision. Its possible English equivalent is retina.

aqueous humour.def: A fluid within the eyeball, which fills the space between the cornea in front and the lens behind. Its possible English equivalent is aqueous humour.

vitreous body.def: A fluid which fills the posterior four fifths of the eyeball. Its possible English equivalent is vitreous body.

lens.def: A bi-convex structure, kept in place by the suspensory ligament. Its possible English equivalent is lens.

eye brow.def: The two prominent arches situated above the orbit. Its possible English equivalent is eye brow.

eyelashes.def: The thick and curved hair attached to the free margins of the eyelids. Its possible English equivalent is eyelashes.

auricle /pinna.def: A part of the external ear. The great part of it is made up of the cartilage. Its possible English equivalent is auricle /pinna.

lobule.def: The lowest part of the auricle/pinna, made up of connective tissue. Its possible English equivalent is lobule.

tympanic membrane.def: A membrane that separates the external ear from the middle ear. Its possible English equivalent is tympanic membrane.

middle ear / tympanic cavity/ tympanum.def: A space between the external ear and the internal ear. Its possible English equivalent is middle ear / tympanic cavity/ tympanum.

auditory tube.def: A tube through which the middle ear communicates with the nasopharynx. Its possible English equivalent is auditory tube.

bony labyrinth.def: The internal ear is made up of the bony labyrinth within which there is a membranous labyrinth. Its possible English equivalent is bony labyrinth.

vestibule.def: The central part of the bony labyrinth of the internal ear. Its possible English equivalent is vestibule.

semicircular canals.def: Three bony semicircular canals lying posterosuperior to the vestibule. They open into the vestibule. Its possible English equivalent is semicircular canals.

cochlea.def: A spiral tube that forms a part of the inner ear. It resembles the shell of a common snail and lies anterior to the vestibule. Its possible English equivalent is cochlea.

membranous labyrinth.def: A closed membrane filled with a transparent fluid of thin consistency. Its possible English equivalent is membranous labyrinth.

utricle.def: One of the parts of the membranous labyrinth of the internal ear. It lies in the poster superior part of the vestibule. Its possible English equivalent is utricle.

saccule.def: One of the parts of the membranous labyrinth of the internal ear. Its possible English equivalent is saccule.

palate.def: It makes the roof of the oral cavity. It is of two types; hard palate and soft palate. Its possible English equivalent is palate.

hard palate.def: The bony hard part of the palate. Its possible English equivalent is hard palate.

soft palate.def: A movable part, forming an incomplete wall between the oral cavity and the pharynx. Its possible English equivalent is soft palate.

cricoid cartilage.def: The cartilage which forms the inferior wall of larynx lies below the lower border of thyroid cartilage. Its possible English equivalent is cricoid cartilage.

epiglottic cartilage.def: A leaf like cartilage of the larynx. Its possible English equivalent is epiglottic cartilage.

corniculate cartilage.def: A soft triangular paired hyaline cartilage of larynx located at the apex of each arytenoid cartilage. Its possible English equivalent is corniculate cartilage.

cuneiform cartilage.def: Club-shaped pieces of elastic cartilage located anterior to the corniculate cartilages and they are two in number. Its possible English equivalent is cuneiform cartilage.

nasal cartilages.def: The cartilages which form the framework of the nose. Its possible English equivalent is nasal cartilages.

salivary glands.def: The three pairs of glands, which secrete the saliva. Its possible English equivalent is salivary glands.

parotid gland.def: The largest salivary gland. The duct arising from it opens opposite the crown of the second molar teeth. Its possible English equivalent is parotid gland.

sublingual salivary gland.def: submandibular salivary gland. The smallest of the three glands, secreting the saliva. It lies below the mucosa of the floor of the mouth. Its possible English equivalent is sublingual salivary gland.

lip.def: The fleshy part which forms the boundary of mouth. Its possible English equivalent is lip.

tooth.def: mouth cavity. The bony projections in jaws serving as organs of mastication. Its possible English equivalent is tooth.

lung.def: A respiratory organ situated in the thoracic cavity. Its possible English equivalent is lung.

lungs.def: A term used for both the lungs. Its possible English equivalent is lungs.

pericardium.def: A double membranous sac which encloses the heart. Its possible English equivalent is pericardium.

right atrium.def: The right upper chamber of the heart. Its possible English equivalent is right atrium.

left atrium.def: The left upper chamber of the heart. Its possible English equivalent is left atrium.

right ventricle.def: The right lower chamber of the heart. Its possible English equivalent is right ventricle.

left ventricle.def: The left lower chamber of the heart. Its possible English equivalent is left ventricle.

coronary artery.def: Artery which supplies oxygenated blood to the heart. It has two branches; right and left. Its possible English equivalent is coronary artery.

left coronary artery.def: The left coronary artery which is larger than the right coronary artery. Its possible English equivalent is left coronary artery.

arch of aorta.def: A continuation of the ascending aorta. It has three main branches. Its possible English equivalent is arch of aorta.

left common carotid artery.def: The branch of aortic arch which arises from the convex surface on the left side of innominate artery. It supplies blood to head and neck. Its possible English equivalent is left common carotid artery.

left subclavian artery.def: The branch of aortic arch which arises b e h in d t h e le f t c o m m o n c a ro t i d a r t er y . I t su p p li e s b l o o d m a in ly t o u p p e r l i m b . I t s possible English equivalent is left subclavian arte

right common carotid artery.def: A branch of innominate artery. It supplies blood to head and neck. Its possible English equivalent is right common carotid artery.
A branch of innominate artery. Its possible English equivalent is right common carotid artery.

descending aorta.def: It begins as a continuation of aortic arch. It has two parts; thoracic and abdominal. Its possible English equivalent is descending aorta.

descending thoracic aorta.def: A part of aorta that lies in the thorax. Its possible English equivalent is descending thoracic aorta.

posterior intercostal arteries.def: The arteries which supply blood to lower nine intercostal spaces. Its possible English equivalent is posterior intercostal arteries.

oesophageal arteries.def: The artey which supplies blood to the oesophagus. These are four in numbers. Its possible English equivalent is oesophageal arteries.

bronchial arteries.def: The arteries which supply blood to bronchus, brochioles and lungs. These are three in numbers. Its possible English equivalent is bronchial arteries.

pericardial arteries.def: The arteries which supply blood to pericardium. Its possible English equivalent is pericardial arteries.

diaphragmatic arteries.def: The arteries which supply blood to the diaphragm and pericardium. Its possible English equivalent is diaphragmatic arteries.

abdominal aorta.def: A part of descending aorta which begins at the level of lower border of twelfth thoracic vertebra. Its possible English equivalent is abdominal aorta.

phrenic artery.def: The artery which supplies the diaphragm. Its possible English equivalent is phrenic artery.

left gastric artery.def: is coeliac artery. The smallest branch of celiac artery which spreads in the stomach. Its possible English equivalent is left gastric artery.

hepatic artery.def: A branch of celiac artery which spreads in the liver. Its possible English equivalent is hepatic artery.

cystic artery.def: The artery which arises from the right branch of hepatic artery and goes to the gall bladder. Its possible English equivalent is cystic artery.

umbilical artery.def: The artery which carries deoxygenated blood from the foetus upto the placenta. Its possible English equivalent is umbilical artery.

splenic artery.def: A branch of celiac artery which spreads out in the spleen. Its possible English equivalent is splenic artery.

superior mesenteric artery.def: A branch of abdominal aorta which s u p p l i e s b l o o d t o s m a ll i n t e s t in e s e x c e p t u p p e r h a lf o f d u o d en u m , a s c e n d i n g a n d transverse colon. Its possible English equivalent is s

renal artery.def: A branch of abdominal aorta which arises below the superior mesenteric artery and supplies blood to the kidneys. Two in numbers-left and right. Its possible English equivalent is renal artery.

gonadal arteries.def: Long branches of abdominal aorta arise below the renal artery and supply gonads (Testes in male & ovaries in female). Its possible English equivalent is gonadal arteries.

inferior mesentric artery.def: A branch of abdominal aorta which s p re a d s a lo n g th e m e s e n t e r y a n d s u p p l ie s b lo o d t o d es c e n d in g c o l o n , sp le n i c fl e x u re and rectum. Its possible English equivalent is inferi

external carotid artery.def: A branch of common carotid artery which gives branches in the face and head. Its possible English equivalent is external carotid artery.

internal carotid artery.def: A branch of common carotid artery, supplies blood to the meninges brain and other structures. Its possible English equivalent is internal carotid artery.

ophthalmic artery.def: A branch of internal carotid artery. It supplies the eye ball and contents of orbit. Its possible English equivalent is ophthalmic artery.

axillary artery.def: The artery is a continuation of subclavian, at the outer border of first rib. Its possible English equivalent is axillary artery.

brachial artery.def: Artery of the arm. Its possible English equivalent is brachial artery.

ulnar artery.def: One of the terminal branches of brachial artery. Its possible English equivalent is ulnar artery.

posterior ulnar carpal artery.def: The artery lies on the posterior side of the wrist. Its possible English equivalent is posterior ulnar carpal artery.

dorsalis pedis artery.def: The artery is a continuation of anterior tibial artery at the ankle. Its possible English equivalent is dorsalis pedis artery.

first dorsal metatarsal artery.def: A branch of dorsalis pedis artery which spreads on the dorsal and lateral side of the toe. Its possible English equivalent is first dorsal metatarsal artery.

peroneal artery.def: A branch of the posterior tibial artery and runs on the lateral side of leg. Its possible English equivalent is peroneal artery.

lateral malleolar artery.def: The artery is a branch of anterior tibial artery lies on the lateral side of the ankle. Its possible English equivalent is lateral malleolar artery.

pulmonary artery.def: arteries. Artery which carries blood from the heart to the lungs. Its possible English equivalent is pulmonary artery.

facial artery.def: The main artery of the face, and a branch of external carotid artery. Its possible English equivalent is facial artery.

capillaries.def: Very thin vessels which are junctions between the end of arterioles and beginning of veins. Its possible English equivalent is capillaries.

superficial veins.def: Veins found below the skin. Its possible English equivalent is superficial veins.

deep veins.def: Veins that are found deep in the body and run along the arteries. Its possible English equivalent is deep veins.

venae comitans.def: Paired veins which are found on both sides of small arteries. Its possible English equivalent is venae comitans.

vasa vasorum.def: Arteries which provide nutrition to the vessels. Its possible English equivalent is vasa vasorum.

facial vein.def: A vein formed with the union of supratrochlear and supraorbital veins. Its possible English equivalent is facial vein.

cardiac veins.def: Veins present in the heart. These veins carry the cardiac venous blood back into the right atrium. Main veins are seven in numbers. Its possible English equivalent is cardiac veins.

portal vein.def: A vein formed by union of splenic and superior mesenteric veins. It drains the blood in the liver. Its possible English equivalent is portal vein.

pulmonary veins.def: Veins which carry oxygenated blood from the lungs to the heart. Two veins arise from each lung. Its possible English equivalent is pulmonary veins.

intercostal veins.def: Veins running in the intercostal spaces. Its possible English equivalent is intercostal veins.

subclavian vein.def: A continuation of axillary vein, lies in front of the subclavian artery. Its possible English equivalent is subclavian vein.

innominate or brachiocephalic vein.def: A vein formed behind the strerno- clavicular joint by the union of internal jugular vein and subclavian vein. Its possible English equivalent is innominate or brachiocephalic vein.

superior vena cava.def: A vein formed by the union of left and right brachieocephalic veins. It opens into the right atrium. Its possible English equivalent is superior vena cava.

azygos vein.def: possible English equivalent is azygos vein. A large superficial vein of the leg and thigh. Its possible English equivalent is vein. great saphenous

common iliac vein.def: vein. Vein that is formed by the union of the external iliac and the internal iliac veins. Its possible English equivalent is common iliac vein.

external iliac vein.def: A continuation of the femoral vein, joins with internal iliac vein to form the common iliac vein. Its possible English equivalent is external iliac vein.

gonadal veins.def: Present in the abdomen on left and right side. Left one drains the left pampiniform plexus and empties into the left renal vein and right one empties into the inferior vena cava. Its possible English equivalent is gonada

superior mesenteric vein.def: Vein that follows the distribution of its homonymous artery and empties into the splenic vein to form the hepatic portal vein. Its possible English equivalent is superior mesenteric vein.

inferior mesenteric vein.def: Vein that follows the distribution of its homonymous artery and empties into the splenic vein to form portal vein. Its possible English equivalent is inferior mesenteric vein.

gastric vein.def: A small vein which runs with the gastric artery from left to right of the stomach and ends at the porta hepatis. Its possible English equivalent is gastric vein.

hepatic veins.def: Several veins that receive blood from the portal vein of the liver and drain into the inferior vena cava on the posterior aspect of the liver. Its possible English equivalent is hepatic veins.

cerebral veins.def: English equivalent is phrenic veins. Veins serving the cerebral hemispheres of the brain. Its possible English equivalent is cerebral veins.

meningeal veins.def: Veins which drain the dura mater. Its possible English equivalent is meningeal veins.

temporal vein.def: A vein formed with the joining of anterior and posterior temporal veins. It lies in the temporal region. Its possible English equivalent is temporal vein.

umbilical vein.def: Vein which collects purified blood from the placenta. It runs upward with suspensory ligament reaches the liver where it divides into small branches. Its possible English equivalent is umbilical vein.

stomach.def: The widest part of the digestive tube. Its possible English equivalent is stomach.

cardiac orifice.def: The upper orifice of the two orifices of the stomach. Its possible English equivalent is cardiac orifice.

pyloric orifice.def: The lower orifice of the two orifices of the stomach. Its possible English equivalent is pyloric orifice.

lesser curvature.def: The upper border of the stomach. Its possible English equivalent is lesser curvature.

greater curvature.def: The lower border of the stomach. Its possible English equivalent is greater curvature.

right iliac fossa.def: fossa. One of the nine regions of the abdomen. Its possible English equivalent is right iliac fossa.

left lumbar region.def: One of the nine regions of the abdomen. Its possible English equivalent is left lumbar region.

umbilical region.def: One of the nine regions of the abdomen. Its possible English equivalent is umbilical region.

right lumbar region.def: One of the nine regions of the abdomen. Its possible English equivalent is right lumbar region.

left hypochondrium.def: One of the nine regions of the abdomen. Its possible English equivalent is left hypochondrium.

epigastrium.def: One of the nine regions of the abdomen. Its possible English equivalent is epigastrium.

right hypochondrium.def: One of the nine regions of the abdomen. Its possible English equivalent is right hypochondrium.

hypogastrium.def: One of the nine regions of the abdomen. Its possible English equivalent is hypogastrium.

pyloric valve.def: A valve which closes the pyloric opening of the stomach till the food gets digested into it. Its possible English equivalent is pyloric valve.

villus.def: equivalent is villi. It is the singular of Khamal. Its possible English equivalent is villus.

solitary lymphatic nodules.def: The lymphatic nodules scattered into the whole length of the small intestine. Its possible English equivalent is solitary lymphatic nodules.

aggregated lymphatic nodules.def: The patches formed due the accumulation of solitary lymphatic nodules into the small intestine. Its possible English equivalent is aggregated lymphatic nodules.

vermiform appendix.def: A worm like diverticulum arising from the posteromedial wall of the caecum. Its possible English equivalent is vermiform appendix.

ileocaecal valve.def: The valve found at the junction of small intestine and the caecum. Its possible English equivalent is ileocaecal valve.

ascending colon.def: A part of the colon similar to the caecum. It extends from the right iliac fossa to the inferior surface of the right lobe of the liver. Its possible English equivalent is ascending colon.

transverse colon.def: The part of the colon which lies between the ascending and descending colon transversely. Its possible English equivalent is transverse colon.

right colic flexure / hepatic flexure.def: A bent formed due to tilting of ascending colon to the left. Its possible English equivalent is right colic flexure / hepatic flexure.

left colic flexure / splenic flexure.def: A bent formed by the tilting of transverse colon towards the lower side near the spleen. Its possible English equivalent is left colic flexure / splenic flexure.

descending colon.def: The part of colon that extends from the left colic flexure to the sigmoid colon. Its possible English equivalent is descending colon.

sigmoid colon.def: It is a narrow part of the colon and is similar to greek word (s). It is continuous to another part of large intestine, i.e., rectum. Its possible English equivalent is sigmoid colon.

anus.def: The lower part of the rectum forming the terminal part of the large intestine. Its possible English equivalent is anus.
