common hepatic duct.def: A duct formed by the union of right and left hepatic ducts. Its possible English equivalent is common hepatic duct.

cystic duct.def: A short duct, beginning at the neck of the gall bladder. Its possible English equivalent is cystic duct.

common bile duct.def: A duct which transfers the bile from the liver and the gall bladder to the intestine. Its possible English equivalent is common bile duct.

pancreatic duct.def: A duct, which runs through the whole length of the pancreas. It opens into the duodenum. Its possible English equivalent is pancreatic duct.

kidnies.def: The bean shaped paired excretory organs, on the posterior abdominal wall. Its possible English equivalent is kidnies.

suprarenal glands.def: A pair of endocrine glands situated on the posterior abdominal wall over the upper pole of the kidneys. Its possible English equivalent is suprarenal glands.

urethra.def: A membranous duct which extends from the internal urethral orifice (neck of the urinary bladder) to the external urethral orifice at the tip of the penis. Its possible English equivalent is urethra.

glans clitoridis.def: equivalent is clitoris. A rounded tubercle formed on the free end of the clitoris. Its possible English equivalent is glans clitoridis.

vestibule of vagina.def: The space between the two labia minora. The urethra opens in its centre. Its possible English equivalent is vestibule of vagina.

bulb of vestibule.def: Two oval bodies, lying on either side of the vaginal orifices. Its possible English equivalent is bulb of vestibule.

fundus of uterus.def: equivalent is uterus. The upper convex end of the uterus. Its possible English equivalent is fundus of uterus.

body of uterus.def: A part of the uterus, lying in between the fundus and the cervix. Its possible English equivalent is body of uterus.

external os.def: The external opening of the cervix of uterus. Its possible English equivalent is external os.

uterine cavity.def: The cavity of the uterus. It is trigonal in shape and is situated within its body. Its possible English equivalent is uterine cavity.

uterine /fallopian tube.def: Two ducts which transfer ova from the ovary to the uterus. Its possible English equivalent is uterine /fallopian tube.

ovary.def: The female gonads, two in number and oval in shape. They produce the ovum. Its possible English equivalent is ovary.

testes.def: Its possible English equivalent is testes. Qadib is the male organ of copulation. Its possible English equivalent is penis.

prostate.def: An accessory gland of the male reproductive system. Its possible English equivalent is prostate.

epididymis.def: The first part of the excretory duct of each testis. Its possible English equivalent is epididymis.

bulbo urethral gland / cowper’s gland.def: A gland which secretes a viscid fluid forming a part of the seminal fluid. Its possible English equivalent is bulbo urethral gland / Cowper’s gland.

ejaculatory duct.def: A duct formed by the union of the ductus deferens and the seminal vesicle. Its possible English equivalent is ejaculatory duct.

spermatic cord.def: A structure composed of veins, arteries, lymphatics, nerves and the ductus deferens. Its possible English equivalent is spermatic cord.

scrotum.def: A cutaneous bag containing right and left testis, epididymis and lower part of the spermatic cord. Its possible English equivalent is scrotum.

perfumed powder.def: An aromatic sprinkling powder prepared from certain herbs. Its possible English equivalent is perfumed powder.

syrup.def: A liquid preparation obtained by mixing fresh fruit juice or decoction of drugs with a base of sugar. Its possible English equivalent is syrup.

decanted water.def: The unstrained liquid obtained by soaking the drugs overnight in water or suitable liquid. Its possible English equivalent is decanted water.

throat drops.def: A medicinal preparation instilled in throat drop by drop. Its possible English equivalent is throat drops.

nasal drop.def: A liquid preparation which is used as nasal drop. Its possible English equivalent is nasal drop.

massaging agent.def: A liquid preparation that is applied on the body and massaged vigorously. Its possible English equivalent is massaging agent.

enema.def: through the mouth. Its possible English equivalent is enema. Smoking an affected organ by burning the drugs. Its possible English equivalent is fumigation.

steam inhalation.def: The exposure of a part of the body or the whole body to the vapours obtained from the decoction of drugs or boiled simple water. Its possible English equivalent is steam inhalation.

paste.def: A thick paste prepared by mixing powdered drugs in a suitable oil or water. It is used for local application. Its possible English equivalent is paste.

soot.def: The collected smoke obtained from burning of drugs and used as ophthalmic application. Its possible English equivalent is soot.

astringent enema.def: An enema used as an astringent. Its possible English equivalent is astringent enema.

moderative enema.def: enema. An enema which is moderate in potency. Its possible English equivalent is moderative enema.

anthelmintic enema.def: An enema used predominantly as anthelmintic. Its possible English equivalent is anthelmintic enema.

concoctus of bile.def: An agent which matures yellow bile for evacuation. Its possible English equivalent is concoctus of bile.

digestive.def: An agent which aids in digestion. Its possible English equivalent is digestive.

repellent.def: An agent that diverts the matter from one part of the body/organ to another part. Its possible English equivalent is repellent.

antihelminthic.def: astringent. The agent which kills the intestinal worms. Its possible English equivalent is antihelminthic.

coagulant.def: The drugs that aid or produce coagulation and cause a liquid/blood to coagulate. Its possible English equivalent is coagulant.

cicatrizant/healing agent.def: The drugs which help in the process of wound healing and scar formation. Its possible English equivalent is cicatrizant/healing agent.

calorific.def: An agent which, increases the metabolism of the body due to its hot temperament .Its possible English equivalent is calorific.

emmenogogue.def: The drugs which induce menstrual bleeding. Its possible English equivalent is emmenogogue.

nervine stimulants.def: An agent which stimulates the nerves. Its possible English equivalent is nervine stimulants.

eruptive.def: An agent which causes eruptions on the surface of the body due to its irritant properties. Its possible English equivalent is eruptive.

demulcent.def: An agent which liquefies thick and viscous matter. Its possible English equivalent is demulcent.

lubricant.def: An agent which provides lubrication. Its possible English equivalent is lubricant.

vesicant.def: An agent which produces blisters on the surface of the skin. It is much less severe in its effect than the escharotics. Its possible English equivalent is vesicant.

phlegmagogue.def: An agent which causes evacuation of Balgham (phlegm) through purgation. Its possible English equivalent is phlegmagogue.

cholagogue.def: An agent which causes evacuation of Safra’ (yellow bile) through purgation. Its possible English equivalent is cholagogue.

melanogogue.def: An agent which causes evacuation of Sawda’ (black bile) through purgation. Its possible English equivalent is melanogogue.

haemopoietic.def: An agent which stimulates the production of blood. Its possible English equivalent is haemopoietic.

lithotriptic.def: An agent that breaks down calculi. Its possible English equivalent is lithotriptic.

mydriatic.def: An agent which dilates the pupil. Its possible English equivalent is mydriatic.

anaesthetic.def: An agent which causes loss of sensation in the organ. Its possible English equivalent is anaesthetic.

toxic drug.def: An agent which proves injurious or fatal due to its toxic effects. Its possible English equivalent is Toxic drug.

hypnotic.def: An agent which induces sleep. Its possible English equivalent is hypnotic.

retentive of semen.def: An agent that prevents premature ejaculation. Its possible English equivalent is retentive of semen.

antiperspirant.def: An agent which prevents the production of sweat from the body. Its possible English equivalent is antiperspirant.

hair remover.def: An agent which removes the hair from body surface. Its possible English equivalent is hair remover.

hair elongator.def: An agent which increases the length of hair. Its possible English equivalent is hair elongator.

trichogen/ hair grower.def: An agent which promotes the growth of hair. Its possible English equivalent is trichogen/ hair grower.

combustion/oxidation.def: technically it is used in relation to oxidation of body fluids and humours. Due to excessive heat (morbid heat) the body fluids gets charred. Its possible English equivalent is combustion/oxidation.

discontinuity.def: The breach in the continuity of tissue. Its possible English equivalent is discontinuity.

efficacy.def: equivalent is increasing period. The resultant effect of the action of a drug or thing on the human body. Its possible English equivalent is efficacy.

cauterization /cautery.def: The process in which the body tissues are destroyed as a part of treatment by caustic agent, hot iron rod etc. Its possible English equivalent is cauterization /cautery.

purgation.def: Equivalent is emesis. Purging or free evacuation of faeces by some drugs either as a part of treatment or as a manifestation of some disease. Its possible English equivalent is purgation.

impaired nourishment.def: The impairment or alteration of the process of nourishment. Its possible English equivalent is impaired nourishment.

chronic.def: It means old or chronic. It is generally used to define the type of the disease. Its possible English equivalent is chronic.

low calorie diet.def: A diet low in nutritional value. Its possible English equivalent is low calorie diet.

purification of blood.def: any drug or regimen is called as al-Dam. Its possible English equivalent is purification of blood. Elimination of morbid material from the body.

contagious diseases.def: The types of diseases occuring due to invasion and multiplication of micro organisms in body tissues. Its possible English equivalent is contagious diseases.

wasting/emaciation.def: The wasting of body organs. Its possible English equivalent is wasting/emaciation.

healing / granulation.def: Healing of the wounds. Its possible English equivalent is healing / granulation.

transfer of matter.def: Transfer of matter from one organ to the other remote organ. Its possible English equivalent is transfer of matter.

temperamental equilibrium / alteration.def: To achieve the temperamental equilibrium Istifragh. through Tanqiya or Its possible English equivalent is temperamental equilibrium / alteration.

vascular deobstruction.def: To dilate the blood vessel or to dissolve thick / viscous matter to remove the intravascular obstruction. Its possible English equivalent is vascular deobstruction.

healthy food.def: Foods affording nourishment. Its possible English equivalent is healthy food.

susceptibility / predisposition.def: Internal susceptibility to a disease or having the ability to accept the effects of pathogens. Its possible English equivalent is susceptibility / predisposition.

appetite / desire.def: The desire for food. Its possible English equivalent is appetite / desire.

slow to percolation.def: Tardy or slow action or penetration in the body. Its possible English equivalent is slow to percolation.

harm / injury / damage.def: Injury or harm affecting the body mainly by drug. Its possible English equivalent is harm / injury / damage.

exudation / infiltration flow / effusion.def: Flow of matter or infiltration of matter (exudates) into the tissue. Its possible English equivalent is exudation / infiltration flow / effusion.

absorption / assimilation.def: Absorption of any medicine or substances through wall of digestive tract into the blood stream. Its possible English equivalent is absorption / assimilation.

hypothesis.def: To put an idea for assumption based on the past experiences. Its possible English equivalent is hypothesis.

resistance.def: Ability of body to repel a disease. Its possible English equivalent is resistance.

mental health.def: The normal state of mind in which a person feels happy and comfortable. He neither underestimates nor overestimates himself. Its possible English equivalent is mental health.

firm massage.def: equivalent is short massage. A type of massage in which hard strokes with pressure are used. It makes the body hard and firm. Its possible English equivalent is firm massage.

gentle massage.def: massage. A type of soft massage done to relax the body. Its possible English equivalent is gentle massage.

short massage.def: A type of massage, in which body is rubbed for a short duration. It is done to enhance the blood circulation, make the body parts shiny and produce heat in the body. Its possible English equivalent is short massage.

heavy massage.def: A type of massage done for a long duration at frequent intervals in each sitting. It helps in reducing the fat of the body. Its possible English equivalent is heavy massage.

smooth massage.def: A type of massage which is done gently with the palm or with a soft cloth. It draws and retains the blood in the muscles and skin of the treated area. Its possible English equivalent is smooth massage.

ante-natal care.def: herself and the foetus healthy. It also includes the management of ailments during pregnancy. Its possible English equivalent is ante-natal care.

neo-natal care.def: The regimen to keep the newborn healthy. It covers the period starting from the time of birth to the time they start to stand. Its possible English equivalent is neo-natal care.

regimen of aged person or geriatric care.def: The regimen to keep the elderly persons healthy. Its possible English equivalent is regimen of aged person or geriatric care.

children care.def: The regimen to keep the children healthy. Its possible English equivalent is children care.

balanced diet.def: nutrients such as vitamin, protein, fat, carbohydrates and minerals in balanced proportion. Its possible English equivalent is balanced diet.

air/wind.def: impure one damages health. Its possible English equivalent is air/wind. Al-Hawa’ al-Barid. Same as

southern winds.def: Air blowing towards south. Its possible English equivalent is southern winds.

western winds.def: Air blowing towards the west direction. Its possible English equivalent is western winds.

endemic.def: Prevalence of an infectious disease, within a particular geographic area or group of a population of a country. Its possible English equivalent is endemic.

pandemic.def: Outbreak of the infectious disease in a wide geographic area of the world. Its possible English equivalent is pandemic.

natural immunity.def: found naturally against a specific disease in humans or animals e.g. hens are naturally immune to Tetanus, immunity against T.B. is found in sheep and goat. Its possible English equivalent is natural immunity.

passive immunity.def: immunity. A type of acquired immunity produced by administration of preformed antibodies. Its possible English equivalent is passive immunity.

active immunity.def: Immunity acquired against specific disease in a body through injecting live or live attenuated or dead bacteria in the body. Its possible English equivalent is active immunity.

disinfection.def: are killed or measures adopted for killing the germs and prevention of infection. Its possible English equivalent is disinfection.

sterilization.def: A process through which microbes / germs or their spores are destroyed. Its possible English equivalent is sterilization.
A process carried out to render a male or female sterile without any interference with potency. It is a permanent method of contraception. Its possible English equivalent is sterilization.

vectors.def: Insects that transmit the germs from one person to another. Its possible English equivalent is vectors.

exercise.def: equivalent is indirect infection. Voluntary movements entailing deep and rapid respiration. Its possible English equivalent is exercise. Exercise, which results from professional

intentional exercise.def: unintentional exercise. Exercise done only for the sake of its benefits. Its possible English equivalent is intentional exercise.

short term exercise.def: Exercise, done for short duration. Its possible English equivalent is short term exercise.

frequent exercise.def: Exercise, done for long duration and frequently. Its possible English equivalent is frequent exercise.

strenuous or vigorous exercise.def: Exercise, done with strong and forceful movements of the body. Its possible English equivalent is strenuous or vigorous exercise.

gentle exercise.def: Exercise, done with gentle movements. Its possible English equivalent is gentle exercise.

hard and rapid exercise.def: Exercise, done with rapid and vigorous body movements. Its possible English equivalent is hard and rapid exercise.

gentle and slow exercise.def: It refers to the exercise in which mild body exertion is done slowly. Its possible English equivalent is gentle and slow exercise.

general exercise.def: Exercise involving the whole body exertion. Its possible English equivalent is general exercise.

partial exercise.def: Exercise done for a particular part of the body. Its possible English equivalent is partial exercise. Yaqza Nawm means sleep and means Asbab

external causes.def: equivalent is sleep and wakefulness. Non-corporeal (out side the body) causes which produce corporeal states directly e.g. blow, atmospheric heat, hot or cold food or psychological factors like anger or fear. Its possibl

bath/bathing.def: regimenal therapy where treatment through bath is adopted. It also prevents various humoral diseases. It is of various types. Its possible English equivalent is bath/bathing.

mustard bath.def: Hammam in which, mustard powder is added in the hot water in a dose of 1.75- 3.5gms per gallon and the patient is advised to take bath for 5-10 minutes. It is useful for skin diseases. Its possible English equivalent is

thermal bath.def: bath. Hammam in which, the person is advised to take bath in hot springs. It is beneficial in the weakness of nerves. Its possible English equivalent is thermal bath. Hammam

oil bath or immersion in oil.def: in which, the affected part of the body is immersed in lukewarm oil. It is beneficial for fatigue, nervine pain, joints pain, convulsion, tetanus and urinary retention. Its possible English equivalent is oil bath or imme

cold air.def: English equivalent is hot air. The cold air as a result of qualitative change in it. Its possible English equivalent is cold air.

occupational diseases.def: The diseases caused / produced by the occupation of a person. Its possible English equivalent is occupational diseases.

industrial medicine.def: The branch of medicine which deals with the information and preventive measures regarding the health promotion of the employees and to make the work place health friendly. Its possible English equivalent is industrial me

sex education.def: Education dealing with the human reproductive system, sexual health and other aspects of human sexuality and sexual behavior. Its possible English equivalent is sex education.

health education.def: Education dealing with the interventions of health promotion through which people learn to behave in a manner conducive to the promotion and maintenance of health Its possible English equivalent is health education.

ferruginous water/ water having iron con.def: the viscera, prevents sprue, resolves splenitis and serves as appetizer. Its possible English equivalent is ferruginous water/ water having iron contents.

mineral water.def: English equivalent is stagnant water. Water that contains appreciable amounts of certain salts and has therapeutic properties. Its possible English equivalent is mineral water.

social medicine.def: The medicine which deals with the study of man as a social being in his environment. Its focus is on the health of the community as a whole. Its possible English equivalent is social medicine.

preventive medicine.def: The branch of medicine which deals with the promotion of health, prevention of disease, disability and rehabilitation. Its possible English equivalent is preventive medicine.

vaccine.def: An immuno-biological substance designed to produce specific protection against a given disease. Its possible English equivalent is vaccine.

restorative massage.def: The massage which is done after completion of exercise. This type of massage is usually done with oil and should begin vigorously but end with moderate strokes. Its possible English equivalent is restorative massage.

warning notice.def: activity and peace. Information about certain unethical practices in a professional respect which is issued by Indian Medical Council to the registered medical practitioner. Its possible English equivalent is warning not

quickening.def: The feeling of foetal movements during pregnancy. Its possible English equivalent is quickening.

hanging.def: body by a ligature tied around the neck. In this condition death occurs usually due to asphyxia or vagal inhibition or due to spinal cord injury. Its possible English equivalent is hanging.

strangulation.def: A type of violent asphyxia caused by constriction of the neck. In this condition, force of constriction is greater than the victim’s body weight. It is usually homicidal. Its possible English equivalent is strangulation.

suffocation.def: A form of asphyxia caused by mechanical obstruction to the air passages by other means and not due to constriction of the neck. Its possible English equivalent is suffocation.

smothering.def: A form of asphyxia caused by mechanical occlusion of external air passages. Its possible English equivalent is smothering.

drowning.def: It is a type of asphyxial death caused by submersion of mouth & nostril in water or other fluid which prevent the entry of air into the lungs. Its possible English equivalent is drowning.

penal erasure.def: The erasure or removal of the name of a registered medical professional from the register / penal after an offense against him is confirmed beyond doubt. Its possible English equivalent is penal erasure.

haemorrhage / bleeding.def: insemination. The escape of blood from blood vessels. Its possible English equivalent is haemorrhage / bleeding.

medical jurisprudence / forensic medicin.def: the internal and external changes, signs & symptoms in the human body that helps in making the correct decision by the court. Its possible English equivalent is medical jurisprudence / forensic medicine.

international medical ethics.def: The code of medical ethics framed as sets of duties of doctor in general, duties of doctors to the sick and duties of doctors to each other. Its possible English equivalent is international medical ethics.

medical ethics.def: Code or principles governing the professional conduct of physicians. Its possible English equivalent is medical ethics.

medical etiquette.def: It refers to respect of doctors towards each other. Its possible English equivalent is medical etiquette.

sudden death.def: The death which is unpreceded or preceded for a short time by morbid symptoms. Its possible English equivalent is sudden death.

post mortem examination / autopsy examin.def: The examination of the body after death to see the changes occurring in the organs. Its possible English equivalent is post mortem examination / autopsy examination.

witness.def: English equivalent is medical evidence. A person who gives the description of incidence in the court. Its possible English equivalent is witness. The witness on the basis of professional

exhumation.def: The lawful digging out of a buried dead body from the grave. Its possible English equivalent is exhumation.

identification.def: The recognition of the individuality of a person live or dead. Its possible English equivalent is identification.

cadaveric spasm.def: A spasmodic condition seen after death where in stiffing occurs in the muscles which were already in a state of contraction at the time of death. Its possible English equivalent is cadaveric spasm.

rigor mortis.def: It refers to rigidity in the muscles of the cadaver. Its possible English equivalent is rigor mortis.

asphyxia.def: Unconsciousness from suffocation or impaired blood oxygenation. Its possible English equivalent is asphyxia.

putrefaction / decomposition.def: A process in which there is gradual dissolution and liquefaction of the tissues observed in a dead body. It is a permanent sign of death. Its possible English equivalent is putrefaction / decomposition.

mummification.def: A process seen in a cadaver when the water content in the dead body gets evaporated making it dehydrated and dry. Its possible English equivalent is mummification.

poisonous wound.def: which is produced by a weapon dipped in poison or wound in which poison has been introduced. Its possible English equivalent is poisonous wound.

lacerated wound.def: Type of wound in which the tissues are torn due to application of blunt force. Its possible English equivalent is lacerated wound.

septic wound.def: Wound which is infected with some pathogenic organism. This putrid wound contains pus. Its possible English equivalent is septic wound.

scalp wound.def: Wound on the head involving the scalp. Its possible English equivalent is scalp wound.

compound injury.def: It refers to multiple injuries or an injury which involves more than one organ of the body and shows various symptoms and complications. Its possible English equivalent is compound injury.

criminal.def: A person who is responsible for an illegal act which may be injurious to the public welfare or moral or health. Its possible English equivalent is criminal.

scald.def: It refers to the injury resulting from exposure to moist heat like vapors involving only superficial layer of the skin. Its possible English equivalent is scald.

gun shot wound.def: Wound produced by a projectile from an object. It shows the characteristic of bruise and lacerated wound. Its possible English equivalent is gun shot wound.

foeticide.def: English equivalent is intoxication. Killing of the foetus in the womb. Its possible English equivalent is foeticide. Hippocrates, the founder of the Unani

hurt.def: An action which leads to deformity, disability or a disease in a person. Its possible English equivalent is hurt. The injury which is produced by a person

fabricated wound.def: on his own body or by another person with his consent. The main objective behind such injuries is to produce false evidence against another person or to avert suspicion from oneself. Its possible English equivalent is fa

natural death.def: Death which occurs due to exhaustion of innate heat in old age. Its possible English equivalent is natural death.

unnatural death.def: Death which occurs due to unnatural circumstances or accidentally like murder, accident, drowning etc. Its possible English equivalent is unnatural death.

molecular death.def: Death of all cells of the body. It is completed within few hours of the somatic death. Its possible English equivalent is molecular death. A process in which organ or part of the

toxicology.def: with toxic drugs / poisons, their types, signs and symptoms, fatal dose and the treatment to combat their effects. Its possible English equivalent is toxicology.

vegetable poisons/phyto poisons.def: Afyun (Datura stramonium), (Papaver somniferum) etc. Its possible English equivalent is vegetable poisons/phyto poisons.

animal poisons.def: Poisons of animal origin e.g. snake venom. Its possible English equivalent is animal poisons.

mineral poisons.def: Poisons of mineral origin e.g. Arsenic. Its possible English equivalent is mineral poisons.

action of the poisons.def: It refers to actions of poisons which may be local, remote, local and remote both, combined and general. Its possible English equivalent is action of the poisons.

fatal dose.def: equivalent is poisons. The amount of poison which is required to make it fatal. Its possible English equivalent is fatal dose.

asphyxiants.def: The poisons that act on the respiratory system and lead to asphyxia e.g. Carbon monoxide. Its possible English equivalent is asphyxiants.

cerebral poisons.def: Poisons which act on the cerebrum. These poisons may cause different effects like sedation, inebriation, hypnosis e.g. opium, cocaine. Its possible English equivalent is cerebral poisons.

cardiac poisons.def: Poisons which affect the heart e.g. Bachnak Tambaku (aconite), Digitalis, (tobacco) etc. Its possible English equivalent is cardiac poisons. Poisons which after absorption act and

spinal poisons.def: neurotoxins. Poisons which act on spinal cord e.g. Kuchla (Strychnos nux vomica) and Zard chameli (gelsemium). Its possible English equivalent is spinal poisons.

peripheral nerve poisons.def: Poisons which produce toxic effect on the Shokran peripheral nerves e.g. (curare). Its possible English equivalent is peripheral nerve poisons.

corrosive.def: Poison / drug which causes ulceration of the tissues due to strong caustic and ulcerative properties. Its possible English equivalent is corrosive.

mechanical combination.def: Alteration of the action of poison when it is quantitatively combined with other substance e.g. dilution of acid with water reduces it toxic action. Its possible English equivalent is mechanical combination.

excretion of poisons.def: action. The term refers to the elimination of poisons from the body through various routes like diuresis, purgation & emesis. Its possible English equivalent is excretion of poisons.

local and remote combined action.def: al-Sumum, In the context of ‘Ilm some poisons show toxic effect at the site of contact with the body as well as after absorption produce toxic action on the other system of the body. Its possible English equivalent is lo

parasite.def: The term means an animal / plant or micro-organism that draws its sustainance from another source. Its possible English equivalent is parasite.

delaying of action of poison.def: is counter action of poison. One of the principles of treatment of poison in which antidote is used to reduce action of poison. Its possible English equivalent is delaying of action of poison.

stage of in coordination.def: In this stage of poisoning the symptoms seen are delirium, unclear voice, loss of pupilary reflex, nausea and vomiting. Its possible English equivalent is stage of in coordination.

stage of narcosis.def: A condition seen in neurotic poisoning. In this stage, patients become unconscious, skin becomes cold, wet and pale with bluish discoloration of lips, pupils are constricted, pulse is weak & rapid and anuria is present.

chemical antidote.def: A substance that combines chemically with a poison to form an innocuous chemical compound. Its possible English equivalent is chemical antidote.

mechanical antidote.def: A substance that prevents the absorption of a poison by its presence. Its possible English equivalent is mechanical antidote. This term is used for a particular antidote

poisons causing cellular damage.def: Poison which acts on the cells of the body resulting in destruction of the cell. Its possible English equivalent is poisons causing cellular damage.

intra venous injection.def: chronic poisoning. One of the routes of administration of poison in which poison is injected in the body through veins. Its possible English equivalent is intra venous injection.

amputation.def: It refers to cutting or removal of the affected part of the body. Its possible English equivalent is amputation.

symptoms of poisoning.def: It refers to the indications or changes in the body due to poisoning observed by the patient or physician. Its possible English equivalent is symptoms of poisoning.

uremia.def: It is the presence of nitrogenous waste products in the blood resulting in toxic effects. Its possible English equivalent is uremia.

cardiac depressant.def: Poison which affects the heart and consequently weakens it. Its possible English equivalent is cardiac depressant.

site of snake bite.def: It refers to the site of the body where the snake bites. Its possible English equivalent is site of snake bite. A potent antidote compound which is

poisoning.def: innate heat. The presence of the poisonous substance in the body, consumed by an individual. Its possible English equivalent is poisoning.

secretion.def: The release of a substance from circulating fluid of the body. Its possible English equivalent is secretion.

acute poisoning.def: severity of signs and symptoms is high and duration of appearance is very short. Its possible English equivalent is acute poisoning.

allergy.def: An increased sensitivity of the body to various substances. Its possible English equivalent is allergy.

alkali.def: A basic substance which is alkaline in reaction and capable of saponifying fats. It is included in corrosive poison. Its possible English equivalent is alkali.

form of poison.def: The physical state of poison, for example solid, liquid or gas. Poison in gaseous form is the most rapid in action. Its possible English equivalent is form of poison.

insecticide.def: poisoning. An agent which is used to kill the insects. Its possible English equivalent is insecticide.
